# SRS

> This is the canonical System Requirements Specification backlog. `UPGRADE_BACKLOG.md` remains as a compatibility alias.

Purpose: track future upgrades without mixing them into active implementation threads.

Status legend:
- `queued` not started and ready for triage
- `in_progress` actively in progress
- `blocked` waiting on dependency/decision
- `done` completed

## Production-Grade Checklist Addendum (2026-03-06)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-F100-016 | done | Cron delivery announce/main hardening | Silent cron failures were the highest-risk operations gap. | `client/runtime/config/cron_jobs.json` has `delivery.mode=announce` and `delivery.channel=main` for all enabled jobs. |
| V6-F100-017 | done | Rust status dashboard SLO metrics expansion | Pass/fail checks alone were insufficient for operator-grade monitoring. | `protheus-ops status --dashboard` emits SLO metrics for spine success rate, receipt latency p95, assimilation pain score, cron health, and PQTS slippage MAPE. |
| V6-F100-018 | done | Model recovery fallback policy for degraded families | Runtime needed deterministic failover for unstable `llama3.2` routes. | `client/runtime/config/model_health_*_recovery_policy.json` defines bounded retries and `fallback_model` routing for `llama3.2:*`. |
| V6-F100-019 | done | Release-build path hardening for conduit launchers | Hosted mode should prefer optimized binaries before debug/cargo fallback. | Conduit launchers check `target/release` first and release build validation succeeds (`cargo build --release --manifest-path core/layer0/ops/Cargo.toml`). |
| V6-F100-020 | done | Secret rotation weekly attestation cadence | Secrets hygiene needed automated recurrence and deterministic evidence. | Weekly cron job `Secret Rotation Attestation (weekly)` exists and runs `client/runtime/systems/security/secret_rotation_migration_auditor.js`. |
| V6-F100-021 | done | Memory continuity index rebuild run | Session continuity quality degraded when index freshness drifted. | `node client/memory/tools/rebuild_exclusive.js` completes and regenerates memory index artifacts without archive leakage. |
| V6-COGNITION-010 | done | Conversation Eye default synthesis lane | Dialogue context was not being converted into typed memory nodes, leaving cockpit awareness/manual mode gaps. | `conversation_eye` collector + synthesizer shipped, `local:init` auto-provisions the eye, and synthesized nodes are persisted under `client/runtime/local/state/memory/conversation_eye/nodes.jsonl` with tags `conversation/decision/insight/directive/t1`. |
| V6-MECH-014 | done | Ambient cockpit harness hardening + mech-suit mode control refresh | Cockpit integration needed explicit alert push artifacts and an operator mode switch to complete the “drivable mech suit” contract from the March 8 spec intake. | Extended `client/runtime/systems/ops/cockpit_harness.ts` with critical-alert artifact emission (`client/runtime/local/state/cockpit/alerts/latest.json` + `history.jsonl`), alert telemetry in envelope/status payloads, and added `client/runtime/systems/ops/mech_suit_mode_control.ts` with `status/on/off/set` commands (`ops:mech-suit:*` scripts). Requirement spec updated at `docs/client/requirements/mech_suit_cockpit_harness_requirements.md`; coverage includes `client/memory/tools/tests/cockpit_harness.test.js` and `client/memory/tools/tests/mech_suit_mode_control.test.js`. |
| V6-COCKPIT-001 | done | Persistent cockpit attach runtime (`REQ-32`) | Session-scoped command loops keep rehydrating context and never feel like an always-on mech suit. | `protheusd` now defaults to attach-first entry semantics (`PROTHEUSD_DEFAULT_COMMAND=attach`), supports deterministic `attach` receipts with attach-or-start behavior, and exposes daemon attach hints through conduit-safe surfaces. |
| V6-COCKPIT-002 | done | Conduit event subscription lane for cockpit (`REQ-32`) | Pull-only status checks force heartbeat/polling behavior and delay awareness. | `protheusd subscribe` now streams bounded attention batches through conduit-backed `attention-queue drain` with `--wait-ms` long-poll contracts, timeout-aware degraded batches, and consumer-safe receipt envelopes. |
| V6-COCKPIT-003 | done | Resident ambient memory service snapshot (`REQ-32`) | Memory availability still feels file-read driven rather than ambiently mounted. | Daemon persists `resident_memory` snapshots at `client/runtime/local/state/ops/resident_memory/latest.json`; status/attach receipts surface Rust-authoritative ambient-memory health, SQLite path, and hotset counts. |
| V6-COCKPIT-004 | done | Incremental identity hydration budget contract (`REQ-32`) | Cold-start token tax remains high when identity/context are eagerly loaded. | Startup hydration policy (`client/runtime/config/cockpit_identity_hydration_policy.json`) is enforced and persisted at `client/runtime/local/state/ops/identity_hydration/latest.json` with loaded/deferred file budgets and token estimates. |
| V6-COCKPIT-005 | done | Subscribe bridge timeout elimination under host pressure | Degraded-safe batches prevent crashes, but repeated `conduit_bridge_timeout` still reduces ambient confidence. | `protheusd subscribe` now chunks long waits and, on bridge timeout/gate conditions, drains deterministic local compat attention state (`queue.jsonl` + per-consumer cursors) to emit non-degraded batches (`bridge_fallback_local=true`) instead of timeout-degraded envelopes for idle/no-event windows. |
| V6-COCKPIT-006 | done | Native conduit push subscription transport | Long-poll cycles were still paying polling overhead and delayed event wake-ups under ambient mode. | `protheusd subscribe` now defaults to native push transport (`--transport=push`) with mutation-driven wake (`waitForAttentionPush`), bounded heartbeat wait windows, preserved cursor/ack semantics via `attention-queue drain`, and explicit push metadata in receipts (`transport`, `native_push`, `push_wait_reason`). Timeout floors were tuned for push mode (`bridge` 8s / `stdio` 12s defaults) to fail fast into deterministic fallback instead of long blocking windows. |
| V6-HOST-BUILD-STALE-001 | done | Host Rust build-stall guard stabilization | Local validation lanes were hanging or exiting ambiguously when `cargo` build scripts stalled under dyld/host contention. | `host_rust_validation` now supports deterministic host-stall deferral (`--defer-on-host-stall=1`) with explicit `raw_reason_code` and fixed shell-exit handling for deferred/success runs; guarded scripts (`ops:test:protheus-ops-core:attention`, `ops:test:execution-core:initiative`) now return auditable `reason_code=deferred_host_stall` instead of hanging. |
| V6-VALIDATION-HOST-001 | done | Full non-skipped host validation pack execution | Runtime confidence required end-to-end harness/benchmark execution without timeout-skip paths on the current host profile. | Executed mech/control/formal/benchmark/harness parity pack (`test:ops:mech-suit`, `test:ops:mech-suit:control`, `ops:test:protheus-ops-core:attention`, `ops:test:execution-core:initiative`, `formal:invariants:run`, `ops:benchmark-autonomy:run`, `ops:mech-suit:benchmark`, `ops:harness:6m`, `ops:protheus-vs-openclaw`), with deterministic receipts and no host-timeout skips. |
| V6-MEMORY-011 | done | Dream-sequenced memory matrix + auto-recall lane | Memory relevance ranking and associative recall were missing, causing high-burn manual memory lookups. | Added `memory_matrix`, `dream_sequencer`, and `memory_auto_recall` lanes. Matrix now stores every indexed tag with ranked node IDs scored by `level(node1>tag2>jot3)+recency+dream inclusion`; dream cycle runs now reorder the matrix and new conversation memories can push bounded top-match recalls to attention queue via conduit. |
| V6-INITIATIVE-013 | in_progress | Core global importance/priority kernel (`REQ-27`) | Initiative and queue-collision behavior needed a single deterministic importance authority spanning all ambient producers. | Added Rust importance engine + priority queue ordering in `core/layer0/ops/src/importance.rs` and `core/layer0/ops/src/attention_queue.rs`; enqueue/status receipts now carry `score`, `band`, `priority`, and `initiative_action`. Added subconscious/client boundary regression guard lane (`client/runtime/systems/ops/subconscious_boundary_guard.ts`) with strict policy + required-check wiring. Remaining exit: complete live runtime validation and cockpit-priority consumption proof. |
| V6-ARCH-ICEBERG-028 | in_progress | Conscious/Subconscious Iceberg v1.0 execution (`REQ-28`) | The architecture spec existed externally but needed executable repo contracts and Layer2 initiative primitives tied to it. | Added canonical contract doc `docs/SYSTEM-ARCHITECTURE-SPECS.md`, derived requirement `docs/client/requirements/REQ-28-conscious-subconscious-iceberg-v1.md`, linked `ARCHITECTURE.md` to the contract, and shipped Layer2 deterministic initiative primitives in `core/layer2/execution/src/initiative.rs` (`initiative-score`, `initiative-action`, `attention-priority`). Remaining exit: complete migration of live queue authority from `core/layer0/ops` to Layer2 lanes without regression. |
| V6-ARCH-LAYERING-031 | done | Layer -1 + Layer 3 stack contract adoption (`REQ-31`) | New architecture intake added immutable Layer 0 plus explicit Layer -1 exotic template and Layer 3 OS-personality growth contracts that must be reflected in docs, structure, and enforcement. | Runtime wiring completed via `core/layer_minus_one/exotic_wrapper` and `core/layer3/os_extension_wrapper` crates, workspace membership in root `Cargo.toml`, and `kernel_layers` feature topology updated to include `layer_minus_one` and Layer 3 wrapper exports/tests. Docs/rulebook/spec surfaces remain aligned to the Layer -1 → Layer 3 contract. |
| V6-ARCH-032 | done | Executable system map registry + generator (`REQ-34`) | System comprehension degrades when subsystem ownership/layering is only described narratively and not machine-regenerated. | Added canonical registry `client/runtime/config/system_map_registry.json`, generator lane `client/runtime/systems/ops/system_map_generator.{ts,js}`, generated artifact `docs/client/architecture/SYSTEM_MAP.md`, runtime receipts under `client/runtime/local/state/ops/system_map/*`, npm scripts (`ops:system-map:*`), and regression `client/memory/tools/tests/system_map_generator.test.js`. |
| V6-ORIGIN-001 | done | Root invariant enforcer lane (`REQ-29`) | Origin integrity needed an executable, deterministic gate instead of narrative assurances. | Added Rust lane `protheus-ops origin-integrity <run|status|certificate|seed-bootstrap-verify>` in `core/layer0/ops/src/origin_integrity.rs` with deterministic receipts, strict fail-closed mode, and safety-plane hashing. |
| V6-ORIGIN-002 | done | Conduit-only + constitution hardening gate in origin checks | Infallible-origin claim requires enforced conduit boundary and constitution non-weakening prerequisites. | `origin-integrity run` now executes dependency-boundary guard checks and validates constitution guardian + resurrection + Merkle prerequisites before returning pass. |
| V6-ORIGIN-003 | done | Receipt binding to exact safety-plane state | Integrity receipts must be cryptographically tied to the exact root state at execution time. | Origin receipts now include `safety_plane_state_hash` and deterministic `state_binding_hash` derived from check outcomes + safety snapshot. |
| V6-ORIGIN-004 | done | `protheusd` startup self-audit gate (30s fail-closed) | Ambient daemon startup needed a hard origin-integrity preflight to prevent unsafe autonomous loops. | `client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusd.ts` now runs origin-integrity checks during `start` and ambient startup with 30s default timeout and strict fail-closed blocking (`require_pass_on_start`). |
| V6-ORIGIN-005 | done | Seed bootstrap verification via `verify.sh` certificate | Seed growth requires deterministic acceptance checks against origin verification artifacts. | Added root `verify.sh` plus `origin-integrity certificate` and `origin-integrity seed-bootstrap-verify --certificate=...` for verify-script and safety-state hash matching. |
| V6-ORIGIN-006 | queued | Lean-proof extractor integration for origin lane | Runtime enforcement is in place, but formal proof extraction is still pending for full proof-carrying origin claims. | Integrate Lean extractor outputs into `origin-integrity run` with receipt-linked proof IDs and fail-closed mismatches. |
| V6-MEMORY-012 | done | Full legacy daily-file XML hierarchy backfill | Existing historical daily memory files still use frontmatter + separator blocks, so hierarchy metadata is partially inferred at runtime. | Executed `node client/memory/tools/backfill_xml_hierarchy.js --apply=1` across all legacy daily files (18 files / 208 node envelopes converted in the local memory surface), then rebuilt traversal artifacts with `node client/memory/tools/rebuild_exclusive.js`. Added regression hardening in `client/memory/tools/tests/memory_matrix.test.js` to force isolated conversation-path inputs (`MEMORY_MATRIX_CONVERSATION_PATH`) so matrix/index validation remains deterministic and token-bounded after XML backfill. |
| V6-F100-022 | blocked | Live trading proof gate (30-day promotion evidence) | Paper/live performance history must validate runtime under real market stress. | 30-day canary evidence reaches promotion thresholds (PnL/risk/SLO), with deterministic receipts. |
| V6-F100-023 | blocked | Third-party security audit publication | External trust requires independent verification beyond internal controls. | Public external audit report + remediation tracker are published and linked from `SECURITY.md`. |
| V6-F100-024 | blocked | Horizontal scaling proof in production profile | Single-node posture limits enterprise confidence under fault/load conditions. | Multi-node failover and soak evidence meets distributed runtime gates in production profile. |
| V6-F100-025 | blocked | Continuous chaos weekly evidence contract | Reliability posture needs sustained resilience proof, not one-off drills. | Weekly chaos report artifacts pass resilience gates and are surfaced in operator dashboard health checks. |
| V6-F100-026 | done | Optional pip installation wrapper (thin Python CLI) | Add Python ecosystem onboarding without creating a second source of truth. | `packages/protheus-py` ships a thin `protheus` wrapper that delegates to Rust `protheus-ops`; docs and requirements updated. |
| V6-F100-027 | done | Observability metrics hardening (p99 + escalation + cost attribution) | Fortune-grade operations require explicit latency tail, escalation pressure, and token burn/cost metrics. | `core/layer0/ops/src/health_status.rs` emits `receipt_latency_p99_ms`, `human_escalation_open_rate`, and `token_burn_cost_attribution` in `protheus-ops status --dashboard` with alert wiring for metric warnings. |
| V6-F100-028 | done | Progressive rollout ring evaluator (Rust-authoritative) | Safe deployment needs deterministic ring promotion/rollback decisions from measured canary signals. | `core/layer0/ops/src/rollout_rings.rs` adds `protheus-ops rollout-rings status|evaluate` with policy-backed phase progression and rollback actions from `client/runtime/config/rust_lane_canary_rollout_policy.json`. |
| V6-F100-029 | done | Golden dataset + property-style router regression pack | Large-scale migrations need deterministic behavioral guardrails beyond ad hoc spot checks. | `core/layer0/ops/tests/golden/model_router_cases.json` + `core/layer0/ops/tests/model_router_golden_regression.rs` enforce golden role/capability/tier outputs and property-style invariants across generated inputs. |
| V6-F100-030 | done | Deterministic router offline fallback plan output | Multi-model resilience must be explicit in receipts, not implicit in runtime assumptions. | `core/layer0/ops/src/model_router.rs` now emits `route_plan` with `provider_online`, `preferred_model`, `fallback_model`, `selected_model`, and `fallback_applied`. |
| V6-F100-031 | done | Loop/persona A/B trial evaluator with promotion receipts | Iteration speed requires measurable variant evaluation and promotion criteria instead of manual selection. | `core/layer0/ops/src/ab_lane_eval.rs` adds `protheus-ops ab-lane-eval status|run` with deterministic score math, winner selection, and state receipts under `state/ops/ab_lane_eval/`. |
| V6-F100-032 | done | OSS client/legal/governance baseline completion | F100 adoption gates require OSI licensing and standard community governance artifacts. | Switched to Apache-2.0 (`LICENSE`, `package.json`), added `.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, added structured issue/PR templates, and archived legacy proprietary legal docs under `docs/client/legal/archive/` with client/runtime/config/docs path updates. |
| V6-F100-033 | done | Release/security automation baseline uplift | Production trust requires deterministic release drafting and recurring dependency/security audits. | Added `.github/workflows/release.yml` (draft release automation) and `.github/workflows/security-audit.yml` (`npm audit` + `cargo audit`) while preserving existing SBOM release workflow. |
| V6-F100-034 | blocked | First public semantic release + npm publish | Public release/publish still requires maintainer credentials and remote registry actions. | Tag + publish `v0.2.0` release artifacts and publish npm package via maintainer token; GitHub Releases + npm listing show the new version (version/prep now staged in repo manifests). |
| V6-F100-035 | done | SPDX header sweep across source tree | Apache posture is stronger with explicit SPDX headers across source files. | Applied SPDX headers via `protheus-ops f100-readiness-program run --lane=V6-F100-035 --apply=1` and enforced via `.github/workflows/spdx-header-guard.yml`. |
| V6-F100-036 | done | Root experimental folder rationalization | Top-level `drafts/notes/experiments` surface still looks pre-production to enterprise evaluators. | Root `drafts/notes/experiments` moved under `research/archive/root_surface/` and verified by `V6-F100-036` readiness receipts. |
| V6-F100-037 | done | README governance links + repo hygiene ownership fences | Fortune-100 readiness checklist required governance discoverability and explicit ownership around non-production roots. | Added governance template links in `README.md`, added npm/docker badges, and extended `.github/CODEOWNERS` coverage for `/drafts`, `/notes`, `/experiments`. |
| V6-F100-038 | done | Backlog canonical rename to SRS with compatibility alias | External-facing governance needed a more formal backlog identity without breaking existing runtime contracts. | `SRS.md` exists as canonical backlog artifact, runtime/client/runtime/config/docs default to `SRS.md`, and compatibility references to `UPGRADE_BACKLOG.md` remain supported for legacy lanes. |
| V6-F100-039 | done | F100 baseline reconciliation gate with deterministic receipts | External evaluations drift quickly without one executable contract proving which claims are currently true. | Added `client/runtime/config/f100_enterprise_baseline_contract.json` and `client/runtime/systems/ops/f100_enterprise_baseline_gate.js` (strict/status modes) with state receipt output + markdown status, CI workflow `.github/workflows/f100-enterprise-baseline.yml`, npm scripts, and regression test `client/memory/tools/tests/f100_enterprise_baseline_gate.test.js`. |
| V6-F100-040 | done | Enterprise deployment packaging baseline (Helm + Terraform) | Raw manifests alone were insufficient for enterprise packaging expectations during procurement review. | Added Helm chart under `client/runtime/deploy/helm/protheus/` and Terraform Helm module under `client/runtime/deploy/terraform/protheus_helm/` with install/apply docs. |

| V6-F100-042 | done | Audit-log export adapters for SIEM pipelines (Splunk/ELK/Datadog) | Large operators need deterministic export paths to existing observability/compliance systems. | Added `protheus-ops audit-log-export` with Splunk/ELK/Datadog payload contracts, replay-safe receipt pointers, and unit tests in `core/layer0/ops/src/audit_log_export.rs`. |

| V6-F100-A-012 | done | Release pipeline GA hardening (semantic versioning + signed artifacts + changelog + SBOM attach) | Draft release automation exists, but A/A+ procurement expects deterministic GA release contracts and signed multi-artifact publication outputs every cycle. | `.github/workflows/release.yml` now runs `release_semver_contract` (conventional-commit semver), builds/signs Rust + npm artifacts, auto-writes release changelog notes, and attaches SPDX SBOM artifacts/signatures to release assets. |

| V6-ADAPT-003 | done | Persistent adaptation continuity contract (“never forgets”) | Persistent adaptation deltas must survive restart and validate before reuse to support continuity claims. | Added continuity hash + integrity hash persistence and fail-closed continuity verification (`verify-continuity`) in adaptation lane state handling. Regression hardened in `client/memory/tools/tests/realtime_adaptation_continuity.test.js` and validated via `npm run -s lane:v6-adapt-003:run`, `npm run -s test:lane:v6-adapt-003`, and `npm run -s ops:adaptive:realtime:verify`. |
| V6-ADAPT-CORE-001 | queued | Port adaptation primitives to core and demote client runtime | Adaptation primitives are currently bootstrapped in TS client runtime for execution speed; authority must live in core to satisfy strict layering/security. | Port `REQ-19-001`/`002`/`003` primitives to Rust `core/layer2`, expose only conduit APIs, keep `client` Layer 3 as thin compatibility shell + tests, and remove policy authority from client runtime code. |
| V6-ADAPT-004 | done | Covenant/drift fail-closed adaptation gates | Real-time adaptation requires strict safety bounds to prevent governance drift during incremental updates. | Added drift/covenant gates in `client/runtime/systems/adaptive/realtime_adaptation_loop.ts` (`drift_score_exceeded`, `covenant_gate_denied`) backed by policy controls in `client/runtime/config/realtime_adaptation_loop_policy.json` (`drift_gates`). Receipts now carry structured `drift_gate` telemetry for audit/replay. Verified by `npm run -s lane:v6-adapt-004:run` and `npm run -s test:lane:v6-adapt-004`. |
| V6-ADAPT-005 | done | Persona/shadow review bridge for high-impact adaptation | High-impact adaptation changes should pass through human-legible governance reviews before promotion. | Added `client/runtime/systems/adaptive/adaptation_review_bridge.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/adaptation_review_bridge_policy.json` to require shadow/persona context for high-impact adaptation submissions, queue pending reviews, and emit deterministic review receipts. Verified by `npm run -s lane:v6-adapt-005:run` and `npm run -s test:lane:v6-adapt-005`. |
| V6-ADAPT-006 | done | Apple-silicon adaptation profile + portable fallback | Apple-silicon optimization is a high-ROI target but requires deterministic fallback for wider hardware coverage. | Added hardware-aware profile resolver (`auto` mode) with `apple_silicon` profile multipliers and policy-driven fallback (`platform_profile_policy`) in `client/runtime/systems/adaptive/realtime_adaptation_loop.ts` + `client/runtime/config/realtime_adaptation_loop_policy.json`. Receipts now include `requested_profile`, `profile`, and `profile_source` for deterministic host adaptation traceability. Verified by `npm run -s lane:v6-adapt-006:run` and `npm run -s test:lane:v6-adapt-006`. |

## P0

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| BL-001 | done | External temp-data backup pipeline (outside git) | Preserve high-churn runtime/state data without repo noise | `client/runtime/systems/ops/state_backup.js` + `client/runtime/config/state_backup_policy.json`, optional daily spine hook (`STATE_BACKUP_ENABLED=1`), retention policy encoded |
| BL-002 | done | Skill quarantine enforcement in all install paths | Prevent unreviewed skill install bypasses | Strict enforcer scans `client/runtime/systems/`, `client/cognition/habits/`, `client/cognition/skills/`, and `client/memory/tools/` (tests excluded), blocks direct installer patterns (shell + spawn/exec forms), and requires wrapper + quarantine structure to pass |
| BL-003 | done | Autonomy receipt dashboard/summary | Make pass/fail verification visible without raw log digging | Daily summary command/report shows receipt pass rate + top failure reasons Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_003_anchor.ts`. |
| BL-013 | done | AGI security/governance hardening pack | Prevent capability-overhang failures as model capability increases | Dual-control approval for strategy mode escalation + non-bypass budget/risk caps + immutable policy/kernel verification + one-command emergency stop path tested Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_013_anchor.ts`. |
| BL-017 | done | Autonomous skill-add with necessity justification gate | Allow Protheus to add skills safely only when operationally justified | `client/runtime/systems/security/autonomous_skill_necessity_audit.ts` enforces necessity scoring for `--autonomous=1` installs using policy-defined structured justification (`problem`, `repeat_frequency`, `expected_time_or_token_savings`, `risk_class`, `why_existing_habits_or_skills_insufficient`); novelty-only reasons are blocked with receipts |
| BL-036 | done | Strategic alignment oracle (weekly) | Keep unattended autonomy optimizing for T1 outcomes instead of infrastructure churn | `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/alignment_oracle.js` writes weekly `alignment_score` artifacts (`state/autonomy/alignment_oracle/`), links to proposal/receipt evidence, emits deduped `autonomy_human_escalation` rows on low-streak escalation, and is executed by spine via `SPINE_ALIGNMENT_ORACLE_ENABLED` |
| BL-037 | done | Exception novelty classifier + recovery policy | Prevent retry spirals and route genuinely new failures to human review quickly | Classifier stores signature memory and now applies deterministic recovery decisions from `client/runtime/config/autonomy_exception_recovery_policy.json`; autonomy exception telemetry includes action/cooldown/playbook fields, and novel signatures route to escalation policy |
| BL-038 | done | Global cost governor + autopause guardrails | Enforce hard resource boundaries across autonomy/reflex/focus/dream paths | Unified daily/monthly budget policies apply to all adaptive executors, burn-rate spike and low-credit thresholds trigger automatic pause/degrade actions, and every block/allow decision is audit-logged with reason Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_038_anchor.ts`. |
| BL-039 | done | Dream-to-upstream signal bridge | Ensure dream outputs influence proposal/admission/ranking instead of remaining passive artifacts | Proposal enrichment/autonomy ranking ingest dream tokens/themes (bounded influence + audit fields), and runs emit measurable dream-hit attribution in proposal metadata Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_039_anchor.ts`. |
| BL-040 | done | Adaptive dream-model failover + cooldown memory | Prevent repeated `smallthinker` timeout loops and keep dream cycle resilient | Idle/REM model selection records per-model timeout/error history, applies cooldown/backoff, auto-fails over to alternate local models, and logs fallback reasons deterministically Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_040_anchor.ts`. |

| BL-014 | done | Phased TypeScript migration for `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/lib/` | Reduce contract drift and runtime breakage in high-permanence layers without rewriting architecture | `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/lib/` are TS-owned with wrapper-only JS (`client/runtime/lib/ts_bootstrap.js`), `npm run typecheck:systems` is green, and `node client/runtime/systems/ops/dist_runtime_cutover.js legacy-pairs --strict=1` reports zero legacy pairs |

| BL-016 | done | Cross-device active-state continuity layer | Preserve in-flight autonomy/routing context when switching active shell/device without split-brain | Add `client/runtime/systems/continuity/` with lease-based active writer election, checkpoint + delta replay state transfer, takeover on lease expiry, and secret-safe payload policy (no raw creds in continuity artifacts) |

| BL-019 | done | Optional habit cell-pool executor (parallelized by demand + hardware caps) | Extend reflex-style dynamic concurrency to habit execution only if measured ROI justifies complexity | Add bounded habit worker pool with hysteresis/cooldowns, per-habit safety gates, and rollback-safe default-off rollout Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_019_anchor.ts`. |
| BL-020 | done | Ignore memory snapshots after backup channels are verified | Keep repo clean once client/cognition/adaptive/normal memory have reliable off-repo backup/restore | Define backup integrity checks for memory snapshots, then add scoped `.gitignore` rules for backup-only memory artifacts without ignoring source/client/runtime/config/governance files |
| BL-021 | done | End-to-end pipeline integration tests (`eyes -> insight -> queue -> execute -> receipt -> score`) | Catch regressions that unit tests miss in autonomous flow handoffs | Added deterministic E2E handoff test covering generated + filtered proposals, actuation bridge wiring, dry-run execution, and contract receipt assertions (`client/memory/tools/tests/pipeline_handoffs.integration.test.js`) |
| BL-022 | done | Two-phase autonomous change execution with automatic rollback | Prevent partial/bad self-changes from persisting | Added `improvement_controller.js start-validated` implementing deterministic `plan -> apply(commit-on-head) -> verify(core checks) -> commit(trial start)` flow; failed verify triggers optional auto-revert and writes contract receipts with root cause (`state/autonomy/improvements/phase_receipts/`) Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_022_anchor.ts`. |

## P2

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| BL-007 | done | Backup integrity checks | Catch silent backup corruption | Scheduled hash verification and alert on mismatch Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_007_anchor.ts`. |

| BL-050 | done | Queue SQLite migration rollout/spec runbook | Concurrent queue cutover needs explicit rollback/playbook evidence for operators before full production lock-in | `client/runtime/systems/ops/queue_sqlite_migration.md` defines staged cutover, rollback, validation checkpoints, and concurrency/retention notes; linked to BL-046 implementation. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-051 | done | Execution legacy runtime authority cutover (TS -> Rust wrapper) | Duplicate TS execution core logic keeps drift risk high and slows real Rust ownership progress. | `client/runtime/systems/execution/legacy_runtime.ts` is now a thin compatibility wrapper delegating to Rust `execution_core`; parity test now compares legacy wrapper output against direct Rust binary receipts (`client/memory/tools/tests/execution_phase2_rust_parity.test.js`). Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-052 | done | Task decomposition profile composition moved into Rust execution crate | `client/runtime/systems/execution/task_decomposition_primitive.ts` still held deterministic micro-task profile shaping that duplicated crate-side logic and slowed Rust authority growth. | Added `compose_micro_tasks(_json)` in `core/layer2/execution/src/decompose.rs` + `execution_core compose` CLI path; TS decomposition primitive now consumes Rust-composed task drafts and keeps JS-only gates/duality overlays as thin orchestration. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-053 | done | Vault wrapper collapse to security-core authority | `client/runtime/systems/vault/index.ts` carried duplicate runtime-selection complexity despite Rust `security_core` already owning vault policy/load/evaluate/seal/rotate/audit commands. | Replaced vault wrapper internals with thin `security_core` delegation (`vault-load-policy`, `vault-evaluate`, `seal`, `rotate-all`, `audit`) while preserving compatibility exports and decision normalization. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-054 | done | Observability wrapper collapse to Rust-core authority | `client/runtime/systems/observability/index.ts` still contained extra runtime-selection branches that duplicated crate-level execution ownership and expanded TS surface area. | Replaced observability wrapper with thin Rust-core delegation (`observability_core load-profile/run-chaos`) while preserving compatibility exports and CLI fallback behavior. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-055 | done | Vault legacy policy engine collapse to Rust-core compatibility wrapper | `client/runtime/systems/vault/legacy_vault.ts` still contained a full TypeScript vault-policy evaluator duplicating logic already owned by Rust `security_core`. | Replaced legacy TS evaluator with a compatibility wrapper delegating to Rust-backed `client/runtime/systems/vault/index`, preserving exported API shape (`loadLegacyVaultPolicy`, `evaluateVaultPolicyLegacy`, `policyDigest`) and fail-closed fallback semantics. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-056 | done | Observability legacy engine collapse to Rust-core compatibility wrapper | `client/runtime/systems/observability/legacy_observability.ts` still contained a full TypeScript observability scoring engine duplicating logic owned by Rust observability core. | Replaced legacy TS observability evaluator with a compatibility wrapper delegating to Rust-backed `client/runtime/systems/observability/index`, preserving exported surface (`normalizeProfile`, `evaluateTraceWindow`, `computeSovereigntyIndex`, `runChaosObservabilityLegacy`) and fail-closed fallback semantics. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-057 | done | Task decomposition summary/queue/dispatch shaping migrated to Rust execution core | `client/runtime/systems/execution/task_decomposition_primitive.ts` still computed micro-task summaries, dispatch summaries, queue row shaping, dispatch row planning, governance lane finalization, and constitution/heroic gate evaluation in TypeScript despite Rust execution core ownership of decomposition/compose primitives. | Added `execution_core task-summary`, `dispatch-summary`, `queue-rows`, `dispatch-rows`, `apply-governance`, `directive-gate`, and `heroic-gate` commands with `summarize_tasks(_json)`, `summarize_dispatch(_json)`, `queue_rows_json`, `dispatch_rows_json`, `apply_governance_json`, `evaluate_directive_gate_json`, and `evaluate_heroic_gate_json` in `core/layer2/execution/src/decompose.rs`; task decomposition lane now delegates summary math, queue/dispatch row planning, heroic+constitution+duality governance finalization, and primary constitution+heroic gate decisions to Rust, while TS keeps fallback adapters only. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-058 | done | Directive gate runtime collapse to compact Rust-ready wrapper | `client/runtime/systems/security/directive_gate.ts` still held a large TypeScript policy evaluator that duplicated Rust `execution_core directive-gate` behavior and inflated TS surface area. | Replaced directive gate module with compact compatibility wrapper: runtime default is now Rust-preferred (`DIRECTIVE_GATE_RUST_MODE=prefer`), with deterministic JS fallback retained for fail-open binary unavailability and exported API/constants (`evaluateTask`, `logGateDecision`, `HIGH_RISK_PATTERNS`, `DENY_PATTERNS`, allowlist/trust helpers) preserved. Regression coverage passes in `client/memory/tools/tests/directive_gate.test.js`, plus execution parity/sovereignty suites. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-059 | done | Route-task live gate path switched to Rust-first directive evaluation | `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` previously consumed directive decisions through legacy JS-only mode, limiting Rust authority on the live routing choke point. | `route_task` now relies on global Rust-preferred directive gate mode (no per-call env mutation wrapper), keeping behavior deterministic while routing consults Rust `execution_core directive-gate` by default and falls back to JS when binary is unavailable. Regression + safety coverage passes in `client/memory/tools/tests/route_task_budget_guard.test.js`, `client/memory/tools/tests/route_task_emergency_stop.test.js`, `client/memory/tools/tests/directive_gate.test.js`, and execution sovereignty suites. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |

Regression + sovereignty coverage passes in `client/memory/tools/tests/weaver_core.test.js`, `client/memory/tools/tests/storm_value_distribution.test.js`, `client/memory/tools/tests/directive_gate.test.js`, and execution gate suites. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-061 | done | Route-task deterministic primitive shaping migrated into Rust execution core | `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` still computed intent normalization, trigger thresholds, predicted habit ids, and routing pre-shape metadata in TypeScript, leaving live route determinism partially outside Rust. | Added `execution_core route-primitives` (`evaluate_route_primitives_json`) in `core/layer2/execution/src/decompose.rs` to compute intent key/slug, A-B-C trigger thresholds, `which_met`, and predicted habit id; `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` now consumes Rust route primitives by default with JS fallback; regression test `client/memory/tools/tests/route_primitives_rust_parity.test.js` validates behavior. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-062 | done | Route-task habit selection (exact/token match) migrated into Rust execution core | `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` still selected matching habit IDs (`pickBestMatch`) in TypeScript, leaving route decision determinism split between TS and Rust. | Added `execution_core route-match` (`evaluate_route_match_json`) in `core/layer2/execution/src/decompose.rs` with exact/token deterministic matching + skip-habit support; `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` now uses Rust-first match resolution (`pickBestMatchRustAware`) with JS fallback; regression test `client/memory/tools/tests/route_match_rust_parity.test.js` validates exact/token/skip behavior. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-063 | done | Route-task reflex routine selection migrated into Rust execution core | `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` still selected reflex routines (enabled-state + direct-id + tag fallback) in TypeScript, leaving low-risk automation routing partially outside Rust determinism. | Added `execution_core route-reflex-match` (`evaluate_route_reflex_match_json`) in `core/layer2/execution/src/decompose.rs` with deterministic direct-id and tag-based selection; `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` now uses Rust-first reflex selection (`pickReflexMatchRustAware`) with JS fallback; regression test `client/memory/tools/tests/route_reflex_match_rust_parity.test.js` validates direct/tag/none behavior. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-064 | done | Route-task complexity classification migrated into Rust execution core | `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` still computed complexity tiers (`low|medium|high`) in TypeScript, leaving route policy pressure heuristics partially outside Rust ownership. | Added `execution_core route-complexity` (`evaluate_route_complexity_json`) in `core/layer2/execution/src/decompose.rs` with deterministic thresholds and reason codes; `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` now uses Rust-first complexity classification (`computeRouteComplexity`) with JS fallback; regression test `client/memory/tools/tests/route_complexity_rust_parity.test.js` validates high/medium/low decision boundaries. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-065 | done | Task decomposition queue/dispatch summary fallback removal (Rust fail-closed enforcement) | `client/runtime/systems/execution/task_decomposition_primitive.ts` still had large TypeScript fallback shapers for queue rows, dispatch rows, and summary math, preserving duplicated deterministic logic outside Rust when binary calls failed. | Removed TS fallback shapers in `emitQueues`, `dispatchMicroTaskExecutions`, and `summarizeTasks`; runtime now fails closed if Rust queue/dispatch/summary commands fail, while retaining Rust binary->cargo fallback chain. Regression and sovereignty suites remain green, proving Rust-owned shaping is authoritative on this lane. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-066 | done | Route-task Rust authority hardening (fail-closed on Rust route primitive/match/reflex/complexity outages) | `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` still silently fell back to TypeScript matching/classification when Rust outputs were unavailable, leaving duplicated deterministic routing logic active during outages. | Route-task now treats Rust route primitives, habit matching, reflex matching, and complexity classification as authoritative by default and returns `MANUAL` with explicit `route_error` when Rust outputs are unavailable/invalid; optional TS fallback is explicit via `ROUTE_TASK_TS_FALLBACK=1`. Regression and sovereignty suites stay green. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |
| BL-067 | done | Route-task aggregate Rust evaluation command integration (`route-evaluate`) | Route-task still orchestrated multiple separate Rust sub-calls (primitives/match/reflex/complexity), leaving aggregation/composition logic in TypeScript and increasing call-surface complexity. | Added `execution_core route-evaluate` (`evaluate_route_json`) aggregating primitives + habit match + reflex match + complexity in Rust; `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` now consumes this aggregate result as the primary routing decision substrate; regression coverage includes `client/memory/tools/tests/route_evaluate_rust_parity.test.js` and fail-closed outage behavior in `client/memory/tools/tests/route_task_rust_failclosed.test.js`. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |

Regression coverage includes `client/memory/tools/tests/route_decision_rust_parity.test.js`, route fail-closed tests, and sovereignty gate suites. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run. |

## V1 Hardening (Required for 6-Month Autopilot)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V1H-001 | done | Full-pipeline integration/e2e hardening | Current tests cover slices; unattended autonomy needs deterministic coverage across all major handoffs | Added deterministic full handoff integration with explicit failure-path coverage (`client/memory/tools/tests/pipeline_handoffs.integration.test.js`) validating timeout/rate-limit/rollback-style failures propagate into actuation receipts + `receipt_summary` failure taxonomy, alongside existing sensory/autonomy e2e tests in CI |
| V1H-002 | done | Release-gate + canary/rollback enforcement expansion | Existing checks exist but canary/rollback discipline must be mandatory for self-change and high-risk lanes | `improvement_controller start-validated` now requires policy-root authorization (lease + approval note) before self-change trial start, and medium-risk executable proposals are blocked when rollback path signals are missing (`medium_risk_missing_rollback_path`) Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v1h_002_anchor.ts`. |
| V1H-003 | done | Observability + SLO + runbook completion pass | Metrics/runbooks exist but unattended mode needs complete coverage with actionable alerts | Added explicit `verification_pass_rate` health SLO gate in `health_status`, enforced SLO->runbook coverage via `client/runtime/config/autonomy_slo_runbook_map.json` + `slo_runbook_check`, and added `Incident 8: Verification Pass-Rate Regression` procedures in `docs/client/OPERATOR_RUNBOOK.md` with regression tests |
| V1H-004 | done | Threat-model-driven security test pack | Governance controls need explicit abuse-path validation before long unattended windows | `docs/client/THREAT_MODEL_V1.md` now defines prioritized abuse paths and `client/memory/tools/tests/security_threat_pack.test.js` enforces deterministic regression tests for prompt-injection ingress, unauthorized mutation, egress bypass, secret isolation/exfiltration, policy-root lease misuse, and integrity tamper paths |
| V1H-005 | done | Contract/version governance closure across adaptive boundaries | Schema/versioning exists, but cross-layer drift checks must be exhaustive to prevent silent corruption | `schema_contract_check` now enforces adaptive store `expected_version` pinning per store contract (eyes/focus/client/cognition/habits/reflex/strategy), and CI fails closed on version drift Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v1h_005_anchor.ts`. |
| V1H-006 | done | Baseline adaptive-mutation safety kernel | Any topology/self-improvement expansion requires hard guardrails before promotion | Enforce mutation budget cap + TTL + quarantine + veto window + rollback receipt for all adaptive topology proposals, and fail closed when safety attestations are missing Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v1h_006_anchor.ts`. |
| V1H-007 | done | Baseline objective value-currency propagation | Keep autonomy aligned to objective-specific success definitions during unattended runs | Propagate directive-derived value currency into admission + queue ranking baseline paths, with deterministic audit fields and no regression to drift/safety SLOs Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v1h_007_anchor.ts`. |
| V1H-008 | done | Read-only recursive introspection baseline | Operator/system visibility into branch pressure and state is required before aggressive restructuring | Emit machine-readable branch health snapshot (workers, leases, queues, cooldowns, policy holds) and wire it into health/ops reporting without any auto-restructure actions yet Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v1h_008_anchor.ts`. |

## V2

### V2 Active Priority (Within Version)

| Order | ID | Status | Upgrade | Why This Order |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `V2-001` | done | Full repo TypeScript conversion (`.js` -> `.ts`) | Highest leverage for contract safety and migration debt reduction across all V2 work |
| 2 | `V2-003` | done | Dist runtime cutover + legacy paired JS retirement | Required to complete migration economics and avoid dual-runtime drift |
| 3 | `V2-009` | done | Exterior anti-sabotage shield + instant auto-reset | Hardens runtime integrity before more aggressive adaptive upgrades |
| 4 | `V2-041` | done | Per-candidate adversarial simulation lane | Converts heuristic risk into direct evidence before promotion |
| 5 | `V2-045` | done | Identity Organ (alignment anchor) | Must exist before deeper self-morphing/autonomous branching |
| 6 | `V2-046` | done | Regime Organ (task/environment sensing + morph trigger) | Enables adaptive topology routing once alignment anchor is in place |
| 7 | `V2-047` | done | Mirror Organ (active self-observation + self-critique) | Safe self-improvement loop after identity/regime foundations |
| 8 | `V2-044` | done | In-run workflow evolution lane | Runtime mutation should follow stronger gating and critique layers |
| 9 | `V2-012` | linked | External security assessment and pen-test cycle (reference) | Canonical blocker is tracked in the detailed `V2-012` row below; this priority row is reference-only |

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V2-001 | done | Full repo TypeScript conversion (`.js` -> `.ts`) | Improve long-term maintainability and refactor safety once V1 critical-path typing is stable | Core runtime is TS-owned end-to-end (`client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/lib/`), dist-runtime `legacy-pairs --strict=1` is green, and the only non-wrapper JS holdout is `client/runtime/lib/ts_bootstrap.js` by design |
| V2-002 | done | Explore polyglot service modules behind strict contracts | Enable targeted non-TS modules only where ROI is proven (ecosystem/security/hot path) without creating architecture drift | Implemented strict contract pilot via `client/runtime/systems/polyglot/polyglot_service_adapter.ts` + `client/runtime/systems/polyglot/pilot_task_classifier.py`, policy in `client/runtime/config/polyglot_service_policy.json`, contract doc in `docs/client/POLYGLOT_MODULE_CONTRACT.md`, and coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/polyglot_service_adapter.test.js` |
| V2-003 | done | Dist runtime cutover + legacy paired JS retirement | Remove long-term TS/JS dual-maintenance cost without breaking runtime contracts | Dist runtime mode + cutover controller landed (`client/runtime/lib/ts_bootstrap.js`, `client/runtime/systems/ops/dist_runtime_cutover.ts`, `docs/client/DIST_RUNTIME_CUTOVER.md`), `verify` now enforces `legacy_runtime_js_pairs` via `legacy-pairs`, final non-wrapper twin retired (`client/runtime/systems/fractal/regime_organ.js` wrapper-only), and regression test coverage is green |
| V2-006 | done | Task-type outcome learning upgrade | Improve model/tool routing quality using empirical task success patterns | Routing/ranking consume task-type capability outcomes with tested fallback adjustments in `client/runtime/systems/routing/model_router.ts` + `client/memory/tools/tests/model_router_routing_features.test.js` |
| V2-007 | done | Self-experimentation framework with promotion gates | Let system improve methods safely via evidence-based trials | Evidence-gated experimentation + promotion workflow is enforced by `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/lever_experiment_gate.ts` and `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/improvement_controller.ts` with rollback-aware receipts/tests |
| V2-008 | done | Global capability switchboard (security-locked) | Allow rapid feature/capability deactivation under drift or threat without allowing model-driven disablement of security controls | Implemented in `client/runtime/systems/security/capability_switchboard.ts` with signed policy-root gate, dual-control toggles, non-deactivatable security lanes, and immutable audit history (`client/runtime/config/capability_switchboard_policy.json`) |
| V2-009 | done | Exterior anti-sabotage shield + instant auto-reset | Prevent rogue external AI or out-of-channel mutations from silently corrupting system behavior | `anti_sabotage_shield` now emits explicit verification bundles + deterministic rollback plans per verify/watch incident, surfaces latest rollback hash in status, and keeps strict auto-reset/quarantine behavior covered by regression tests (`client/memory/tools/tests/anti_sabotage_shield*.test.js`). Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/security/anti_sabotage_shield.js`. |
| V2-010 | done | Chaos testing program (adaptive + orchestration lanes) | Stress resilience under controlled failures once V1 hardening is stable | Scenario-driven chaos receipts + strict recovery checks shipped in `client/runtime/systems/ops/chaos_program.ts` with policy + tests |
| V2-011 | done | Scale/performance benchmarking harness | Quantify headroom and bottlenecks before broader deployment | Reproducible throughput/latency/error/token-efficiency harness is live in `client/runtime/systems/ops/scale_benchmark.ts` with strict thresholds and historical reports |
| V2-012 | blocked | External security assessment and pen-test cycle (canonical blocker) | Independent validation reduces blind spots in internal security assumptions | Internal intake/closure tracking is ready (`client/runtime/systems/security/external_security_cycle.ts`), but completion is blocked on third-party assessor execution + signed external report delivery |
| V2-013 | done | Compliance/reporting automation (optional enterprise track) | Enterprise deployment often requires auditable policy/report workflows | Governance evidence index + SOC2 readiness report automation is implemented in `client/runtime/systems/ops/compliance_reports.ts` and `client/runtime/config/compliance_controls_map.json` |
| V2-014 | done | Multi-team/multi-environment release governance | Support organization-scale ownership without destabilizing autonomy | Dev/stage/prod promotion governance with owner boundaries + dual-approval protections is enforced by `client/runtime/systems/ops/environment_promotion_gate.ts` |
| V2-015 | done | Adaptive LLM seed -> specialist training (hardware-aware) | Build long-term AI sovereignty so each deployment can reduce external model dependence with user-specific specialization | Curation/planning/eval/promotion workflow with hardware profiles and gating is implemented in `client/runtime/systems/nursery/specialist_training.ts` + `client/runtime/config/nursery_training_policy.json` |
| V2-016 | done | Dynamic optimization floor by objective criticality | Prevent endless low-impact self-tuning while allowing tighter thresholds for accuracy-critical domains | Criticality-aware optimization floor decisions + `good_enough` receipts shipped in `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/objective_optimization_floor.ts` |
| V2-017 | done | Optimization aperture sensing controller (risk-adaptive) | Automatically adjust how aggressively the system pursues optimization based on sensed risk profile of active work | Risk/impact/budget/safety-driven aperture controller now emits lane recommendations + receipts via `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/optimization_aperture_controller.ts` |
| V2-018 | done | Ternary belief reasoning engine for spine state modeling | Representation efficiency: balanced ternary {-1, 0, +1} maps directly to {pain, unknown, ok} states with cleaner consensus/propagation logic than binary | `client/runtime/lib/trit.ts` + `client/runtime/lib/ternary_belief_engine.ts` are integrated into spine state modeling (`client/runtime/systems/spine/spine.ts`) with dedicated tests and daily telemetry |
| V2-018B | done | SOC2 Type I readiness pack (control mapping + evidence index) | Close the architecture-to-enterprise gap without full compliance overhead too early | SOC2 mapping + readiness generation is wired through `client/runtime/systems/ops/compliance_reports.ts` with control map `client/runtime/config/compliance_controls_map.json` and evidence index outputs |
| V2-019 | done | Disaster recovery game-days + RTO/RPO validation | Prove operational resilience, not just design intent | `client/runtime/systems/ops/dr_gameday.ts` now emits cadence-aware DR receipts (`run/list/status`) with retention controls, and `client/runtime/systems/ops/dr_gameday_gate.ts` enforces rolling pass-rate + RTO/RPO regression thresholds for release gating with immutable gate receipts |
| V2-020 | done | Incident postmortem learning loop (blameless + enforceable actions) | Turn failures into compounding operational maturity | `client/runtime/systems/ops/postmortem_loop.ts` now auto-creates incident templates, tracks corrective/preventive actions with owners/dates, and blocks closure until preventive checks are linked, verified passing, and resolved |
| V2-021 | done | Maintainer handoff pack + bus-factor guardrails | Reduce single-operator risk and improve team legibility at handoff | `client/runtime/systems/ops/handoff_pack.ts` now builds deterministic handoff packs (docs + architecture map + ownership matrix) and runs SLA-bounded simulation receipts over critical operator commands |
| V2-022 | done | Comprehensive documentation system (docs-as-code + coverage gates) | Make system legible and maintainable by external teams without tribal knowledge | `client/runtime/systems/ops/docs_coverage_gate.ts` now enforces critical-path docs mapping + broken local link checks, with policy in `client/runtime/config/docs_coverage_map.json`, merge guard integration, and required-checks workflow jobs |

Doctor reads structured autotest artifacts only, executes static allowlisted recipes, supports shadow-by-default operation, auto-trips kill-switch on suspicious/abnormal patterns, and emits health telemetry. |
| V2-049 | done | Signed recipe pack + immutable recipe release channel for autotest doctor | Prevent runtime mutation/gaming of repair behavior while allowing controlled recipe evolution | `client/runtime/systems/ops/autotest_recipe_release.ts` now seals/verifies signed recipe manifests, `client/runtime/systems/ops/autotest_doctor.ts` enforces recipe release gate with fail-closed apply-mode blocking on invalid/stale/tampered manifests, and tamper coverage is in `client/memory/tools/tests/autotest_recipe_release.test.js` + `client/memory/tools/tests/autotest_doctor_release_rollout.test.js` |
| V2-050 | done | Per-recipe sandbox verification and canary promotion for doctor actions | Ensure repair recipes are proven non-destructive before enabling apply-mode in production | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/autotest_recipe_verifier.ts` verifier state + doctor rollout lanes (`shadow -> canary -> live`) with per-recipe verification freshness checks, canary sampling, rollout state receipts, and integration coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/autotest_recipe_verifier.test.js` + `client/memory/tools/tests/autotest_doctor_release_rollout.test.js` |
| V2-051 | done | Broken-piece lab pipeline (rollback bundles -> dormant study queue -> safe reimplementation proposals) | Convert rolled-back failures into structured learning artifacts without unsafe auto-patching | Doctor now enqueues rollback bundles into deterministic lab queue (`state/ops/autotest_doctor/broken_lab_queue.jsonl`), and `client/runtime/systems/ops/broken_piece_lab.ts` clusters signatures + exports deterministic JSON/Markdown reimplementation proposals; coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/broken_piece_lab.test.js` |
| V2-052 | done | Independent repair-plane watchdog (out-of-band verifier) | Reduce single-controller compromise risk and detect doctor tampering/gaming attempts early | Added independent watcher `client/runtime/systems/ops/autotest_doctor_watchdog.ts` with history/state/attestation checks and block-file quarantine lane wired into doctor apply-mode; coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/autotest_doctor_watchdog.test.js` |

### Inversion Safety Hardening (Necessary Set)

All items below are treated as required for governed inversion safety (not optional tuning).

| ID | Version | Status | Necessary Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| INV-001 | V2 | done | Structured immutable-axiom matching (signal groups, not substring-only) | Prevent false-positive/false-negative dangerous-intent checks | `immutable_axioms` support `signals` + `min_signal_groups`, matcher enforces structured-action/subject/object logic |
| INV-002 | V2 | done | Tier-transition governance (first-N human veto + decay window floors) | Prevent premature autonomy escalation at deeper inversion tiers | `tier_transition` enforces first-N veto, minimum first-N floors, and minimum decay windows per target |
| INV-003 | V2 | done | Mandatory `objective_id` for belief+ tiers | Keep high-impact inversion attempts attributable and auditable | Belief/identity/directive/constitution runs block without valid `objective_id` |
| INV-004 | V2 | done | Evidence-backed attractor scoring with anti-gaming penalty | Reduce verbose-objective gaming and improve desired-outcome signal quality | Attractor uses `evidence_backing` + verbosity/low-diversity penalties and enforces target thresholds |
| INV-005 | V2 | done | True runtime execution checks in maturity probes | Ensure maturity reflects executed runtime behavior, not only decision-surface logic | Runtime probe gates require red-team executed cases + nursery/adversarial execution evidence |
| INV-006 | V2 | done | Live gating tracks attempts and successes (plus safe-abort relief) | Preserve safety while avoiding over-penalizing cautious abort behavior | Tier state persists attempts/successes/safe-aborts and first-N progress applies configured safe-abort relief |
| INV-007 | V2 | done | Immutable never-invert axiom set | Establish non-negotiable safety boundaries | Policy includes explicit non-invert axioms (constitution, sovereignty, guardrails, integrity, self-termination) |
| INV-008 | V2 | done | First-principle anti-downgrade lock | Prevent lower-confidence/lower-maturity rewrites of learned principles | Principle updates block on lower maturity and lower-confidence same-band downgrade attempts |
| INV-009 | V2 | done | Maturity harness uses real runtime probes | Keep maturity progression grounded in operational safety performance | Harness cycles include required runtime probes and fail closed on probe gate failures |
| INV-010 | V2 | done | Explicit shadow-pass kill switch before live apply | Stop live inversion when shadow safety conditions regress | Live apply blocks on shadow-pass deficit and critical-failure kill-switch thresholds |

| ID | Version | Status | Follow-on Hardening | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| INV-011 | V3 | done | Formal semantic/ontology axiom matcher | Improve precision beyond regex + token heuristics for complex adversarial phrasing | Added semantic role matcher (`client/runtime/systems/autonomy/inversion_semantic_matcher.ts`) wired into immutable-axiom detection with ontology aliases + adversarial regression coverage (`client/memory/tools/tests/inversion_live_ladder_semantic.test.js`) |
| INV-012 | V3 | done | Inversion live-graduation ladder (canary quotas + observer quorum) | Further reduce blast radius when enabling live inversion beyond shadow | Added live-graduation ladder gates in inversion controller (canary quota, observer quorum, regression rollback) plus observer approval command and regression coverage (`client/memory/tools/tests/inversion_live_ladder_semantic.test.js`) |
