| P0 | RM-114 | V2 | done | Signal-SLO deadlock breaker with automatic remediation escalation | 315+ failed streak indicates repeated unmet acceptance gates without deterministic operator escalation | `client/runtime/systems/ops/signal_slo_deadlock_breaker.ts` is wired into spine daily flow, emits thresholded escalation proposals with blocked-reason histograms, and records closure receipts when streak resets |
| P1 | RM-115 | V2 | done | Secrets-in-logs remediation (redaction + historical scrub utility) | Plaintext auth tokens in logs are a deployment blocker despite strong policy controls | Expanded redaction in `client/runtime/lib/tool_response_compactor.ts`, added `client/runtime/systems/security/log_redaction_guard.ts` (`run` + `scrub`), policy in `client/runtime/config/log_redaction_policy.json`, and coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/log_redaction_guard.test.js` |
| P1 | RM-116 | V2 | done | Model health auto-recovery lane (cooldown/timeout repair) | 5/12 model cooldowns reduce throughput and increase single-model fragility | `client/runtime/systems/ops/model_health_auto_recovery.ts` now performs retry/backoff checks, auto-bans unhealthy provider-local models for failover, clears bans on recovery, publishes daily pass-rate, and is executed by spine |
| P1 | RM-117 | V2 | done | Config rationalization wave 1 (typed registry + consolidation targets) | 1,266 JSON files create config drift, discoverability, and governance overhead | Added typed registry inventory + shape-fingerprint consolidation candidates (`client/runtime/systems/ops/config_registry.ts`), policy-managed legacy alias sync (`client/runtime/config/config_registry_policy.json`, `client/runtime/config/config_aliases.json`), and regression coverage |
| P2 | RM-118 | V2 | done | Observability actionability pack (alert-to-runbook + exec KPI view) | Telemetry quality is high but actionability is weak for operators/C-suite | Alert routing now auto-attaches runbook/owner metadata from `client/runtime/config/autonomy_slo_runbook_map.json`, and ops dashboard exports daily execution/yield/safety/queue-health KPI snapshots with trend deltas |
| 26 | RM-201 | V4 | done | Chaos engineering + self-healing automation | Validate resilience under real failure conditions and reduce MTTR | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/chaos_self_healing_automation.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/chaos_self_healing_automation_policy.json`) with scheduled fault slots, remediation/postmortem readiness checks, and deterministic receipts/state in `state/ops/chaos_self_healing_automation/*`; regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/v4_ops_pack.test.js`. |
| 27 | RM-202 | V4 | done | Distributed runtime (leader election + replicated queue/state) | Single-node orchestration limits reliability and scale | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/federated_sovereign_mesh_runtime.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/federated_sovereign_mesh_runtime_policy.json`) with attested peer join, deterministic leader election, replicated stream digests, partition drills, and auditable receipts/state (`state/ops/federated_sovereign_mesh_runtime/*`); coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/v4_mesh_runtime_pack.test.js`. |
| 28 | RM-203 | V4 | done | Cross-instance federated learning | Enable multi-instance improvement without centralized raw-data pooling | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/cross_instance_federated_learning.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/cross_instance_federated_learning_policy.json`) with summary-only contribution ingest, participant quorum aggregation, and deterministic receipts/state (`state/ops/cross_instance_federated_learning/*`); coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/v4_mesh_runtime_pack.test.js`. |
| 29 | RM-204 | V4 | done | Formal verification for critical protocols | High-autonomy systems need stronger guarantees than tests alone | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/critical_protocol_formal_suite.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/critical_protocol_formal_suite_policy.json`) with required-path formal checks plus delegated `critical_path_formal_verifier` execution and strict-mode pass/fail receipts; coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/v4_ops_pack.test.js`. |

## Narrow-Agent Supremacy Track (ROI Requirements)

Objective: eliminate categories where narrow agent stacks outperform Protheus (execution reliability, startup efficiency, adapter breadth, provider onboarding, and mobile viability).

| Priority | ID | Version | Status | ROI Requirement | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P0 | RM-119 | V2 | done | Execution reliability parity SLO closure | Narrow systems currently win on immediate end-to-end task completion | 30-day rolling SLO enforced via `client/runtime/systems/ops/execution_reliability_slo.ts`: `workflow_executor` success >=97%, `queue_drain_rate` >=0.90, `time_to_first_execution_ms` p95 <=120000, and no 7+ day zero-shipped streaks; consolidated status emitted daily at `state/ops/rm_progress_dashboard.json` |
| P0 | RM-120 | V2 | done | Adapter parity pack (top practical channels) | Narrow systems win on out-of-box integrations | `client/runtime/config/actuation_adapters.json` now ships deterministic adapters for webhook/http, browser, filesystem, shell, Slack, email, calendar, payments, git, CRM, and helpdesk lanes via `client/runtime/systems/actuation/multi_channel_adapter.ts` with governed dry-run/live execution and receipt coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/actuation_adapter_parity_pack.test.js` |
| P0 | RM-121 | V2 | done | Provider onboarding in one manifest | Narrow systems win on fast model/provider plug-in | `client/runtime/systems/routing/provider_onboarding_manifest.ts` now wires routing model profiles, spawn allowlist, provider budgets/guardrails, model adapter provider profiles, secret broker entries, and trainability policy from `client/runtime/config/provider_onboarding_manifest.json`, with receipts and contract coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/provider_onboarding_manifest.test.js` |
| P1 | RM-122 | V2 | done | Runtime efficiency floor (desktop seed) | Startup/client/memory/install footprint is a visible competitive gap | `client/runtime/systems/ops/runtime_efficiency_floor.ts` now emits cold-start/idle-RSS/install-size metrics + pass/fail receipts; `public_benchmark_pack` ingests and trends these metrics. Item closes when thresholds hold in rolling benchmark history (cold start <=300ms, idle RSS <=120MB, install artifact <=60MB) |
| P1 | RM-123 | V2 | done | Narrow-agent parity benchmark harness | Need objective proof of “at least as good” across common tasks | Added standardized parity harness in `client/runtime/systems/ops/narrow_agent_parity_harness.ts` (+ policy `client/runtime/config/narrow_agent_parity_harness_policy.json`) with scenario scorecards across reliability/latency/cost, strict pass/fail gates, weekly receipt publishing to `state/ops/parity_scorecards/*.json`, history/state outputs, npm scripts, and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/narrow_agent_parity_harness.test.js` |
| P1 | RM-124 | V3 | done | Phone Seed profile (usable, fast, bounded) | Seed accessibility on mobile is key to adoption and “run anywhere” goal | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/phone_seed_profile.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/phone_seed_profile_policy.json`) with sampled boot/RSS probes, workflow+memory latency probes, heavy-lane-disable embodiment gate validation, strict pass/fail receipts (`state/ops/phone_seed_profile/*`), package scripts, and guard wiring (`merge_guard`, `foundation_contract_gate`, `contract_check`) with regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/phone_seed_profile.test.js` |
| P1 | RM-125 | V3 | done | Surface-budget controller (hardware sensors -> capability envelope) | Automatic scale-down/up is required for hardware-agnostic operation | Added `client/runtime/systems/hardware/surface_budget_controller.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/surface_budget_controller_policy.json`) with tiered CPU/RAM/thermal/battery/network budget envelopes, auditable lane caps (dream/right-brain/inversion/fractal breadth), transition-cadence guardrails, and receipts (`state/hardware/surface_budget/*`); wired `runtime_scheduler` to enforce budget mode allowlists (`surface_budget_mode_block`) and exported package scripts/docs with regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/surface_budget_controller.test.js` and updated `runtime_scheduler`/foundation contract tests |
| P2 | RM-126 | V3 | done | Auto compression/expansion transfer plane | Need seamless “phone <-> desktop/server” morphing without manual rebuild | Added `client/runtime/systems/hardware/compression_transfer_plane.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/compression_transfer_plane_policy.json`) with deterministic state bundle capture, SHA256 digest attestation, verified restore (`digest_ok`) before apply, profile-rank auto action (`phone < desktop < cluster`), and replayable receipts/state (`state/hardware/compression_transfer_plane/*`); wired into merge/foundation/contract gates with docs and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/compression_transfer_plane.test.js` |
| P2 | RM-127 | V3 | done | Opportunistic offload from seed to stronger nodes | Phone viability depends on graceful heavy-task delegation | Added `client/runtime/systems/hardware/opportunistic_offload_plane.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/opportunistic_offload_policy.json`) with surface-budget/complexity routing, attested scheduling command integration, deterministic local fallback on offload failure, and auditable queue/receipt state (`state/hardware/opportunistic_offload/*`); wired guard contracts and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/opportunistic_offload_plane.test.js` |
| P3 | RM-128 | V4 | done | Continuous parity maintainer | Prevent future regressions vs narrow-agent UX/perf | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/continuous_parity_maintainer.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/continuous_parity_maintainer_policy.json`) to execute parity checks and open bounded remediation queue entries when score/status drifts under threshold; deterministic receipts in `state/ops/continuous_parity_maintainer/*`; coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/v4_ops_pack.test.js`. |
| P3 | RM-129 | V4 | done | Device-mesh adaptive organism runtime | Long-term moat is superiority across any hardware envelope | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/hardware/device_mesh_adaptive_runtime.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/device_mesh_adaptive_runtime_policy.json`) with mesh role assignment (leader/edge/cache/router/standby), load heartbeats, and runtime mode awareness from surface budget, with deterministic receipts/state in `state/hardware/device_mesh_adaptive_runtime/*`; coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/v4_mesh_runtime_pack.test.js`. |

## Constraint Bridge Track (Grok Intake, 2026-02-26)

Objective: close the immediate monetization/execution constraints (token burn, payment rails, client communication, cooldown rigidity, and value-safe high-reward opportunity capture) while preserving governance and drift controls.

| ID | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V2-WVR-001 | V2 | done | Weaver organ epic (metric-agnostic value orchestration layer) | Provide one elegant control layer that generalizes to any objective/value currency (revenue, time saved, knowledge, impact, reliability) while reusing existing organs | Objective contract accepts weighted value metrics + hard constraints (drift/budget/safety); planner emits bounded temporary tendrils/workflows with TTL + atrophy; decisions are trit-weighted and auditable; integrates with Eye/Regime/Mirror/Doctor/Nursery; 30-day run shows no critical SLO regression |
| V2-WVR-002 | V2 | done | Unified value metric schema + adapter interface | Prevent value logic from hard-coding to money-only lanes and keep future metrics pluggable | Introduce typed value schema (`metric_id`, unit, horizon, confidence, guardrails), adapter contract for ingest/scoring, and migration mapping for existing value-currency fields without breaking current workflows |
| V2-WVR-003 | V2 | done | Trit-weighted value arbitration engine | Resolve tradeoffs between high-value opportunities and drift/safety/cost constraints consistently | Arbitration engine outputs ranked actions with explicit reward/risk rationale, supports uncertainty-aware exploration caps, and blocks unsafe high-reward paths with deterministic reason codes + receipts |
| V2-BRG-001 | V2 | done | Token economics engine + smart autopause | Prevent burn-rate/autopause deadlocks that stall productive execution lanes | Per-workflow budget envelopes + predicted token cost are enforced pre-execution; dynamic throttling/defer queue activates before hard stop; 30-day receipts show no unplanned full-system autopause events while critical lanes remain in-budget |
| V2-BRG-002 | V2 | done | Payment skills bridge + secure payout lane | Remove payment execution bottleneck while keeping constitutional/payment safety controls | Stripe/PayPal/Mercury bridges run behind policy-root + dry-run/live gates, include payout risk checks, and emit immutable payout receipts with reversible hold/fail paths |
| V2-BRG-003 | V2 | done | Client communication organ (light) | Convert generated opportunities into delivered outbound actions and structured follow-up loops | Draft -> send -> follow-up lifecycle is operational for Upwork/email/Discord with escalation tiers; high-value or high-risk outbound actions require human gate; response tracking and retry backoff receipts are deterministic |
| V2-BRG-004 | V2 | done | Rate-limit intelligence layer | Replace fixed cooldown behavior with adaptive channel-specific throughput control | Cooldowns are model/platform-specific and tuned by quality, trust, and drift risk signals; high-trust channels can fast-path within policy bounds; rate-limit decisions are auditable and no lane exceeds provider hard limits |
| V2-BRG-005 | V2 | done | Safe high-value play detector | Distinguish profitable low-drift opportunities from risky high-volatility noise | Value-currency scoring incorporates reward potential, drift risk, and reversibility; high-value/low-drift plays auto-propose with simulation evidence and confidence; false-positive rate stays below configured policy threshold; depends_on `V2-WVR-002`, `V2-WVR-003` |
| V3-BRG-001 | V3 | done | Autonomous client relationship manager | Enable end-to-end client lifecycle handling with bounded human intervention | Added `client/runtime/systems/workflow/client_relationship_manager.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/client_relationship_manager_policy.json`) with governed case lifecycle + event flows (`negotiation`, `scope_change`, `dispute`, `repeat_business`), workflow-ref gate for auto handling, SLA tracking by event type, and strict rolling-window manual intervention evaluation (`manual_rate <= 0.05`) backed by deterministic state/receipts and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/client_relationship_manager.test.js` |
| V3-BRG-002 | V3 | done | Capital allocation + reinvestment organ | Let earned value fund controlled growth loops (ads/tools/compute/float) under policy constraints | Added `client/runtime/systems/budget/capital_allocation_organ.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/capital_allocation_policy.json`) with simulation-backed allocation gates, bucket share caps, per-bucket drawdown stop enforcement, settlement ledgering, and rolling risk-adjusted-return evaluation in strict mode; wired into guard contracts and added regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/capital_allocation_organ.test.js` |
| V3-BRG-003 | V3 | done | Drift-aware revenue optimizer | Maximize value velocity without violating alignment/safety envelopes | Added `client/runtime/systems/weaver/drift_aware_revenue_optimizer.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/drift_aware_revenue_optimizer_policy.json`) that reads high-value play + execution reliability signals, enforces drift-cap and SLO envelopes, emits conservative vs balanced-growth workflow/budget plans, and persists auditable optimization history/receipts; integrated into guard contracts with regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/drift_aware_revenue_optimizer.test.js` |

## Fortune-100 Gap Closures (Kimi Delta)

Objective: close remaining enterprise-operability gaps not already covered by existing DR/compliance/chaos items.

| Priority | ID | Version | Status | ROI Requirement | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P0 | RM-130 | V2 | done | Alert transport reliability hardening (Slack primary + fallback channels) | Current assessment shows alert routing/config fragility despite strong telemetry | `client/runtime/systems/ops/alert_transport_health.ts` now enforces synthetic probe transport with dedupe and deterministic fallback (`slack_webhook -> email_fallback -> local_fallback`), emits rolling 30-day success SLO state in `state/ops/alert_transport_health.json`, is wired into spine daily checks, and exports observability metrics/warnings |
| P0 | RM-131 | V2 | done | Compliance retention uplift (5-day -> tiered 90/180/365) | Enterprise audit and incident investigations need longer evidence windows | `client/runtime/systems/ops/compliance_retention_uplift.ts` now enforces policy-driven 90/180/365 tiering with hot retrieval index (`state/ops/compliance_retention_index.json`), compressed archive movement for non-hot tiers, monthly attestation receipts, package scripts, and daily spine execution/ledger events (`spine_compliance_retention_uplift`) under feature flags |
| P1 | RM-132 | V3 | done | SIEM bridge + correlation rule pack | Fortune-100 operations require centralized security telemetry and correlation | Added `client/runtime/systems/observability/siem_bridge.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/siem_bridge_policy.json`) with OTLP/CEF export, correlation rules (`auth_anomaly`, `integrity_drift`, `guard_denies`), and deterministic alert round-trip receipts. Wired status checks into merge/foundation/contract gates (`client/runtime/systems/security/merge_guard.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/ops/foundation_contract_gate.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/spine/contract_check.ts`), added package scripts (`ops:siem:*`), docs (`docs/client/SIEM_BRIDGE.md`), and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/siem_bridge.test.js` + updated `client/memory/tools/tests/foundation_contract_gate.test.js`. |
| P1 | RM-133 | V3 | done | SOC2 Type II execution track (post-Type-I) | Type I readiness exists, but ongoing control operation evidence is needed for enterprise trust | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/soc2_type2_track.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/soc2_type2_policy.json`) to compile 90+ day evidence windows from compliance history, enforce minimum run/coverage gates, track exception lifecycle (`exception-open`/`exception-close`) to closure, and emit on-demand attestation bundles. Wired status checks into merge/foundation/contract guards, added package scripts (`ops:soc2-type2:*`), docs (`docs/client/SOC2_TYPE2_TRACK.md`), and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/soc2_type2_track.test.js` + updated `client/memory/tools/tests/foundation_contract_gate.test.js`. |
| P1 | RM-134 | V3 | done | Predictive capacity forecasting + preemptive scaling playbook | Current capacity response is mostly reactive; forecast-driven ops reduces saturation incidents | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/predictive_capacity_forecast.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/predictive_capacity_forecast_policy.json`) with 7/30-day forecasts for queue depth, latency, token burn, and model cooldown risk, threshold-driven scaling recommendations (`steady`/`preemptive_scale`/`urgent_scale`), and realized-error tracking for matured forecasts. Wired status checks into merge/foundation/contract guards, added scripts (`ops:capacity-forecast:*`), docs (`docs/client/PREDICTIVE_CAPACITY_FORECAST.md`), and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/predictive_capacity_forecast.test.js` + updated `client/memory/tools/tests/foundation_contract_gate.test.js`. |

| V2-058 | V2 | done | Build watermark + soul-token anti-cloning guard | Raise cost of unauthorized cloning and prevent stolen copies from operating at full autonomy Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v2_058_anchor.ts`. | Guard is live in `client/runtime/systems/security/soul_token_guard.ts` with release attestation checks + shadow forcing on mismatch, and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/soul_token_guard.test.js` + `client/memory/tools/tests/workflow_executor_soul_token_gate.test.js` |

Foundation gate now enforces presence/content via legal checks. |

| SEC-M05 | V3 | done | Complete IP posture review (trade client/secrets/provisional patents) | Protect novel mechanisms and reduce strategic imitation risk | Added governed IP posture lane `client/runtime/systems/security/ip_posture_review.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/ip_posture_review_policy.json`, strategy doc `docs/client/IP_POSTURE_REVIEW.md`, invention register/counsel/evidence artifacts under `state/security/ip_posture_review/*`, and strict status contract for filing-decision traceability. |

## Helix Codex Track (Immortal Helix + Sentinel Protocol)

Objective: deliver a cryptographically chained runtime DNA layer with tiered defense, keeping default behavior fail-secure and recoverable while preserving a permanent quarantine path for confirmed malicious copies.

| ID | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V2-HLX-001 | V2 | done | Shadow-mode Helix manifest + signed root codex | Establish immutable digital DNA baseline before enforcement | Added explicit baseline lane in `client/runtime/systems/helix/helix_controller.ts` (`baseline`) plus codex/manifest/reweave-snapshot initialization with receipts; policy now carries shadow baseline contracts in `client/runtime/config/helix_policy.json` (`shadow_only`, reweave snapshot paths), and merge/contract/foundation gates enforce baseline viability before promotion. |
| V2-HLX-002 | V2 | done | Runtime strand verifier + tamper receipts | Detect unauthorized mutation in real time without blocking live operations yet | Helix attestation emits mismatch taxonomy (including manifest freshness/staleness), codex/signature verification results, append-only event receipts, and stable clear-state behavior on unchanged runs |
| V2-HLX-003 | V2 | done | Fail-secure quarantine + Doctor atomic reweave plan | Default response must be recoverable (stasis/quarantine/reweave), not destructive | `client/runtime/systems/helix/reweave_doctor.ts` now implements snapshot capture + policy-gated apply reweave (approval note required, shadow-mode deny-by-default) with quarantine/receipt paths; `client/runtime/systems/helix/helix_controller.ts reweave --apply=1` executes one-command recovery and refreshes manifest/snapshot. Regression proof in `client/memory/tools/tests/helix_reweave_apply.test.js`. |
| V3-031 | V3 | done | Critical-path Eye enforcement for helix attestation | Prevent high-risk execution when attestation trust is broken | `client/runtime/systems/eye/eye_kernel.ts` now enforces helix attestation on every route decision with advisory/enforced modes and reason-code propagation; default policy enables shadow advisory (`client/runtime/config/eye_kernel_policy.json`), and enforcement behavior is regression-tested (`client/memory/tools/tests/eye_kernel.test.js`) |
| V3-032 | V3 | done | Helix admission checks for Assimilation/Forge/Doctor grafts | Ensure all new organs are born helix-compliant instead of patched later | Added `client/runtime/systems/helix/helix_admission_gate.ts`; Assimilation/Forge now emit strand candidates, graft/promotion paths enforce helix admission checks before apply, and approved apply paths atomically refresh helix manifest with admission receipts (`client/runtime/config/helix_admission_policy.json`, `client/memory/tools/tests/helix_admission_gate.test.js`) |
| V3-033 | V3 | done | Sentinel protocol + confirmed-malice permanent quarantine lane | Differentiate recoverable drift from confirmed hostile copies and contain theft/rogue branches Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_033_anchor.ts`. | Added `client/runtime/systems/helix/confirmed_malice_quarantine.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/confirmed_malice_quarantine_policy.json`) and integrated it into Helix attestation (`client/runtime/systems/helix/helix_controller.ts`) so confirmed-malice outcomes now pass independent-signal + confidence thresholds, trigger permanent quarantine state/forensic receipts, and require human-approved release. Wired status enforcement into merge/contract/foundation gates with regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/confirmed_malice_quarantine.test.js` and updated Helix/foundation tests. |
| V3-034 | V3 | done | Soldier Ant Red Team Evolution | Convert red-team into adaptive immune swarm: friendly probing by default, hard containment only on high-confidence breach consensus | `client/runtime/systems/redteam/ant_colony_controller.ts` now runs peacetime/war morphing through triple-consensus gates (`helix tamper + sentinel agreement + confidence`), routes new grafts into 72h priority stress probes via `recentAssimilationTargets`, and emits after-action battle/wisdom/obsidian receipts. Enforcement is now wired into merge/contract/foundation lanes (`client/runtime/systems/security/merge_guard.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/spine/contract_check.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/ops/foundation_contract_gate.ts`) with regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/redteam_ant_colony.test.js`. |
| V3-038 | V3 | done | Gated Autonomous Self-Improvement Loop | Enable self-improvement with strong governance so capability growth cannot bypass safety and alignment constraints Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_038_anchor.ts`. | Added `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/gated_self_improvement_loop.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/gated_self_improvement_policy.json`) to enforce objective-bound proposals, simulation+red-team gate checks, staged `shadow->canary->live` progression, sandboxed apply/merge controls, and automatic rollback receipts on regression (`client/runtime/systems/autonomy/self_code_evolution_sandbox.js`). Wired status checks into merge/contract/foundation gates and added regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/gated_self_improvement_loop.test.js`. |

Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v4_clean_002_anchor.ts`. | V4-CLEAN-001 |
| V4-CLEAN-003 | security | V4 | done | Secret broker backend uplift (keychain/age additive) | Secret handling should be stronger without replacing working broker interfaces abruptly. | Add optional encrypted-at-rest backend(s) (OS keychain and/or age) behind `client/runtime/lib/secret_broker` provider interface; preserve current env/json providers as fallback; migration path + rollback path documented; rotation-check and broker tests pass in strict mode. | V4-CLEAN-001 |
| V4-CLEAN-004 | hygiene | V4 | done | Root scaffolding rationalization with contract-first moves | `drafts/`, `notes/`, `client/runtime/patches/`, `research/` include mixed utility and runtime-adjacent artifacts; bulk deletion is unsafe. | Classify each root scaffold directory into `runtime_required`, `docs_required`, `internal_only`; move only `internal_only` material under `.internal/` (gitignored) or archive; update root/docs/client/data-scope policies and scripts; `root_surface_contract` + dependent guards pass after move. | V4-CLEAN-001, V4-CLEAN-003 |

| V4-SCI-002 | scientific | V4 | done | HypothesisForge background trend + hypothesis engine | Trend/hypothesis generation is not yet continuously operational as a background scientific lane. | Promote adversarial challenger + cross-temporal delta capabilities into a continuous hypothesis service that ranks candidates by prior, VOI, and disconfirming experiment value; verify ranking quality and consent gates with regression tests + audit receipts; rollback via service disable flag and queue-lane fallback. | V4-SCI-003 | scientific | V4 | done | Reasoning mirror lane for Phase-1 UI | Operators cannot yet inspect "why this hypothesis" with confidence/evidence/disconfirming test trace in one coherent surface. | Add a reasoning mirror view contract exposing active hypothesis, confidence interval, evidence strength, key statistical outputs, disconfirming tests run, and next experiment suggestion; verify with UI contract tests + receipt linkage checks; rollback by hiding mirror panel while keeping base UI behavior intact. | V4-SCI-004 | scientific | V4 | done | Scientific mode integration + launch-safe feature flag | Scientific lanes need controlled integration with existing governance/routing without launch breakage. | Add `scientific_mode_v4` integration flag (default off) wiring scientific loop, hypothesis forge, and reasoning mirror through typed control plane + receipt logging + research/weaver/redteam gates; verify end-to-end gated behavior with strict integration tests; rollback by forcing flag off and preserving previous execution paths. | V4-SCI-005 | scientific | V4 | done | Autonomous long-horizon experiment scheduler | High-value hypotheses lack a governed, reversible scheduling lane for consent-gated experiment execution. | Add background experiment scheduler that prioritizes hypotheses by VOI + risk, enforces sandbox + consent timeout-default-deny, emits full scientific receipt chains, verifies scheduling/rollback behavior with integration tests, and supports explicit rollback to no-op scheduling mode. | V4-SCI-007 | scientific | V4 | done | Advanced statistical extensions for scientific engine | Current analytics bridge needs stronger causal/uncertainty/model-selection breadth for high-confidence scientific conclusions. | Extend analytics bridge with advanced causal models, uncertainty quantification, and model selection/ensembles with explicit result provenance in scientific receipts; include governed fallback to Python/Rust paths when needed; verify parity/accuracy contracts with regression tests and rollback to baseline analytics profiles. | V4-SCI-008 | scientific | V4 | done | Enhanced reasoning mirror for Phase-1 UI | Operators still need richer confidence calibration and disconfirming-evidence visibility before scientific-mode default promotion. | Upgrade reasoning mirror with real-time calibration metrics, uncertainty charts, explicit "what would change my mind" evidence targets, and one-click suggested experiment routing through consent-gated scheduler; verify UI + receipt linkage tests and rollback by disabling enhanced panel features behind the scientific flag. ## V4 Optional Illusion Reinforcement Track (Grok Intake Normalized, 2026-03-02)

Objective: add subtle, factual, low-noise reinforcement cues that improve perceived system maturity without fabricated claims, tone drift, or runtime overhead.

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V4-ILLUSION-001 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Optional Subtle Reinforcement Layer (`--illusion-mode`) | Presentation polish can still feel "small-team obvious" without quiet, cohesive operator-facing cues that communicate technical maturity under real feature flags. | Add an optional gated layer behind `--illusion-mode` / `PROTHEUS_ILLUSION=1` implementing: (1) Reasoning Mirror subtle footer showing factual settled-core state + live binary size when available, (2) CLI reinforcement epilogue for `protheusctl status` and `protheusctl settle` using calm clinical wording that never overclaims in non-settled mode, (3) post-reveal-only narrative easter-egg text path for "The Fort Was Empty" blog surface, and (4) one-flag enable/disable with zero performance impact and no code changes required outside flag plumbing. Enforce professional tone constraints (no meme/boast voice), and verify with snapshot/UX tests plus feature-flag contract tests in both settled and non-settled states. | V4-FORT-006, V4-FORT-007, V4-SETTLE-005 |
| V4-AESTHETIC-001 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Alien Technology Aesthetic Layer (`alien_aesthetic=1`) | Cross-surface tone and wording are still inconsistent, reducing the intended high-discipline system feel across CLI/UI/receipts. | Add a feature-flagged aesthetic/tone pack (`alien_aesthetic=1`) covering CLI (`protheusctl status/settle/audit/migrate`), Reasoning Mirror, `protheus-top`, settle logs, receipts, self-audit reports, and onboarding completion text with calm clinical language and no gimmick style. Reuse `V4-SETTLE-011` substrate fallback behavior and exact line contract, enforce professional-tone guardrails (no hype/humor/exclamation), and verify with snapshot/tone-contract tests plus zero measurable performance regression. | V4-ILLUSION-001, V4-SETTLE-011, V4-UX-002, V4-FORT-006 |
| V4-AESTHETIC-002 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Selective Ethereal Language Layer | Applying stylized language across all client/logs/receipts degrades scanability, dilutes tone, and weakens enterprise operator clarity. | Add scoped language policy that applies ethereal phrasing only to high-visibility, low-frequency moments (settle/coalescing, autogenesis emergence, major transition summaries, Reasoning Mirror summaries) while keeping routine client/logs/errors/receipts/debug output strictly clinical and greppable. Enforce short phrase limits (5-10 words), tense rules (present-progressive in-flight, simple-past completion), preserve exact fallback line from `V4-SETTLE-011`, and verify via snapshot/tone-contract tests plus no performance regression. | V4-AESTHETIC-001, V4-SETTLE-011, V4-FORT-006 |

## V4 Migration & Ecosystem Porting Track (Google Doc `16j8PyjyYt3CpzfR11jpQkG3fAhIPifQBUlBO_pYxfR0`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: execute a transparent, low-friction migration path from community-hosted repo surfaces to official org ownership while preserving user state and enabling import from external agent ecosystems.

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V4-MIGR-001 | migration | V4 | done | Core Migration Bridge + Workspace Port Command | Repo/org migration without first-class tooling risks user friction, state loss, and trust erosion. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/migration/bridge.rs` plus `protheusctl migrate --to <org/repo> [--workspace=<name>]` to move remotes, backup state, transfer client/runtime/config/client/cognition/habits/vault/client/memory/scientific receipts, and emit signed migration receipts with rollback-safe checkpoints. | V4-FORT-006, V4-SELF-001 |
| V4-MIGR-002 | migration | V4 | done | Community Repo Graduation Pack (Banner + Redirect + Pinned Issue) | Users on the legacy/community repo need a clear upgrade path to the official home without ambiguity. | Ship automation/docs for README migration banner, pinned issue text, and repo-description redirect artifacts; verify old→new path is one-click discoverable and references evidence-backed migration guidance. | V4-MIGR-001, V4-FORT-005, V4-FORT-006 |
| V4-MIGR-003 | interoperability | V4 | done | Universal Importers (OpenFang/CrewAI/AutoGen/LangGraph/Common Dumps) | Users coming from other agent stacks face high switching cost without structured import tooling. | Add `client/runtime/systems/migration/importers/` pluggable import surface with first-class OpenFang importer plus generic JSON/YAML/workflow graph adapters, CLI `protheusctl import --from=<engine> --path=<source>`, mapped-state summary reports, and contract tests for no-loss transforms. | V4-MIGR-001, V3-RACE-127 |
| V4-MIGR-004 | migration | V4 | done | Self-Healing Migration Daemon | Installations still pointing to legacy remotes/workspaces will drift unless migration is auto-detected and prompted. | Add startup migration detector/daemon that identifies legacy remotes, offers one-click guided upgrade, records consent + signed receipts, and integrates with `V4-SELF-001` findings for migration suggestions without forced mutation. | V4-MIGR-001, V4-SELF-001 |
| V4-MIGR-005 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Post-Migration Verification + Premium Completion Report | Migration completion needs hard integrity proof and user-visible confidence artifacts, not just command success. | Run post-migration validation for vault, habits, memory, scientific lanes, and routing; emit migration-complete report with transferred assets/perf deltas, consent-gated telemetry, and deterministic pass/fail receipts before marking migration finalized. | V4-MIGR-001, V4-MIGR-003, V4-MIGR-004 |

## V4 Settling Runtime Track (Google Doc `1IPYlZqAROFpN0bjJvaE3dJfU9EPugXFDI9KLS6PIa0Q`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: add a governed "settled runtime" path that compiles the active core into an optimized deployable form while preserving full auditability, reversibility, and safety gates.

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V4-SETTLE-001 | primitive-upgrade | V4 | done | Core Settling Engine (Compile + Memory-Map + Re-exec) | Runtime hot paths still carry avoidable overhead from mixed execution layers and process indirection after initialization. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/settle/settler.rs` and `protheusctl settle` to compile active-core artifacts into a sealed native runtime image, memory-map the image, and re-exec into settled mode with deterministic health checks; verify parity/performance receipts and explicit rollback by reverting to pre-settle runtime profile. | V4-RUST-001, V4-SCI-004, V3-RACE-044 |
| V4-SETTLE-002 | hardening | V4 | done | Signed Snapshot Vault + Revert/Edit-Core Round Trip | Settling cannot be safely adopted without a deterministic source snapshot and reversible edit workflow under cryptographic custody. | Store signed/encrypted source snapshot in vault on first settle, add `protheusctl settle --revert` and `protheusctl edit-core` flow to restore/edit/re-settle with full delta receipts, and verify complete round-trip reversibility with rollback-safe checkpoints. | V4-SETTLE-001, V3-RACE-163, V4-FORT-006 |
| V4-SETTLE-003 | extension | V4 | done | Target Abstraction for Binary + Ternary/Exotic Compile Flags | Settling architecture should not hard-code today's target assumptions if future hardware/runtime targets are introduced. | Add compile-target abstraction and feature flags for binary-first settlement plus optional ternary/exotic target simulation adapters, verify target-selection contracts and compatibility receipts, and provide rollback to default binary target on failed target validation. | V4-SETTLE-001, V4-RUST-003 |
| V4-SETTLE-004 | hardening | V4 | done | Promotion-Coupled Re-Settlement Policy | Lane promotions currently do not automatically refresh settled artifacts, allowing optimization drift from promoted runtime state. | Integrate settlement trigger into promotion/autogenesis pipeline with consent-gated policy, bounded cooldowns, and signed receipts per promotion cycle; verify promotion stability/non-regression and rollback by disabling auto-settle coupling via policy. | V4-SETTLE-001, V3-007, V4-SELF-001 |
| V4-SETTLE-005 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Settled-State Operator Surface in `protheus-top` | Operators lack a first-class view of settled runtime identity, efficiency gains, and edit/revert readiness state. | Add `protheus-top` settled-state panel exposing runtime hash, snapshot lineage, client/memory/latency deltas, and consent-gated edit-core action status; verify UI/receipt linkage and rollback by feature-gating panel without affecting settle runtime. | V4-SETTLE-001, V4-OBS-011 |
| V4-SETTLE-006 | primitive-upgrade | V4 | done | Modular System-Wide Settling + Rust BlobLoader | Core-only settling leaves major lanes/organs on mixed runtime paths and misses module-level optimization and isolation benefits. | Extend settling to module granularity across major organs/lanes with independent settled artifacts and a Rust `BlobLoader` that discovers, memory-maps, and wires module blobs at runtime; require per-module parity/perf receipts and rollback-safe fallback to source/runtime profile when module settle fails. | V4-SETTLE-001, V4-RUST-001, V3-RACE-044 |
| V4-SETTLE-007 | hardening | V4 | done | Per-Module Edit + Re-Settle Workflow (`protheusctl edit <module>`) | Settled modules require a deterministic, auditable edit path to avoid high-risk full-system rebuilds for localized changes. | Add module edit/re-settle workflow (`protheusctl edit <module>`) that restores signed module snapshot, opens editable workspace, recompiles only target module, and re-settles with full signed delta receipts and reversible checkpoints. | V4-SETTLE-002, V4-SETTLE-006, V3-RACE-163 |
| V4-SETTLE-008 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Multi-Module Settle Status Dashboard (`protheus-top`) | Operators cannot currently inspect per-module settled health, mapping status, or targeted re-settle readiness from one surface. | Enhance `protheus-top` with module-level settled inventory (size, map status, last settle, substrate, health), plus consent-gated actions for `re-settle all` and module-targeted edit/re-settle; verify UI-to-receipt traceability and rollback by feature-gating dashboard layer only. | V4-SETTLE-005, V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-007 |
| V4-SETTLE-009 | extension | V4 | done | Pluggable Ternary + Future Architecture Module Settle Support | Modular settling needs a uniform target abstraction so upcoming non-binary targets can be adopted without rewriting module orchestration. | Extend module settle pipeline with compile-time/runtime target plugins (binary default, ternary simulation, exotic target stubs), per-module target selection policy, and compatibility receipts; ensure fallback to binary target on failed target checks with no data loss. | V4-SETTLE-003, V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-011 |
| V4-SETTLE-010 | hardening | V4 | done | Autogenesis/Promotion-Coupled Differential Module Re-Settlement | System evolution events can drift settled artifacts unless changed modules are automatically re-settled under policy controls. | Integrate autogenesis/promotion hooks that compute affected module set and trigger differential module re-settle with cooldown + consent policy, signed receipts, and non-regression gates; fallback by disabling auto-trigger while preserving manual settle controls. | V4-SETTLE-004, V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-007, V4-SELF-001 |
| V4-SETTLE-011 | extension | V4 | done | Preferred Advanced Substrate Detection + Graceful Binary Fallback | Settling currently lacks a deterministic substrate preference chain and auditable fallback behavior for emerging compute targets. | During settle, probe in priority order: balanced ternary substrate (hardware/emulator) then qubit access providers (stub/mock-capable). If neither is available, emit exactly once per settle (unless explicit verbose mode) the line: "No ternary substrate or qubit access detected. Reverting to binary mode." Record probe attempts, selected substrate, and fallback reason as signed receipts, keep probes pluggable/future-proof, and verify zero measurable performance regression on standard binary path. | V4-SETTLE-001, V4-SETTLE-003, V4-SETTLE-005 |

## V4 Rust Acceleration Track (Grok Intake Normalized, 2026-03-02)

Objective: accelerate real Rust-first runtime ownership (not cosmetic language-bar gaming) so performance, safety posture, and external technical credibility move together under parity/safety gates.

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V4-RUST-001 | primitive-upgrade | V4 | done | Authoritative Rust Microkernel Cutover Acceleration | Rust slices exist, but default runtime ownership across core hot paths is still mixed and not yet presented as a single launch-grade cutover program. | Implemented authoritative cutover lane in `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_authoritative_microkernel_acceleration.ts` with command-gate execution, tracked-source language composition report generation, strict gating, and persisted receipts. Verified by `client/memory/tools/tests/rust_authoritative_microkernel_acceleration.test.js`. | V3-RACE-021, V3-RACE-034, V3-RACE-220, V3-RACE-185, V3-RACE-186 |
| V4-RUST-002 | scientific | V4 | done | Scientific Stack Rust Orchestration + R Bridge Hardening | Scientific orchestration is still primarily TS lane composition, and R integration is present but not fully anchored to Rust-first control flow where performance-sensitive. | Move scientific orchestration hot paths (scientific loop + hypothesis scheduling + analysis dispatch) to Rust-backed lanes while preserving optional R backend usage via governed artifact/bridge contracts; add parity/accuracy/rollback receipts proving no scientific regression. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js`. | V4-SCI-001, V4-SCI-002, V4-SCI-004, V3-RACE-361, V4-RUST-001 |
| V4-RUST-003 | hardening | V4 | done | Cargo Workspace Professionalization + Rust CI Quality Signal | Rust credibility and maintainability are weakened without one disciplined workspace, explicit MSRV/toolchain policy, and first-class CI evidence. | Implemented Rust workspace quality gate in `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_workspace_quality_gate.ts` enforcing manifest/toolchain/doc presence with policy-gated cargo command checks and deterministic status/receipts. Verified by `client/memory/tools/tests/rust_workspace_quality_gate.test.js`. | V4-RUST-001, V3-RACE-230, V3-RACE-043 |
| V4-RUST-004 | extension | V4 | done | Habits/Vault/Adaptive Core Rust Migration Wave (Phased) | High-frequency adaptive loops in client/cognition/habits/vault/core layers remain predominantly TS, limiting end-to-end Rust runtime ownership and hotspot efficiency. | Execute phased migration plan for client/cognition/habits/vault/adaptive-core hotspots with parity harness + rollback receipts per phase; preserve TS for UI/plugin/compatibility surfaces only. If reliability/perf gates remain green, track progression toward 75-85% Rust tracked-source share in 4-6 weeks post-launch. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js`. | V4-RUST-001, V4-RUST-003, V3-RACE-011, V3-RACE-163 |

## V4 1M User Scale Readiness Track (Operator Directive, 2026-03-02)

Objective: make million-user scale an engineered capability with explicit throughput/reliability/cost gates, not a narrative target.

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V4-SCALE-001 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Load Model + SLO Contract (1k -> 10k -> 100k -> 1M) | Scale claims are not actionable without one canonical load model, traffic envelope, and SLO/error-budget contract. | Define and version a canonical scale model (`DAU`, peak concurrency, RPS, payload mix, write/read ratios) with stage gates (`1k/10k/100k/1M`) and hard SLO/error-budget criteria; publish receipts from reproducible load profiles before each stage promotion. | V4-RUST-001, V3-RACE-221, V3-RACE-309 |
| V4-SCALE-002 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Stateless App-Tier + Horizontal Autoscaling Contract | Control-plane/app-tier coupling still risks vertical bottlenecks and non-deterministic saturation behavior under burst load. | Enforce stateless worker contract for scale lanes, add autoscaling policy with saturation safeguards (CPU/client/memory/queue depth/latency), and prove horizontal scale behavior in staged load tests with rollback-safe scaling policies. | V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-202, V3-RACE-034 |
| V4-SCALE-003 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Durable Async Pipeline (Queue/Retry/Idempotency/Backpressure) | Million-user traffic requires async durability; missing idempotency/backpressure semantics will cause duplicate work and cascade failures. | Add durable queue contracts with idempotency keys, bounded retries, dead-letter handling, and explicit backpressure controls; verify no duplicate side effects and stable queue drain under stress/failure drills. | V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-078, V3-RACE-044 |
| V4-SCALE-004 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Data Plane Scale Program (Partitioning + Read/Write Split + Online Migration) | Current data paths are not yet governed by one end-to-end partitioning and migration safety contract for high-scale write/read pressure. | Implement partitioning/sharding strategy, read/write split policy, and online migration runbooks with deterministic rollback; validate p95/p99 latency and correctness during re-shard/migration simulations before stage promotion. | V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-118, V3-RACE-044 |
| V4-SCALE-005 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Caching + Edge Delivery Contract (CDN + Hot-Key + Invalidation) | Without disciplined edge/cache contracts, backend saturation and stale-data bugs will block high-concurrency growth. | Add canonical cache hierarchy (edge/CDN + service cache + hot-key controls), strict invalidation semantics, and cache-health SLOs; prove backend load reduction and freshness correctness in staged load tests. | V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-174, V3-RACE-127 |
| V4-SCALE-006 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Multi-Region Resilience + Disaster Recovery Drill Program | Single-region assumptions and untested failover paths are incompatible with million-user availability targets. | Implement active/standby or active/active multi-region contract with RTO/RPO objectives, deterministic failover/failback drills, and backup-restore verification receipts tied to release gating. | V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-057, V2-019, V4-002 |
| V4-SCALE-007 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Release Safety at Scale (Canary + Feature Flags + Schema Compatibility) | High-scale deployments amplify blast radius; release controls must be mandatory and schema-safe under live traffic. | Enforce progressive delivery policy (canary ramps, automated rollback thresholds, feature-flag kill paths, forward/backward schema compatibility checks), with promotion blocked when safety receipts are missing. | V4-SCALE-001, V1H-002, V3-RACE-114 |
| V4-SCALE-008 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Observability + SRE Operations Maturity Pack | Million-user operations require full observability, actionable alerts, and practiced incident response beyond basic telemetry. | Complete metrics/tracing/logging coverage for critical flows, define page policies and runbooks with drill SLAs, and verify incident response performance in recurring game-days with auditable outcomes. | V4-SCALE-001, V1H-003, V3-RACE-012 |
| V4-SCALE-009 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Abuse/Security Hardening at Scale (Rate-Limit + Tenant Isolation + Auth) | User growth increases attack/abuse surface and noisy-neighbor risk; baseline controls are insufficient without explicit scale hardening contracts. | Add tiered rate-limit/abuse controls, tenant-isolation invariants, and auth/session hardening with auditability; validate through adversarial load tests and fail-closed policy checks before each scale-stage promotion. | V4-SCALE-001, V1H-004, V3-RACE-051 |
| V4-SCALE-010 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | Capacity + Unit Economics Governance (p95/p99 + Cost per User) | Scale can fail economically even when technically stable if cost/perf budgets are not enforced continuously. | Define capacity planning model and per-user cost guardrails, enforce latency/cost budgets in release gates, and emit periodic cost-efficiency receipts proving sustainable growth through each scale stage. | V4-SCALE-001, BL-038, V3-RACE-221 |

## Post-Launch Base Migration Readiness (6-12 Weeks)

Objective: migrate from OpenClaw base only after launch stability is proven and extraction can be done without degrading autonomy, safety, or rollback posture.

| ID | Window | Status | Requirement | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PLM-001 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | 30-day operational stability streak | Prevent migration during churn/regression windows | Implemented PLM gate evaluator `client/runtime/systems/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness.ts` consuming execution/workflow reliability signals and producing auditable PLM-001 status in `state/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness/latest.json`. |
| PLM-002 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Runtime guardrail pass-rate gate | Migration should not begin if foundation checks are noisy/flaky | PLM gate pack now evaluates CI/guardrail health inputs and persists deterministic pass/fail telemetry for PLM-002 in `state/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness/receipts.jsonl`. |
| PLM-003 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | JS exception floor before extraction | Avoid carrying legacy JS debt into new base | PLM gate pack enforces JS exception-floor evidence from `state/ops/js_holdout_audit/latest.json` with threshold policy in `client/runtime/config/post_launch_migration_readiness_policy.json`. |
| PLM-004 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Primitive/profile coverage threshold | Ensure abstraction-first architecture survives base split | PLM gate pack evaluates profile-driven execution coverage via adapter defragmentation ratio and emits PLM-004 check output in readiness artifacts. |
| PLM-005 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Canonical state portability contract | Preserve deterministic replay and continuity after extraction | PLM gate pack binds PLM-005 to state-kernel cutover parity/replay evidence (`state/ops/state_kernel_cutover/latest.json`) and records deterministic readiness results. |
| PLM-006 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Dual-run parity harness | Validate behavior equivalence before switching control-plane | PLM gate pack checks dual-run parity + profile compatibility (`state/ops/narrow_agent_parity_harness.json`, `state/ops/profile_compatibility_gate/latest.json`) and receipts PLM-006 status. |
| PLM-007 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Cutover/rollback playbook | Extraction must be reversible under incident pressure | Added formal playbook `docs/client/POST_LAUNCH_MIGRATION_READINESS.md` with rollback linkage to `docs/client/release/templates/rollback_plan.md`; PLM gate validates artifact presence and logs PLM-007 status. |
| PLM-008 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Independent bootstrap + packaging readiness | New base must be self-hosting, not manual-fragile | PLM gate pack verifies bootstrap/package readiness inputs (`state/ops/self_hosted_bootstrap/latest.json`, `state/ops/deployment_packaging/latest.json`) and emits PLM-008 decision telemetry. |
| PLM-009 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Security and secrets migration proof | Avoid introducing a weaker trust boundary in new base | PLM gate pack validates secret-rotation attestation + heartbeat/supply-chain health (`client/runtime/config/secret_rotation_attestation.json`, `state/security/remote_tamper_heartbeat/latest.json`, `state/security/supply_chain/latest.json`) for PLM-009. |
| PLM-010 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Final migration go/no-go review | Force explicit operator decision after evidence collection | Added signed final-review command (`final-review`) in `client/runtime/systems/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness.ts` with persisted decision artifact `state/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness/final_review.json`. |

## Foundation Hardening Sprint (7-14 Days)

Objective: pause feature velocity briefly to harden core runtime abstractions so future revenue/capability work lands on deterministic, primitive-first foundations.

| ID | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V2-FND-001 | V2 | done | Primitive Catalog + Universal Action Grammar Baseline | Prevent bespoke runtime spread by making primitive mapping mandatory immediately | Primitive catalog manifest exists with hard mapping gate for new execution features; CI blocks feature PRs without primitive/opcode mapping; aligns into `V3-042` |
| V2-FND-002 | V2 | done | Event-Sourced Canonical State Baseline | Deterministic replay is required for phone->cluster scaling and safe debugging | Core execution lanes emit append-only event log with replay verifier and drift checks; baseline replay report generated per run; aligns into `V3-044` |
| V2-FND-003 | V2 | done | Policy VM Separation Baseline | Governance logic must be isolated from runtime execution to prevent coupling drift | Policy evaluation lane extracted behind VM boundary for targeted core paths, with contract tests proving no direct policy branching inside execution primitives; aligns into `V3-044` |
| V2-FND-004 | V2 | done | Foundation Contract CI Gate | Automatically enforce abstraction-first and anti-specialization rules | CI fails on bespoke branch growth where profile/primitive path exists, enforces abstraction debt budget, and requires dependency declarations for new runtime modules |
| V2-FND-005 | V2 | done | Scale Envelope Continuous Testing Baseline | Validate invariant behavior across hardware envelopes before expanding autonomy/revenue lanes | Daily envelope tests run on phone-seed profile, desktop profile, and simulated cluster with deterministic outcome parity thresholds; emits regression receipts for release gating |

## Foundation Risk Register (Fast-Shipping Debt)

Objective: track high-impact foundation risks that can silently compound while shipping fast, with explicit ownership and dated mitigations.

| Risk ID | Status | Risk | Why It Can Bite Later | Mitigation / Linked Backlog | Owner | Target Date |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| RR-001 | done | Config and flag fragmentation | Contradictory policy/flag states create non-deterministic runtime behavior | Add precedence + conflict lint and block conflicting gate combinations in CI; linked: `V2-FND-004`, `V3-044` | Platform/Governance | 2026-03-08 |
| RR-002 | done | Duplicate execution pathways | Parallel lanes drift and produce inconsistent behavior under load | Define one canonical execution path per capability and deprecate alternates with expiry receipts; linked: `V3-039`, `V3-040` | Runtime | 2026-03-10 |
| RR-003 | done | Schema drift without migration discipline | Replay/federation break when events/profiles evolve ad hoc | Enforce N-2 schema contract + migration plans for any schema change; linked: `V3-049` | Data Plane | 2026-03-12 |
| RR-004 | done | Determinism gaps in replay | Debugging and cross-hardware parity become unreliable | Deterministic mode + replay parity as release blocker on core lanes; linked: `V2-FND-002`, `V2-FND-005`, `V3-044` | Runtime + QA | 2026-03-15 |
| RR-005 | done | Operational complexity debt | Organ/lane growth raises MTTR and hides failure modes | Enforce complexity budget and net-simplification gate for new organs; linked: `V3-052`, `V2-FND-004` | Architecture | 2026-03-18 |
| RR-006 | done | Key and secret lifecycle fragility | Strong runtime guards fail if rotation/recovery paths are weak | Rotate/revoke/recover drills with audited ceremonies and failover tests; linked: `V3-051` | Security | 2026-03-20 |
| RR-007 | done | Supply-chain trust gap | Build/dependency compromise bypasses runtime protections | Signed reproducible builds + SBOM + provenance verification in release CI; linked: `V3-048` | Build/Release | 2026-03-22 |
| RR-008 | done | Multi-node partition and split-brain semantics | Scale-out behavior can diverge and corrupt global state | Deterministic quorum + partition simulation tests + failover receipts; linked: `V3-046`, `V2-FND-005` | Distributed Systems | 2026-03-25 |
| RR-009 | done | Data retention/churn bloat | Storage and signal quality degrade over time | Retention tiers, compaction policy, and "why-kept" tags on long-lived artifacts; linked: `V2-FND-002`, `V3-053` | Data + Ops | 2026-03-27 |
| RR-010 | done | Human override ergonomics under incident stress | Safety controls fail in practice if operators cannot act quickly | Timed operator drills with runbook SLA gates and usability fixes; linked: `V2-057`, `V2-FND-005` | Ops/UX | 2026-03-30 |
| RR-011 | done | Catastrophic recovery gap | Total-loss scenarios can break continuity despite local self-healing | Implement deterministic resurrection protocol with tested recovery ceremonies; linked: `V3-056` | Continuity + Security | 2026-04-02 |
| RR-012 | done | Long-horizon value drift in Heroic Echo | Alignment can narrow subtly over years without explicit renewal and review | Add periodic value-anchor renewal with reversible updates and user review gates; linked: `V3-057` | Ethics/Governance | 2026-04-05 |
| RR-013 | done | Explainability debt at scale | Future operators/successors may not reconstruct why critical actions occurred | Require canonical explanation artifacts for major decisions and self-modifications; linked: `V3-058`, `V2-063` | Platform/Observability | 2026-04-07 |
| RR-014 | done | External world-model staleness | Tool/profile drift causes silent execution and compliance regressions | Add scheduled world-model freshness loop with staleness SLO gates; linked: `V3-060`, `V2-067`, `V3-039` | Research + Runtime | 2026-04-10 |

## Obsidian Track (Isolated from Core Protheus)
Implementation (2026-03-01): Delivered `client/runtime/systems/obsidian/obsidian_phase_pack.ts` (+ wrapper/policy/test) covering wisdom projection, ops cards, note-intent compiler, canvas generation, cross-view identity bus sync, plugin control lane, phone-seed bounded bundles, and resilience checks.

Policy: this track ships behind explicit feature flags and separate policies so Obsidian integration cannot bypass or destabilize core system lanes.

| Phase | Scope | Entry Gate | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 | Read/write foundation, governance-first | Core execution/security lanes stable; no unresolved P0 core incidents | Obsidian bridge runs read-only + governed write projection with receipts, no bypass paths |
| Phase 2 | Bi-directional intent + canvas intelligence | Phase 1 stable for 30 days, conflict/loop rates within policy | Note intents can compile to proposals safely, auto-canvas is canary-gated and reversible |
| Phase 3 | Unified mind projection + mobile seed mode | Phase 2 stable with acceptance precision target met | Holo and Obsidian share identity graph links; mobile plugin mode is bounded and policy-enforced |

| ID | Phase | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| OBS-001 | Phase 1 | done | Obsidian bridge contracts + policy root | Prevent uncontrolled vault access or action bypass via notes | Implemented `client/runtime/systems/obsidian/obsidian_bridge.ts` + `client/runtime/config/obsidian_bridge_policy.json` with typed contract loading, path allowlists, clearance thresholds, and deny-by-default projection controls |
| OBS-002 | Phase 1 | done | Vault watcher sensory ingest (read-only) | Capture user knowledge flow safely before enabling any actuation coupling | Implemented `client/runtime/systems/obsidian/vault_watcher.ts` (read-only snapshot/diff ingest loop) with idempotent ingest event IDs and no write side effects outside governed bridge receipts |
| OBS-003 | Phase 1 | done | Governed markdown projection lane | Human-readable outputs must appear in vault without editing user-authored content by surprise | Projection writes route through bridge policy roots only, with elevated override gates and projection markers for source tagging/traceability |
| OBS-004 | Phase 1 | done | Sync integrity + conflict loop guard | Bidirectional sync can corrupt content without strict loop/conflict controls | Hash/idempotent ingest index, source tagging, projection loop suppression, deterministic conflict receipts, and regression coverage via `client/memory/tools/tests/obsidian_bridge.test.js` |
| OBS-005 | Phase 1 | done | Wisdom projector v1 | First-principle outputs need durable, linkable memory on human side | New principles generate linked markdown notes with provenance, backlinks, and reference to corresponding holographic node IDs |
| OBS-006 | Phase 1 | done | Doctor/execution visibility cards | Operational legibility should be available in Obsidian without reading raw logs | Rollbacks/heals/executions produce timestamped cards/notes with receipt links and status transitions |
| OBS-007 | Phase 2 | done | Intent compiler from notes (shadow->canary->live) | Converting notes to action is high leverage and high risk | Note intents compile to proposals in shadow first; precision/false-positive gates must pass before any live path |
| OBS-008 | Phase 2 | done | Automatic canvas intelligence | Users need structured planning artifacts generated from high-level intents | Canvas generator creates/updates node maps, linked notes, and live status blocks with reversible patch receipts |
| OBS-009 | Phase 2 | done | Cross-view identity bus (Obsidian <-> Holo) | Two interfaces should behave like one mind, not separate tools | Shared entity IDs enable click/highlight sync between Obsidian graph/canvas and holo graph for mapped nodes |
| OBS-010 | Phase 2 | done | Desktop Obsidian plugin (control + veto UI) | Human needs fast approval/veto at interface layer | Plugin exposes pending actions, approval/veto controls, and receipt drilldown with governance reason codes |
| OBS-011 | Phase 3 | done | Phone-seed Obsidian plugin mode | Mobile continuity requires lightweight bounded integration | Mobile mode syncs selected vault segments with policy-bounded status/intent surfaces and no heavy background lanes |
| OBS-012 | Phase 3 | done | Unified projection and resilience hardening | Final integration should feel seamless while remaining sovereign and reversible | Obsidian + holo projections share consistent state, survive reconnect/replay scenarios, and maintain zero policy-bypass findings in red-team tests |

| ID | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |

### V3 – Ethical Assimilation & Storm Layer

| ID            | Status | Upgrade Name                                      | Why It Matters                                                                 | Exit Criteria |
|---------------|--------|---------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------|

| V3-ATTR-002   | done   | Storm Value Distribution Layer                    | High-level layer that handles compensation, payouts, and compliance using the primitive. | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/storm/storm_value_distribution.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/storm_value_distribution_policy.json`; consumes attribution records, enforces constitution gate, generates reversible payout plans (plan/reverse/status) with receipts and creator feedback loop, validated by `client/memory/tools/tests/storm_value_distribution.test.js`. |
| V3-ATTR-003   | done   | Creator Opt-In & Public Assimilation Ledger       | Turns assimilation into a positive, public, incentivized event.                | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/storm/creator_optin_ledger.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/creator_optin_ledger_policy.json`; supports opt-in/out, contribution tracking, privacy-preserving public ledger projection, badges/tiering, passport-linked receipts, and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/creator_optin_ledger.test.js`. |
| V3-TASK-001   | done   | Task Decomposition Primitive + Parallel Micro-Task Execution | Turns large goals into executable micro-work, enabling fast autonomous and crowd-assisted progress at scale. | Implemented in `client/runtime/systems/execution/task_decomposition_primitive.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/task_decomposition_primitive_policy.json`; emits standardized micro-task profiles with Weaver/Storm parallel routing, Heroic Echo + constitution gates, duality indicators, and per-task attribution/passport linkage; validated by `client/memory/tools/tests/task_decomposition_primitive.test.js`. |
| V3-ASSIM-004  | done   | Memory Evolution Primitive (MemRL-inspired)       | Runtime RL directly on episodic memory for true self-evolution without retraining or human labels. | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/assimilation/memory_evolution_primitive.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/memory_evolution_primitive_policy.json`; two-phase retrieval (episodes + causal graph), Q-value updates, Doctor feedback queueing, and shadow-first receipts validated in `client/memory/tools/tests/memory_evolution_primitive.test.js` + assimilation integration tests. |
| V3-ASSIM-005  | done   | Context Navigation Primitive (RLM-inspired)       | Code-based recursive navigation of massive external data/context without stuffing token windows. | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/assimilation/context_navigation_primitive.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/context_navigation_primitive_policy.json`; recursive context decomposition/filtering with executable profile output for universal execution lanes and token-bloat reduction metrics, validated in `client/memory/tools/tests/context_navigation_primitive.test.js` + assimilation integration tests. |
| V3-ASSIM-006  | done   | Generative Simulation Mode                         | Synthetic digital-twin environments for stress-testing plans before real execution. | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/assimilation/generative_simulation_mode.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/generative_simulation_mode_policy.json`; staged synthetic scenario simulation (beta stage lock, drift/safety/yield verdicts, Heroic Echo/constitution blockers), integrated into assimilation gating and validated by `client/memory/tools/tests/generative_simulation_mode.test.js` + assimilation integration tests. |
| V3-ASSIM-007  | done   | Collective Reasoning Primitive                     | Explicit trust/accountability protocol for multi-agent coordination under uncertainty. | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/assimilation/collective_reasoning_primitive.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/collective_reasoning_primitive_policy.json`; quorum/trust-weighted consensus + delegation profiles + trust ledger updates, emitted weaver collective profile stream (`state/autonomy/weaver/collective_reasoning_profiles.jsonl`), and validated by `client/memory/tools/tests/collective_reasoning_primitive.test.js` + assimilation integration tests. |
| V3-ASSIM-008  | done   | Environment-Centric Evolution Layer               | Agents evolve primarily through environment feedback rather than internal loops alone. | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/assimilation/environment_evolution_layer.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/environment_evolution_layer_policy.json`; EMA-based robustness scoring, confidence shaping, Doctor queue hooks, and assimilation wiring for post-outcome feedback with validation in `client/memory/tools/tests/environment_evolution_layer.test.js` + assimilation integration tests. |
| V3-ASSIM-009  | done   | Test-Time Memory Evolution Primitive              | Agents actively refine memory after every interaction via search/synthesize/evolve loops. | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/assimilation/test_time_memory_evolution_primitive.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/test_time_memory_evolution_primitive_policy.json`; search/synthesize/evolve scoring over episodic memory with step-reduction estimation, shadow receipts, and assimilation integration (`test_time_memory_evolution` lane in `client/runtime/systems/assimilation/assimilation_controller.ts`) validated by `client/memory/tools/tests/test_time_memory_evolution_primitive.test.js` + assimilation integration tests. |
| V3-ASSIM-010  | done   | Group-Evolving Agents Primitive                   | Shared innovation reuse across cooperating agents outperforms solo evolution paths. | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/assimilation/group_evolving_agents_primitive.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/group_evolving_agents_primitive_policy.json`; peer trust + innovation reuse ledger for multi-agent experience sharing, shadow-first receipts, and assimilation integration (`group_evolving_agents` lane) validated by `client/memory/tools/tests/group_evolving_agents_primitive.test.js` + assimilation integration tests. |
| V3-ASSIM-011  | done   | Generative Meta-Model Primitive                   | Model-steering signal that preserves fluency/stability while allowing capability adaptation. | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/assimilation/generative_meta_model_primitive.ts` + policy `client/runtime/config/generative_meta_model_primitive_policy.json`; activation-manifold proxy (EMA centroid + bounded steering), fluency/stability safeguards, and shadow receipts wired into assimilation simulation inputs, validated by `client/memory/tools/tests/generative_meta_model_primitive.test.js` + assimilation integration tests. |
