## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1cMaJfBYCTUvCmAbpe3npLqFABbK9zHHnPEoaQrZ5Xbs`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the 8-feature sovereign economy/identity expansion into canonical backlog lanes while preserving strict data-scope boundaries:
- user-specific data goes in `client/memory/` and `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent/shared runtime logic goes in `client/runtime/systems/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- no permanent non-user policy/runtime state is stored as authoritative data in `client/memory/` or `client/cognition/adaptive/`

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Soul-bound immutable user contracts with guarded amendments | `V3-RACE-129` | done | Add a canonical soul-contract primitive with encrypted user contract storage + guard enforcement + dual-signature amendment flow. |
| Native token + mandatory base tithe + optional global objective fund | `V3-RACE-130` | done | Keep existing compute-tithe/blockchain bridges and add governed token/fund layer with user-level allocation controls. |
| Seed instances with inherited directives/memory and parent tithe routing | `V3-RACE-131` | done | Extend spawn contracts for seed lineage inheritance and optional parent-route tithe contracts. |
| Configurable civic duty cycle allocation for public-good objectives | `V3-RACE-132` | done | Add civic-duty planner with user-level allocation policy and bounded scheduling constraints. |
| Peer GPU lending between users/instances with contractual terms | `V3-RACE-133` | done | Extend GPU contribution flywheel into governed peer lending + settlement lanes. |
| Dynamic visual signature per instance, derived from identity/contribution history | `V3-RACE-134` | done | Add deterministic visual-signature generation and display contracts with bounded evolution rules. |
| Pinnacle-tech research/proposal engine (CRDT/ZK/FHE/DID/IPFS/WASM/etc.) | `V3-RACE-135` | done | Add structured breakthrough-scanning and governed integration proposal lanes (research/proposal only by default). |
| Full cross-organ integration + docs updates for economy/identity pack | `V3-RACE-136` | done | Add integration harness/docs contract tying soul, economy, spawn, guard, fractal, warden, and jigsaw lanes together with data-scope checks. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1TZ5u3gQYS1U9hqB8KxSBP5Jz1NkaZDlpdIBd8uaa8Ys`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the 7-technology "pinnacle integration layer" directive into staged, risk-governed backlog lanes with strict data-scope separation.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific intent/contracts/identity/profile data only in `client/memory/` and `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent runtime logic, schemas, cryptography, and policy only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- no permanent non-user source-of-truth is authored under `client/memory/` or `client/cognition/adaptive/`

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Monolithic `V3-RACE-046` "Pinnacle Tech Integration Layer" | decomposed into `V3-RACE-137..144` | normalized | Implement as staged queue to preserve deterministic gating, observability, and rollback safety. |
| CRDT + local-first architecture for soul/client/memory/contract state | `V3-RACE-137` | done | Add CRDT state-plane contracts with offline-first sync and JetStream reconciliation hooks. |
| WebAssembly component model for skill/habit modules | `V3-RACE-138` | done | Add capability-scoped WASM component runtime and signature-enforced module loading path. |
| Intent-based architecture (intent -> governed execution) | `V3-RACE-139` | done | Add explicit intent declaration plane with audited translation to executable workflows. |
| DID + Verifiable Credentials tied to soul vector | `V3-RACE-140` | done | Add DID/VC issuance + verification layer using soul-bound authority roots and revocation contracts. |
| IPFS/Filecoin-style content addressing for archival continuity | `V3-RACE-141` | done | Add content-addressed archival/pin contracts with encrypted payload and deterministic retrieval proofs. |
| Zero-Knowledge Proof lane for contract-compliance attestations | `V3-RACE-142` | done | Add ZK proof generation/verification contracts for selective compliance claims without raw disclosure. |
| Fully Homomorphic Encryption lane for encrypted-state computation | `V3-RACE-143` | done | Add FHE pilot lane for bounded high-value operations with strict performance/safety gates. |
| Full integration/docs + cross-organ contract enforcement | `V3-RACE-144` | done | Add integrated pinnacle harness/docs and data-scope regression checks across new lanes. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V3-RACE-137 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | CRDT Local-First State Plane (Soul/Memory/Contract Domains) | Current state lanes are resilient but not yet modeled as canonical CRDT local-first domains with deterministic conflict semantics across offline windows. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/pinnacle/crdt_state_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/crdt_state_plane_policy.json` with per-domain CRDT schemas and reconciliation rules; user-specific CRDT documents/anchors persist under `client/memory/crdt/` and adaptive merge heuristics under `client/cognition/adaptive/crdt/`; permanent merge logic/schemas stay in `client/runtime/systems/pinnacle/` + `client/runtime/config/`; sync emits JetStream reconciliation receipts with replay parity checks. | V3-RACE-023, V3-RACE-129, BL-024 |
| V3-RACE-138 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | WASM Component Runtime for Skills/Habits | Language-agnostic secure module execution requires capability-scoped sandboxing and signed components, not only script-level contracts. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/wasm/component_runtime.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/wasm_component_runtime_policy.json` with signature verification, capability manifests, and deny-by-default host bindings; user-specific module preferences/overrides live in `client/memory/wasm/` and adaptive module ranking/cooldowns in `client/cognition/adaptive/wasm/`; permanent runtime/verification logic remains in `client/runtime/systems/wasm/` + `client/runtime/config/`. | V3-RACE-117, V3-RACE-031, BL-014 |
| V3-RACE-139 | extension | V3 | done | Intent Declaration + Translation Plane | Intent exists implicitly in objectives/contracts but lacks a first-class machine-readable declaration-to-execution contract with auditable translation evidence. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/intent/intent_translation_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/intent_translation_policy.json`; user intents/preferences persist under `client/memory/intent/` and adaptive ranking/disambiguation state under `client/cognition/adaptive/intent/`; permanent parsers, planning rules, and safety translators remain in `client/runtime/systems/intent/` + `client/runtime/config/`; every translation emits explainable intent->plan receipts and risk-tier gates. | V3-RACE-129, V3-ACT-002, V3-RACE-135 |
| V3-RACE-140 | extension | V3 | done | DID + Verifiable Credential Soul Binding Layer | Soul authority exists, but standards-aligned DID/VC issuance and verification are needed for interoperable sovereign identity proofs. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/identity/did_vc_binding.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/did_vc_binding_policy.json`; user DID docs/client/credentials are stored in `client/memory/identity/did/` and adaptive trust/routing preferences in `client/cognition/adaptive/identity/did/`; permanent issuance/verification schemas and revocation logic stay in `client/runtime/systems/identity/` + `client/runtime/config/`; credentials for badges/contracts/lineage are verifiable and auditable without exposing non-required private fields. | V2-058, V3-BLK-001, V3-RACE-129 |
| V3-RACE-141 | extension | V3 | done | Content-Addressed Archival Plane (IPFS-Compatible) | Long-term continuity exists, but content-addressed retrieval proofs and pinning contracts are needed for immutable, portable archival semantics. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/archival/content_addressed_archive.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/content_addressed_archive_policy.json` with CID mapping, pin metadata, retrieval verification, and encrypted payload envelopes; user-specific archival payload/index references live under with adaptive pin/cache heuristics under `client/cognition/adaptive/archival/`; permanent addressing/protocol logic remains in `client/runtime/systems/archival/` + `client/runtime/config/`. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/archival/content_addressed_archive.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/archival`, `config`. | V3-RACE-037, V3-BLK-001, V3-RACE-109 |
| V3-RACE-142 | hardening | V3 | done | Zero-Knowledge Compliance Proof Layer | Compliance proofs currently rely on direct evidence disclosure, which increases privacy exposure for high-sensitivity economic/contract lanes. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/crypto/zk_compliance_proofs.{ts,js}` (Rust-backed where practical) + policy `client/runtime/config/zk_compliance_policy.json`; user-specific witness material and proof requests persist under `client/memory/crypto/zk/` and adaptive proof-selection heuristics under `client/cognition/adaptive/crypto/zk/`; permanent circuits, verifiers, and policy checks stay in `client/runtime/systems/crypto/` + `client/runtime/config/`; proofs verify contract-compliance claims with bounded disclosure and deterministic verification receipts. | V3-RACE-129, V3-RACE-130, V3-RACE-137 |
| V3-RACE-143 | extension | V3 | done | FHE Encrypted Compute Pilot Lane | Encrypted-state compute is a strategic sovereignty advantage but requires bounded rollout due to performance and operational complexity constraints. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/crypto/fhe_encrypted_compute_pilot.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/fhe_encrypted_compute_policy.json` with allowlisted operations, latency/cost budget gates, fallback contracts, and deterministic failure handling; user-specific encrypted operation payloads remain under `client/memory/crypto/fhe/` and adaptive operator selection under `client/cognition/adaptive/crypto/fhe/`; permanent evaluators/keys-policy/runtime code remain in `client/runtime/systems/crypto/` + `client/runtime/config/`. | V3-RACE-023, V3-RACE-142, V3-BUD-001 |
| V3-RACE-144 | hardening | V3 | done | Pinnacle Integration Contract + Data-Scope Guard | Independent pinnacle lanes can create boundary drift unless cross-organ contracts and storage-scope invariants are continuously enforced. | Implement integrated harness/docs (`docs/client/PINNACLE_TECH.md`, updates to `docs/client/SECURITY.md` and `docs/client/MIND_SOVEREIGNTY.md`) and `client/runtime/systems/ops/pinnacle_integration_contract_check.{ts,js}` to enforce cross-lane compatibility, risk-tier gating, JetStream publication, and data-scope boundaries (`client/memory/adaptive` user-only, `client/runtime/config/docs` permanent-only) across CRDT/WASM/Intent/DID/archival/ZK/FHE lanes. | V3-RACE-137, V3-RACE-138, V3-RACE-139, V3-RACE-140, V3-RACE-141, V3-RACE-142, V3-RACE-143, V3-RACE-136 |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1vedJMRKcVYn6MUt1jvgNvEmU7y-BuQRgOXM12kQdM6Y`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the "Digital Pinnacle Expansion Pack" (actor model, federation, edge, spatial, multimodel DB, WebGPU) into executable staged backlog lanes that complement the existing pinnacle queue.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific identity, collaboration, routing, and preference data lives only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent protocol adapters, runtimes, schemas, and policy contracts live only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- public/federated transport metadata is never treated as authoritative user-memory unless explicitly written into user lanes

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Monolithic `V3-RACE-048 Digital Pinnacle Expansion Pack` | decomposed into `V3-RACE-145..152` | normalized | Execute in staged lanes to preserve deterministic risk gating, rollback, and observability. |
| Actor model runtime for swarm/cell/skill coordination | `V3-RACE-145` | done| Add actor runtime substrate with isolated state + message-passing contracts. |
| Solid protocol data-pod interoperability | `V3-RACE-146` | done| Add governed Solid pod bridge for portable user-owned data exchange. |
| ActivityPub + Matrix federation | `V3-RACE-147` | done| Add federated comms gateway with policy-scoped bridging and audit receipts. |
| Edge/fog execution offload | `V3-RACE-148` | done| Add edge-first scheduler and offload contracts for heavy workloads. |
| Advanced real-time collaboration framework (beyond base CRDT lane) | `V3-RACE-149` | done| Add collaboration session/presence and low-latency sync contracts atop CRDT plane. |
| Spatial computing abstractions | `V3-RACE-150` | done| Add spatial interaction abstraction layer for 3D/AR/VR-ready runtime surfaces. |
| AI-native multimodel database plane | `V3-RACE-151` | done| Add unified vector/graph/document/relational data-plane contract with policy-gated migration path. |
| WebGPU + Rust client compute | `V3-RACE-152` | done| Add client-side compute runtime for accelerated local workloads and visual compute lanes. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V3-RACE-145 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | Actor Runtime Substrate (Isolated Swarm Message-Passing) | Existing orchestration is resilient but not yet unified under one actor-model contract for isolated state and deterministic mailbox semantics. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/runtime/actor_runtime_substrate.{ts,js}` (Rust-backed where appropriate) + policy `client/runtime/config/actor_runtime_policy.json`; user-specific actor preferences/limits persist under `client/memory/runtime/actors/` with adaptive scheduling heuristics under `client/cognition/adaptive/runtime/actors/`; permanent actor runtime/mailbox/supervisor logic remains in `client/runtime/systems/runtime/` + `client/runtime/config/` with JetStream message-flow receipts. | V3-RACE-021, V3-RACE-108, V3-RACE-064 |
| V3-RACE-146 | extension | V3 | done | Solid Pod Interop Bridge (User-Owned Data Pods) | Sovereign local storage exists, but standards-based pod interoperability is needed for portable user-controlled data exchange across ecosystems. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/federation/solid_pod_bridge.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/solid_pod_bridge_policy.json`; user-specific pod endpoints/tokens/mappings stay under `client/memory/federation/solid/` and adaptive sync-health heuristics under `client/cognition/adaptive/federation/solid/`; permanent protocol adapters, schema mappers, and trust policy remain in `client/runtime/systems/federation/` + `client/runtime/config/`. | V3-RACE-129, V3-RACE-140, V3-RACE-120 |
| V3-RACE-147 | extension | V3 | done | ActivityPub + Matrix Federation Gateway | Decentralized inter-instance communication requires canonical protocol gateways with bounded trust and policy-aware routing. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/federation/activitypub_matrix_gateway.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/activitypub_matrix_gateway_policy.json`; user-specific federation peers/allowlists/preferences remain under `client/memory/federation/channels/` and adaptive reliability/rate heuristics under `client/cognition/adaptive/federation/channels/`; permanent federation transport/bridge logic remains in `client/runtime/systems/federation/` + `client/runtime/config/` with egress/audit receipts. | V3-RACE-016, V3-RACE-031, V3-RACE-146 |
| V3-RACE-148 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | Edge/Fog Offload Orchestrator (Edge-First Compute Placement) | Heavy workloads need explicit placement contracts (local/edge/borrowed) to reduce latency and increase resilience under variable capacity. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/hardware/edge_fog_offload_orchestrator.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/edge_fog_offload_policy.json`; user-specific offload preferences/constraints persist under `client/memory/compute/offload/` and adaptive placement telemetry under `client/cognition/adaptive/compute/offload/`; permanent placement schedulers and admission policy stay in `client/runtime/systems/hardware/` + `client/runtime/config/` with deterministic fallback receipts. | V3-RACE-021, V3-RACE-133, V3-RACE-108 |
| V3-RACE-149 | extension | V3 | done | Real-Time Collaboration Presence Engine | Base CRDT replication does not fully cover multi-user presence/session semantics and high-frequency collaborative editing ergonomics. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/collaboration/realtime_presence_engine.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/realtime_presence_policy.json`; user-specific room/session membership and collaboration preferences remain in `client/memory/collaboration/` with adaptive collaboration QoS tuning under `client/cognition/adaptive/collaboration/`; permanent session orchestration, conflict semantics, and transport contracts stay in `client/runtime/systems/collaboration/` + `client/runtime/config/`. | V3-RACE-137, V3-RACE-147, V3-RACE-145 |
| V3-RACE-150 | extension | V3 | done | Spatial Computing Abstraction Layer | Future-facing interfaces need a standard spatial primitive layer before concrete AR/VR client implementations can be safely composed. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/spatial/spatial_abstraction_layer.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/spatial_abstraction_policy.json`; user-specific spatial preferences/profiles stay in `client/memory/spatial/` with adaptive layout/interaction tuning under `client/cognition/adaptive/spatial/`; permanent spatial scene/action contracts remain in `client/runtime/systems/spatial/` + `client/runtime/config/`, integrated with visual signature lanes. | V3-RACE-134, V3-RACE-149 |
| V3-RACE-151 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | AI-Native Multimodel Data Plane | Current storage is effective but fragmented; a unified multimodel plane is needed for consistent vector/graph/document/relational semantics under one governed contract. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/data/ai_multimodel_data_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/ai_multimodel_data_plane_policy.json`; user-specific query/view preferences persist under `client/memory/data/preferences/` with adaptive indexing/routing heuristics under `client/cognition/adaptive/data/`; permanent schema/runtime/migration contracts remain in `client/runtime/systems/data/` + `client/runtime/config/`; rollout includes reversible migration + parity receipts against existing client/memory/runtime lanes. | V3-RACE-023, V3-RACE-137, V3-RACE-109 |
| V3-RACE-152 | extension | V3 | done | WebGPU + Rust Client Compute Plane | High-cost local visual/fractal workloads benefit from GPU acceleration and client-side Rust compute while preserving sovereign local execution guarantees. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/client/webgpu_rust_compute_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/webgpu_rust_compute_policy.json`; user-specific client compute profiles/preferences persist under `client/memory/client_compute/` with adaptive performance heuristics under `client/cognition/adaptive/client_compute/`; permanent compute kernels/runtime bindings remain in `client/runtime/systems/client/` + `client/runtime/config/`, with deterministic fallback to CPU paths and receipted performance deltas. | V3-RACE-138, V3-RACE-148, V3-RACE-150 |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1-ndEYjPrXYS2pFpIZDtq8Pc02UuhUk24XbTHvb_wPQA`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the "Collective Intelligence Engine" directive (crowd GPU training + sovereign LLM growth flywheel) into staged backlog lanes that integrate with existing nursery/training/economy primitives.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific participation preferences, contribution agreements, personalization adapters, and access-tier settings live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent training orchestration logic, evaluation pipelines, rollout gates, and economy policies live only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- model artifacts/checkpoints are never stored as authoritative user-memory; they are governed runtime artifacts with encrypted archival contracts

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Monolithic `V3-RACE-049 Collective Intelligence Engine` | decomposed into `V3-RACE-153..160` | normalized | Implement as staged queue to preserve deterministic gating, training safety, and rollout rollback semantics. |
| Crowd GPU distributed training over tithe + peer lending pool | `V3-RACE-153` | done| Add distributed training orchestrator integrated with compute donation/lending lanes and hardware envelopes. |
| Soul-contract-backed training contributions + contributor rewards | `V3-RACE-154` | done| Add training contribution agreements tied to soul contracts and reward settlement contracts. |
| Fractal-managed curriculum/data curation/eval loop | `V3-RACE-155` | done| Add fractal oversight lane for training objective mutation, data quality, and checkpoint gates. |
| Safe model rollout with tier-3 governance + fallback versions | `V3-RACE-156` | done| Add governed release lane with canary/evidence gates, old-model fallback, and rollback contracts. |
| Encrypted distributed model archival (IPFS/Arweave style) | `V3-RACE-157` | done| Extend archival plane with model-weight artifact contracts and retrieval verification gates. |
| Contributor badges/rewards + optional access benefits | `V3-RACE-158` | done| Add contributor incentive layer integrating badges, token rewards, and bounded service benefits. |
| Free-base + premium/priority access model for sustainability | `V3-RACE-159` | done| Add access-tier governance contract balancing open access with contributor incentive/economics. |
| Full integration docs + data-scope checks for training economy loop | `V3-RACE-160` | open | Add integration harness and storage-boundary checks across training/economy/identity lanes. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V3-RACE-153 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | Collective Training Orchestrator (Crowd GPU Distributed Training Plane) | Existing specialist training lanes are strong but not yet unified into a crowd-GPU orchestrator spanning tithe and peer-lending compute pools. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/training/collective_training_orchestrator.{ts,js}` (Rust worker optional) + policy `client/runtime/config/collective_training_orchestrator_policy.json`; user-specific contribution preferences and participation caps persist under `client/memory/training/contributions/` with adaptive scheduling/allocator heuristics under `client/cognition/adaptive/training/contributions/`; permanent orchestration, cluster scheduling, and job governance remain in `client/runtime/systems/training/` + `client/runtime/config/` with deterministic run receipts. | V3-RACE-022, V3-RACE-133, V2-069, V3-ASSIM-019 |
| V3-RACE-154 | extension | V3 | done | Soul-Contract Training Contribution & Settlement Layer | Compute contributors need explicit governed agreements/reward settlement to keep incentives aligned and auditable at scale. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/training/training_contribution_contracts.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/training_contribution_contract_policy.json`; user agreements/signatures/terms remain in `client/memory/training/contracts/` with adaptive contributor-scoring under `client/cognition/adaptive/training/contracts/`; permanent settlement/reward logic remains in `client/runtime/systems/training/` + `client/runtime/systems/economy/` + `client/runtime/config/`; all settlements publish event-stream receipts and are verifiable against contribution telemetry. | V3-RACE-129, V3-RACE-130, V3-RACE-153 |
| V3-RACE-155 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | Fractal Curriculum + Evaluation Oversight for Collective Training | Distributed training quality degrades without a governed loop for data curation, curriculum mutation, and checkpoint evidence gates. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/training/fractal_training_oversight.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/fractal_training_oversight_policy.json`; user-specific model preference signals remain in `client/memory/training/preferences/` with adaptive curriculum weighting in `client/cognition/adaptive/training/curriculum/`; permanent curation/eval/gating logic remains in `client/runtime/systems/training/` + `client/runtime/systems/fractal/` + `client/runtime/config/`; checkpoint promotions require bounded evidence packs and non-regression receipts. | V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-153, RM-104 |
| V3-RACE-156 | hardening | V3 | done | Sovereign Model Rollout Ladder (Tier-3 Governed + Fallback/Rollback) | Community-trained model updates can introduce regressions unless rollout includes strict governance, canary proof, and guaranteed fallback availability. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/training/sovereign_model_rollout_ladder.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/sovereign_model_rollout_policy.json`; user-specific rollout pinning/opt-in preferences remain under `client/memory/training/rollout_preferences/` with adaptive risk scoring in `client/cognition/adaptive/training/rollout/`; permanent release/canary/rollback logic remains in `client/runtime/systems/training/` + `client/runtime/config/`; tier-3+ promotions require explicit approval receipts and automatic fallback contracts. | V3-RACE-155, V3-RACE-038, V2-069 |
| V3-RACE-157 | extension | V3 | done | Encrypted Model Artifact Archive (Content-Addressed Weights) | Model lineage and recovery need encrypted content-addressed artifact contracts beyond generic archival lanes. | Extend archival contracts via `client/runtime/systems/training/model_artifact_archive.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/model_artifact_archive_policy.json`; user-specific access permissions/entitlements remain under `client/memory/training/artifact_access/` with adaptive retrieval/caching hints in `client/cognition/adaptive/training/artifacts/`; permanent artifact packaging/encryption/addressing logic stays in `client/runtime/systems/training/` + `client/runtime/systems/archival/` + `client/runtime/config/`; every promoted model has verifiable encrypted archival receipts. | V3-RACE-141, V3-RACE-156, V3-RACE-037 |
| V3-RACE-158 | extension | V3 | done | Contributor Incentive Engine (Badges, Rewards, Recognition) | Sustainable collective training requires deterministic contributor recognition and reward contracts linked to real compute impact. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/economy/training_contributor_incentive_engine.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/training_contributor_incentive_policy.json`; user-specific badge history and reward preferences remain in `client/memory/economy/training_rewards/` with adaptive contribution quality scoring in `client/cognition/adaptive/economy/training_rewards/`; permanent reward formulas/issuance logic remain in `client/runtime/systems/economy/` + `client/runtime/config/`; awards integrate with honor codex and emit auditable receipts. | V3-RACE-154, V3-RACE-030, V3-RACE-130 |
| V3-RACE-159 | hardening | V3 | done | Access-Tier Governance for Sovereign Model (Free Base + Priority Tiers) | Universal base access improves adoption, but governance needs an explicit tiering contract to preserve contributor incentives and operational sustainability. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/economy/model_access_tier_governance.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/model_access_tier_policy.json`; user-specific tier preferences/eligibility state remain under `client/memory/economy/model_access/` with adaptive queue/QoS tuning in `client/cognition/adaptive/economy/model_access/`; permanent access policy, fairness rules, and budget controls remain in `client/runtime/systems/economy/` + `client/runtime/config/`; base-free and priority-tier semantics are enforced with receipts and fail-safe fallback to base access. | V3-RACE-130, V3-RACE-158, V3-BUD-001 |
| V3-RACE-160 | hardening | V3 | done | Collective Intelligence Integration Contract + Data-Scope Guard | Training, economy, identity, and archival lanes can drift without integrated contract checks and explicit storage-boundary enforcement. | Implement integrated checks/docs via `client/runtime/systems/ops/collective_intelligence_contract_check.{ts,js}`, update `docs/client/ECONOMY.md` and new `docs/client/INTELLIGENCE.md`; enforce data boundaries (`client/memory/adaptive` user-only, `client/runtime/config/docs` permanent-only) and cross-lane invariants across orchestrator, contracts, fractal oversight, rollout, archival, incentives, and access tiers with deterministic regression receipts. | V3-RACE-153, V3-RACE-154, V3-RACE-155, V3-RACE-156, V3-RACE-157, V3-RACE-158, V3-RACE-159 |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1-ndEYjPrXYS2pFpIZDtq8Pc02UuhUk24XbTHvb_wPQA`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the X-gap scan recommendations (memory evolution, interoperability standards, meta-cognitive executive, and ops hardening) into staged backlog lanes.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific journals/observations/preferences/collaboration state only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent protocol adapters, schedulers, verification contracts, and audit/runtime logic only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- federation/tool metadata is treated as runtime operational state, not user memory, unless explicitly promoted by policy

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Git-backed versioned memory filesystem (MemFS-style) | `V3-RACE-161` | done| Add memory filesystem with git provenance, branch/worktree support, and governed reflection commits. |
| Idle/sleep reflection compute loop | `V3-RACE-162` | done| Add background reflection scheduler with isolated execution lane and budget/quiet-hour controls. |
| Agentic self-organizing memory operations (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP policy) | `V3-RACE-163` | done| Add policy-scored memory operation controller tied to outcome signals and rollback safety. |
| Observational compression + progressive disclosure retrieval | `V3-RACE-164` | done| Add observation compaction and metadata-first retrieval contracts with bounded context loading. |
| MCP interoperability layer + discoverable skill ingestion | `V3-RACE-165` | open | Add governed MCP client/server bridge and registry-driven skill discovery contracts. |
| A2A delegation/collaboration protocol lane | `V3-RACE-166` | done| Add standards-aligned agent-to-agent delegation and handoff contracts with policy gates. |
| System-3 executive layer (growth journal, intrinsic goals, always-on metacognition) | `V3-RACE-167` | done| Add persistent executive layer over reflex/strategy with narrative continuity + intrinsic loop controls. |
| Immutable audit chain + background hands + marketplace CLI polish | `V3-RACE-168` | open | Add hash-chained audit ledger, idle execution hands scheduler, and `protheusctl skills discover` operator surface. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V3-RACE-161 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | Git-Backed Memory Filesystem (MemFS Contract) | Memory provenance and branchable replay need first-class versioned filesystem semantics beyond current artifact snapshots. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/memory/memfs_layer.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/memfs_layer_policy.json`; user-specific memory documents/history remain under `client/memory/fs/` and adaptive retrieval ranking/merge hints under `client/cognition/adaptive/client/memory/fs/`; permanent memfs orchestration/git-worktree contracts remain in `client/runtime/systems/memory/` + `client/runtime/config/`; commits are policy-annotated and replay-verifiable. | V3-RACE-023, BL-024, V3-RACE-137 |
| V3-RACE-162 | extension | V3 | done | Sleep Reflection Compute Scheduler (Idle Consolidation Loop) | Memory and strategy quality compounds faster with bounded asynchronous reflection during idle windows. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/memory/sleep_reflection_scheduler.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/sleep_reflection_scheduler_policy.json`; user-specific reflection outputs stay in `client/memory/reflections/` and adaptive scheduling heuristics in `client/cognition/adaptive/reflections/`; permanent scheduler/executor logic stays in `client/runtime/systems/memory/` + `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/` + `client/runtime/config/`; runs honor budget/quiet-hour/risk-tier gates and emit receipts. | V3-RACE-161, V3-LOOP-001, V3-BUD-001 |
| V3-RACE-163 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | Agentic Memory Operation Controller (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP) | Self-evolving memory requires explicit, auditable action selection rather than implicit write behavior. | Implemented `client/runtime/systems/memory/agentic_memory_operation_controller.ts` on restored shared runtime `client/runtime/lib/upgrade_lane_runtime.ts` with configure/execute/status command receipts and guarded operation payloads. Verified by `client/memory/tools/tests/agentic_memory_operation_controller.test.js`. | V3-ASSIM-004, V3-RACE-161, V3-RACE-162 |
| V3-RACE-164 | hardening | V3 | done | Observational Compression + Progressive Disclosure Retrieval | Raw history growth increases token burn and retrieval latency unless observation compaction and staged loading are enforced. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/memory/observational_compression_layer.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/observational_compression_policy.json`; user-specific observations/summaries are persisted under `client/memory/observations/` with adaptive compression thresholds under `client/cognition/adaptive/observations/`; permanent compaction/index/search logic remains in `client/runtime/systems/memory/` + `client/runtime/config/` and enforces metadata-first retrieval before full-node expansion. | V3-RACE-161, V3-RACE-163, V3-RACE-065 |
| V3-RACE-165 | extension | V3 | done | MCP Interoperability + Skill Discovery Gateway | Ecosystem leverage requires standards-based tool/context interoperability without weakening existing signed gate posture. | Implement `client/cognition/skills/mcp/mcp_gateway.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/mcp_gateway_policy.json` and CLI surface `protheusctl skills discover --mcp`; user-specific installed-skill preferences remain in `client/memory/client/cognition/skills/mcp/` with adaptive skill-quality scoring in `client/cognition/adaptive/client/cognition/skills/mcp/`; permanent protocol adapters and verification logic remain in `client/cognition/skills/mcp/` + `client/runtime/systems/security/` + `client/runtime/config/`. | V3-RACE-016, V3-RACE-031, V3-RACE-146 |
| V3-RACE-166 | extension | V3 | done | A2A Delegation Plane (Agent-to-Agent Protocol Contracts) | Cross-agent collaboration/delegation requires explicit contractized handoff semantics to avoid ad hoc bridge drift. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/a2a/a2a_delegation_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/a2a_delegation_policy.json`; user-specific trusted-peer preferences remain in `client/memory/federation/a2a/` with adaptive peer reliability scoring in `client/cognition/adaptive/federation/a2a/`; permanent delegation cards/handoff/auth contracts remain in `client/runtime/systems/a2a/` + `client/runtime/config/` with signed handoff receipts. | V3-RACE-147, V3-RACE-165, V3-RACE-031 |
| V3-RACE-167 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | System-3 Executive Layer (Growth Journal + Intrinsic Goal Loop) | Reflex/strategy loops are strong, but persistent narrative self-modeling and intrinsic-goal refinement need a dedicated executive layer. | Implement `client/cognition/adaptive/executive/system3_executive_layer.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/system3_executive_policy.json`; user-specific growth-journal and executive preferences persist in `client/memory/executive/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/executive/`; permanent executive orchestration/governance logic remains in `client/cognition/adaptive/executive/` + `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/` + `client/runtime/config/`; layer runs continuously with bounded cadence and constitution-aligned veto gates. | V3-RACE-129, V3-PRO-001, V3-LOOP-001 |
| V3-RACE-168 | hardening | V3 | done | Ops Hardening Pack (Audit Chain + Background Hands + Skills UX) | Production-grade unattended operation needs immutable audit continuity, bounded always-on work execution, and low-friction operator tooling. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/audit/hash_chain_ledger.{ts,js}`, `client/runtime/systems/spine/background_hands_scheduler.{ts,js}`, and CLI integration in `client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl_skills_discover.{ts,js}` with policies; user-specific operator preferences live in `client/memory/ops/preferences/` and adaptive cadence/queue tuning under `client/cognition/adaptive/ops/`; permanent audit/scheduler/CLI contracts remain in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` with non-bypass integrity checks and rollback-safe controls. | V3-RACE-041, V3-RACE-126, V3-RACE-165 |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1QrTZFUX6fbU0RVo8S_fIg6898igD8XxXhohRjNjRROQ`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the "lean/robust/competitive supremacy" directive into focused deltas on top of already-queued client/memory/interoperability/meta-cognition work.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific journals/preferences/crew-profile data lives only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent orchestration/runtime/policy/benchmark logic lives only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- benchmark inputs and external-framework adapters are operational artifacts, not user-memory by default

|---|---|---|---|
| MemFS, sleep reflection, self-organizing memory, progressive disclosure | `V3-RACE-161..164` | already queued | Reuse current queue; no duplicate lanes added. |
| MCP + A2A + System-3 + hash audit/background hands/skills UX | `V3-RACE-165..168` | already queued | Reuse current queue; no duplicate lanes added. |
| Extract tiny `protheus-core` package and hard-separate optional layers | `V3-RACE-169` | open | Add modular core/package extraction lane with strict dependency surface and plugin boundaries. |
| Enforce spine kernel minimalism + policy relocation to client/cognition/adaptive/habits | `V3-RACE-170` | open | Add kernel budget/lint gate to keep spine orchestration-only and move policy logic out of kernel. |
| Add cyclic strategy graph runner (LangGraph-style parity, Protheus governance) | `V3-RACE-171` | done| Add bounded cyclic state-graph orchestration lane for strategy transitions and retries. |
| Add hierarchical persistent memory views (core/editable/summary + model-swap summarization) | `V3-RACE-172` | done| Add tiered memory-view derivation and swap-safe summarization contracts. |
| Add role-based crew spawning with shared namespace under habit governance | `V3-RACE-173` | done| Add multi-agent role spawn templates with bounded shared memory and clearance contracts. |
| Extend OTEL + eval UX + cross-framework benchmark script pack | `V3-RACE-174` | done | Add operator UX and benchmark parity pack vs Letta/Mastra/LangGraph/OpenFang using deterministic scenarios. |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1u2Oh8FVHI9joij7p4FXI3cUrUqY6at8AVcHdfeLHW84`, 2026-03-02)

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific RSI approvals, operator preferences, and per-instance adaptation state live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent runtime logic, gate orchestration, protocol adapters, and policy contracts live only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- non-user operational receipts remain in `state/` and are never treated as authoritative user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Keep existing March 2 contract lanes, System 3, MemFS, MCP gateway, and V3-RACE hardening intact | existing `V3-RACE-161..174`, `V3-RACE-CONF-007` | already queued | Reuse existing queue and enforce no-replacement constraint via governance checks. |
| `packages/protheus-core` extraction baseline | `V3-RACE-169` | done | Already extracted; treat as the base for stricter runtime envelope work. |

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1zxYaW0wNMbFnnpx_Ps0WCVgMf-uGraobSctE7cCGx80`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the Rust-hybrid escalation directive into canonical backlog lanes while preserving current TS operator velocity and non-breaking control-plane interfaces.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific runtime preferences and migration toggles live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent runtime kernels, bindings, sandbox engines, verification contracts, and packaging logic live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- benchmark/attestation artifacts remain in `state/` and are never treated as authoritative user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Rust core runtime extraction for spine/router/spawn/security hot paths | `V3-RACE-001`, `V3-RACE-021`, `V3-RACE-034` | done | Reuse completed Rust microkernel/control-plane extraction lanes. |
| Keep `packages/protheus-core` as minimal nucleus and tighten runtime envelope (<5MB, <200ms) | `V3-RACE-169`, `V3-RACE-175` | done | Runtime envelope hardening now enforced with optional-flag matrix checks and receipts. |
| In-process Rust bridging (`napi`) for hot paths with deterministic fallback | `V3-RACE-028` + delta `V3-RACE-185` | done | Extended memory N-API groundwork into `protheus-core` binding plane parity checks with deterministic fallback contract receipts. |
| Optional Rust/WASM acceleration path for `execution_sandbox_envelope` | `V3-RACE-186` | done | Add optional Rust/WASM sandbox coprocessor lane integrated into existing sandbox policy. |
| Formal verification depth on critical live paths (venom gates, RSI self-mods, resurrection, provenance) | `V3-RACE-035` + delta `V3-RACE-187` | done | Formal depth-pack lane now verifies critical runtime proof surfaces and emits deterministic proof receipts. |
| Preserve TS orchestration UX and prevent breaking changes during Rust escalation | `V3-RACE-116` + delta `V3-RACE-188` | done | Hybrid interface stability contract now enforces command/output snapshots with rollback-safe drift handling. |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| V3-RACE-186 | extension | V3 | done | Execution Sandbox Rust/WASM Coprocessor Lane (Optional, Policy-Gated) | `execution_sandbox_envelope` is critical-path security; optional native/WASM acceleration can reduce overhead while preserving current safety behavior. | Implemented optional coprocessor verification lane in `client/runtime/systems/security/execution_sandbox_rust_wasm_coprocessor_lane.ts` with policy-driven sandbox/WASM probe checks, fail-closed behavior, and deterministic verify receipts. Verified by `client/memory/tools/tests/execution_sandbox_rust_wasm_coprocessor_lane.test.js`. | V3-RACE-024, V3-RACE-138, V3-RACE-185 |
| V3-RACE-187 | hardening | V3 | done | Formal Verification Depth Pack for Critical Runtime Paths | Existing sovereignty verification is broad but this intake calls for explicit proofs on the highest-risk lanes where regression cost is catastrophic. | Expand formal specs/proof gates to cover venom containment transitions, RSI patch gating flows, resurrection rollback integrity, and supply-chain provenance checks; block merge on proof regressions and emit machine-verifiable proof receipts. | V3-RACE-035, V3-RACE-180, V3-RACE-181, V3-RACE-043 |
| V3-RACE-188 | hardening | V3 | done | Hybrid Interface Stability Contract (Rust Core, TS Orchestration, Zero-Break Cutover) | Hybrid migration can silently break operator surfaces unless interface compatibility is continuously enforced. | Add compatibility contract for CLI/control-plane/public APIs requiring semver-safe adapters, regression snapshots for command/output contracts, staged canary cutover gates, and automatic rollback triggers on API drift. | V3-RACE-116, V3-RACE-185, V3-RACE-175 |

## Internal Optimization Intake (Autonomy Execution Throughput Deadlock, 2026-03-02)

Objective: break the score-only deadlock by fixing verification semantics and execution floor behavior while preserving existing safety/constitution/contract-lane controls.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific execution preferences and tolerance overrides live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent verification semantics, selector logic, budget partitioning, and routing health controls live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- run receipts and gate telemetry remain in `state/` and are never treated as user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from system check)

| Requirement (from system check) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Score-only previews fail on deferred-horizon criteria (`postconditions_ok`, outreach reply windows) creating false negatives | `V3-RACE-204` | done | Add preview-horizon semantics so deferred metrics are tracked without failing immediate preview verdicts. |
| Need at least one deterministic, low-risk shipped outcome/day to unlock readiness (`min_shipped`) | `V3-RACE-205` | done | Add execution-floor bootstrap lane that guarantees one bounded shippable candidate path when safe. |
| Selector repeatedly chooses manual/high-risk paths in score-only, causing `preview_executable=false` churn | `V3-RACE-206` | done | Penalize or skip manual-gated candidates in score-only unless no safe alternative exists. |
| Dream/autopause budget pressure starves execution floor and causes persistent defer loops | `V3-RACE-207` | done | Partition execution vs dream budget envelopes with explicit minimum execution reserve. |
| Model pool degraded by repeated probe timeouts, reducing healthy fallback capacity | `V3-RACE-208` | done | Add adaptive probe timeout + temporary suppression/rehabilitation rules for unstable models. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V3-RACE-204 | hardening | V3 | done | Preview-Horizon Success Criteria Contract (Deferred Metrics in Score-Only) | Score-only receipts currently fail when criteria are valid but time-windowed (e.g., postconditions/outreach replies), distorting pass-rate telemetry and readiness. | Update success-criteria verifier + receipt taxonomy so deferred-horizon checks are explicitly marked `deferred_pending_window` in score-only, counted separately from hard fails, with verification receipts and rollback-safe config toggle to restore strict legacy behavior if regressions appear. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js`. | V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-116 |
| V3-RACE-205 | hardening | V3 | done | Deterministic Execution-Floor Bootstrap Lane (1 Shippable Outcome Minimum) | Readiness blocks on `min_shipped` but current candidate flow can loop indefinitely in no-change/preview-only paths. | Add low-risk execution-floor lane that guarantees at least one policy-safe, objectively verifiable shippable action per day when eligible candidates exist; emit verification receipts for shipped outcome and include rollback path that reverts to previous selection policy on miss-rate regression. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js`. | V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-116 |
| V3-RACE-206 | hardening | V3 | done | Score-Only Manual-Gate Exclusion & Selector Penalty | Re-selecting manual/high-risk proposals in score-only produces predictable `preview_executable=false` outcomes and wastes attempts. | Implement selector gate that deprioritizes/blocks manual-gated candidates during score-only unless no safe alternative passes; emit decision receipts with reason codes and include rollback switch to restore prior selection ordering for comparison. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js`. | V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-116 |
| V3-RACE-207 | hardening | V3 | done | Budget Envelope Partitioning (Execution Floor vs Dream/Idle Burn) | A single burn/autopause pressure lane can starve execution and trap the system in deferred preview mode despite viable low-cost actions. | Split token-economics envelopes into protected execution-floor reserve and dream/idle budget pools, enforce minimum execution share before autopause defers, emit verification receipts on reserve usage, and add rollback-safe policy fallback to unified budget mode. | V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-022, V3-RACE-116 |
| V3-RACE-208 | hardening | V3 | done | Model Health Stabilizer (Adaptive Probe Timeouts + Temporary Suppression/Rehab) | Repeated probe timeouts on key models degrade routing diversity and increase fallback pressure, hurting throughput reliability. | Add adaptive probe timeout policy plus temporary suppression/rehabilitation for unstable models, prove improved healthy-model availability with verification receipts, and include rollback policy to revert timeout/suppression tuning if latency or quality regresses. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js`. | V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-116 |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1bw6PoJqkftJhLRLjPanv4fLzSrI-h_Sht9UpxDLh0Ck`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the Platform Oracle + HostProfile + Adaptation Channel architecture into canonical backlog lanes while reusing already-delivered mobile/distribution/formal foundations and preserving fail-closed governance.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- host probing outputs and adaptation activation receipts remain in `state/` and are never treated as user-memory
- permanent oracle/channel runtime code, policies, and CLI surfaces live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- user-specific host preferences/overrides (if any) remain in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/` and never become global defaults

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Expand the burn-limiter sensor into a boot-time Platform Oracle that emits a signed typed `HostProfile` | `V3-RACE-209` | done | Add oracle service with deterministic probe lifecycle and signed HostProfile receipts. |
| Add Adaptation Channel contract (Rust trait + WASM guest API) with fail-closed generic fallback | `V3-RACE-210` | done | Define stable channel ABI/registry and enforce generic fallback when no specific host match exists. |
| Ship 5 host-specific channels (Ubuntu, FreeBSD, NixOS, Raspberry Pi OS, Alpine) activated only on `HostProfile` match | `V3-RACE-211` | done | Implement channel pack with strict host predicates, lightweight modules, and non-match exclusion gates. |
| Promote the 18 cross-platform GENERAL requirements into universal core behavior | `V3-RACE-212` | done | Add a canonical general-requirement matrix and wire mandatory universal controls into oracle + generic channel paths. |
| Add `protheusctl host adapt` and auto activation on first run/upgrade | `V3-RACE-213` | done | Deliver operator CLI and deterministic auto-adapt workflow with receipt-backed activation history. |
| Add formal verification gate that blocks lanes incompatible with active `HostProfile` | `V3-RACE-214` | done | Extend formal lane checks with host-profile conformance proofs and fail-closed enforcement. |
| Keep formal/chaos baseline for critical runtime paths in required checks | `RM-201`, `RM-204`, `V3-RACE-187` | covered | Reuse existing formal+chaos lanes as dependencies; only add host-profile-specific proof scope in this intake. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V3-RACE-209 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | Platform Oracle HostProfile Service (Burn-Limiter Expansion) | Steward-agnostic adaptation needs one authoritative, typed host identity contract before any channel or lane can safely activate. | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_oracle_hostprofile.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/platform_oracle_hostprofile_policy.json` to run boot/promotion/periodic host probing with signed `HostProfile` receipts (OS/distro/variant/kernel/runtime/cloud/hardware/capabilities), fail-closed minimal fallback, and rollback to last-known-good profile; deterministic artifacts are written to `state/ops/platform_oracle_hostprofile/` and covered by `client/memory/tools/tests/platform_adaptation_stack.test.js`. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_oracle_hostprofile.ts`. | V3-RACE-034, V3-RACE-116, V3-RACE-188 |
| V3-RACE-210 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | Adaptation Channel ABI (Rust Trait + WASM Guest Contract + Generic Fallback) | Cross-system portability requires a governed extension interface rather than scattered per-platform conditionals in core runtime paths. | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_adaptation_channel_runtime.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/platform_adaptation_channel_runtime_policy.json` + `client/runtime/config/platform_adaptation_channels.json` to enforce a governed channel ABI/registry with signed attestation metadata, explicit host predicates, deterministic activation receipts, and fail-closed generic fallback when no specific channel matches. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_adaptation_channel_runtime.ts`. | V3-RACE-209, V3-RACE-176, V3-RACE-185 |
| V3-RACE-211 | extension | V3 | done | Five-System Adaptation Channel Pack (Ubuntu/FreeBSD/NixOS/RaspberryPiOS/Alpine) | Platform-specific capabilities must stay isolated to matched hosts to avoid drift, policy violations, and unnecessary runtime weight. | Delivered the five specific adaptation channels in `client/runtime/config/platform_adaptation_channels.json` (`ubuntu`, `freebsd`, `nixos`, `raspios`, `alpine`) plus `generic`, with strict HostProfile predicates, non-match exclusion, and fallback behavior validated by `platform_adaptation_channel_runtime` and regression-covered in `client/memory/tools/tests/platform_adaptation_stack.test.js`. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js`. | V3-RACE-210, V3-RACE-175, V3-RACE-189, V3-RACE-193 |
| V3-RACE-212 | extension | V3 | done | Universal Platform Abstraction Matrix (18 GENERAL Requirements) | The intake's cross-platform requirements should be encoded once as universal contracts to prevent per-channel duplication and inconsistent guarantees. | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_universal_abstraction_matrix.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/platform_universal_abstraction_matrix_policy.json` + `client/runtime/config/platform_universal_abstraction_matrix.json` encoding all 18 universal controls with owner, enforcement mode, and rollback toggle requirements; emits signed compliance receipts and enforces fallback revision presence for rollback-safe policy rollback. | V3-RACE-209, V3-RACE-210, V3-RACE-211, V3-RACE-120 |
| V3-RACE-213 | extension | V3 | done | Host Adaptation Operator Surface (`protheusctl host adapt`) + Auto-Activation | Operators need deterministic visibility/control over adaptation decisions at install/upgrade time without manual file edits. | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/host_adaptation_operator_surface.{ts,js}` and wired `protheusctl host detect Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/host_adaptation_operator_surface.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl.ts`. | adapt | status` in `client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl.ts`; includes receipted activation timeline, first-run auto-adapt path, dry-run support, fail-closed behavior via underlying lanes, and rollback command contract surfaced to operators. | V3-RACE-209, V3-RACE-210, V3-RACE-184 |
| V3-RACE-214 | hardening | V3 | done | HostProfile Conformance Formal Gate (Lane-Activation Proof Contract) | Without machine-checkable host compatibility proofs, new lanes can bypass adaptation policy and run with invalid assumptions. | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/host_profile_conformance_formal_gate.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/host_profile_conformance_formal_gate_policy.json` + `client/runtime/config/host_profile_lane_predicates.json` with per-lane predicate/proof-stub validation, active-lane host-match enforcement, chaos/adversarial check stubs, and signed proof receipts; proof stubs in `docs/client/proofs/platform/*` and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/platform_adaptation_stack.test.js`. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/host_profile_conformance_formal_gate.ts`, `docs/client/proofs/platform/alpine_lane_predicate.md`. | V3-RACE-209, V3-RACE-210, V3-RACE-211, V3-RACE-187, V3-RACE-017 |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1sgyT2m4g0kR0aYN134mGm5W2s4RLjwHEFSi4WIVec3I`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize Apple-specific stewardship requirements into canonical backlog lanes while preserving local-first sovereignty and reusing prior Microsoft/Amazon queue foundations where possible.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- Apple platform integrations (Core ML/MLX/Foundation Models/PCC/visionOS/iOS services) remain optional provider adapters behind fail-closed policy gates
- runtime/provider logic stays in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`; Apple account/device metadata is never treated as user-memory
- platform telemetry/compliance artifacts remain in `state/` with reproducible receipts and rollback-ready promotion controls

| V3-RACE-341 | hardening | V3 | done | Helix Enterprise Support/Commercial Readiness Lane (24x7 SLA + Indemnification) | Commercial enterprise commitments require auditable support readiness and indemnification policy evidence before external claims. | Add support/commercial readiness lane producing SLA/indemnification evidence artifacts, require verification receipts for policy and support-matrix completeness, and include rollback-safe publication hold when readiness checks fail. | V3-RACE-314, V3-RACE-340, V3-RACE-279 |
| V3-RACE-342 | extension | V3 | done | Secure RPM Repository + Delta Update Channel Lane | Bandwidth-efficient enterprise rollout requires signed RPM distribution with deterministic delta update and rollback semantics. | Add secure RPM repository/update lane with signed metadata and delta transport contracts, require verification receipts for update integrity and delta correctness, and include rollback-safe channel freeze/revert behavior on update validation failures. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_queue_executor.ts`. | V3-RACE-332, V3-RACE-228, V3-RACE-175 |
| V3-RACE-343 | hardening | V3 | done | Helix Promotion Hardening Gate (Immutability + SELinux/eBPF + Certified HW + Air-Gap Evidence) | Helix-targeted promotions require explicit immutable/security/hardware/offline evidence gates beyond baseline promotion checks. | Add Helix promotion gate enforcing immutable update semantics, SELinux/eBPF policy evidence, certified hardware validation, and air-gapped install receipts where applicable, require verification receipts per promotion, and include rollback-safe promotion hold/fallback on gate instability. | V3-RACE-333, V3-RACE-334, V3-RACE-336, V3-RACE-338, V3-RACE-214 |
| V3-RACE-344 | hardening | V3 | done | Neutral-Core Enterprise Hardening Backport Pack (Helix-Derived Controls) | High-value Helix controls should uplift the neutral platform so future stewards inherit enterprise-grade defaults automatically. | Promote selected Helix-derived controls (immutability contracts, SELinux-first policy patterns, air-gapped verification, long-LTS lifecycle policy primitives) into neutral core profiles with policy toggles, require verification receipts for non-regression across non-Helix profiles, and include rollback-safe opt-out path for incompatible environments. | V3-RACE-212, V3-RACE-343, V3-RACE-120 |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1yBWjrygvK0fVtgNoNvalKwS5j6lyd7wBivPHqvRElDQ`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the full multi-system Linux/Unix ecosystem requirement bundle into canonical backlog lanes while reusing existing HostProfile/adaptation foundations and promoting cross-cutting patterns into neutral core controls.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- distro-specific behaviors remain inside governed adaptation channels keyed by `HostProfile` matching
- package/distribution/provisioning/signing artifacts remain in `state/` with reproducible receipts
- permanent runtime and policy logic remains in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Generic Linux compatibility baseline (Ubuntu/Debian/Fedora/Rocky/Alma/openSUSE) is already production-ready | `covered` (`V3-RACE-209..214`) | covered | Keep as baseline; no duplicate epic required for generic Linux execution. |
| Ubuntu pack (`PSEED-300..332`: Snap/Core, Landscape/Juju, AppArmor/seccomp, Pro/Livepatch, MAAS/cloud-init, MicroK8s, Store/Brand Store, certification, migration/support) | `V3-RACE-345` | done | Add Ubuntu enterprise adaptation lane with packaging/orchestration/security/support contracts. |
| FreeBSD pack (`PSEED-340..360`: Ports/Poudriere, ZFS BE, Capsicum/Jails, PF/ipfw, GEOM/geli, hardened kernel defaults) | `V3-RACE-346` | done | Add FreeBSD hardened adaptation lane with OS-native security/storage/network contracts. |
| NixOS pack (`PSEED-370..390`: modules, flakes, impermanence, Home Manager/Nix Darwin, Cachix, containers, Hydra) | `V3-RACE-347` | done | Add NixOS reproducibility adaptation lane with declarative configuration and release-channel controls. |
| Raspberry Pi OS pack (`PSEED-400..420`: Pi boot config, GPIO/I2C/SPI/camera/HAT, firmware bootloader, Pi power/thermal, Imager/SD provisioning, foundation OTA) | `V3-RACE-348` | done | Add Raspberry Pi edge-hardware adaptation lane with provisioning and hardware interfaces. |
| Alpine pack (`PSEED-430..450`: musl/apk, hardened kernel, rootless scratch containers, read-only overlay, static/busybox, secure mirror channels) | `V3-RACE-349` | done | Add Alpine minimal-hardening adaptation lane for container/edge minimal deployments. |
| Cross-distro repository/channel publishing and signed update distribution (Snap/Ports/Cachix/Pi/apk) | `V3-RACE-350` | done | Add unified multi-distro artifact/repository publishing plane with provenance and rollback semantics. |
| Zero-touch provisioning patterns across ecosystems (MAAS/cloud-init/Pi Imager and equivalents) | `V3-RACE-351` | done | Add provisioning automation matrix for bare-metal/edge bootstrap across channel packs. |
| Certified hardware/tuning matrices across Ubuntu and enterprise vendors + Pi classes | `V3-RACE-352` | done | Add multi-vendor hardware certification and tuning-profile lane. |
| Long support alignment + migration tooling (Ubuntu LTS/ESM path and continuity contracts) | `V3-RACE-353` | done | Add long-window lifecycle/migration lane for distro-upgrade continuity. |
| Cross-cutting enforcement: immutable/atomic/rollback, strict SELinux+eBPF/security verification, certified-hardware + air-gapped promotion evidence | `V3-RACE-354`, `V3-RACE-343`, `V3-RACE-344`, `V3-RACE-214` | done | Add ecosystem conformance promotion gate while reusing existing Helix-derived hardening backport controls. |
| Requested follow-on: promote strongest cross-cutting ecosystem patterns back into neutral core for all future stewards | `V3-RACE-355` | done | Add a formal cross-ecosystem uplift lane extending neutral-core backport scope beyond Helix-only inputs. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V3-RACE-345 | extension | V3 | done | Ubuntu Enterprise Adaptation Pack (Snap/Core + Landscape/Juju + Pro/Livepatch + MAAS/MicroK8s) | Ubuntu remains a dominant enterprise Linux surface and needs first-class contracts beyond generic host adaptation. | Add Ubuntu-specific channel adapters for Snap/Core packaging, Landscape/Juju orchestration, AppArmor/seccomp policy packs, Pro/Livepatch lifecycle hooks, MAAS/cloud-init bootstrap, MicroK8s integration, and migration tooling; require verification receipts for each capability path and include rollback-safe fallback to generic channel on feature mismatch. | V3-RACE-211, V3-RACE-212, V3-RACE-213 |

| V3-RACE-347 | extension | V3 | done | NixOS Declarative Adaptation Pack (Modules/Flakes/Impermanence/Hydra/Cachix) | Declarative Nix workflows demand dedicated integration so reproducibility guarantees remain end-to-end. | Add NixOS adapters for module/flake-native config, impermanence/tmpfs patterns, Home Manager/Nix Darwin compatibility bridges, Cachix distribution, container specialisations, and Hydra release hooks; require verification receipts for reproducibility and include rollback-safe channel fallback on evaluation/regression failures. | V3-RACE-211, V3-RACE-228, V3-RACE-120 |

| V3-RACE-354 | hardening | V3 | done | Multi-Platform Linux/Unix Conformance Promotion Gate | Expanding distro coverage increases drift risk unless one consolidated conformance gate enforces adaptation/security/reliability invariants. | Add conformance gate enforcing host-profile match, immutable/atomic/rollback behavior, security policy checks (SELinux/eBPF/AppArmor/Capsicum where relevant), certified-hardware and air-gapped validation requirements, require verification receipts per promotion, and include rollback-safe promotion hold/fallback on gate instability. | V3-RACE-214, V3-RACE-343, V3-RACE-350, V3-RACE-352 |
| V3-RACE-355 | hardening | V3 | done | Neutral-Core Cross-Ecosystem Hardening Uplift (Post-Intake Consolidation) | Valuable patterns from ecosystem-specific lanes should become neutral defaults so future steward variants start from a stronger base. | Extend neutral-core backport pipeline to ingest approved cross-ecosystem controls (immutability patterns, declarative config contracts, minimal-footprint lanes, capability security defaults, hardware-certification checks, long-LTS lifecycle controls), require verification receipts for non-regression across existing steward profiles, and include rollback-safe opt-out toggles for incompatible environments. | V3-RACE-344, V3-RACE-354, V3-RACE-212 |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `10yHeUnXAygr6pRLknGTKF7YIhNgrVVU1wIDHo8ohMUA`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the Platform Adaptation Socket architecture directive into canonical backlog lanes that refactor existing HostProfile/adaptation-channel foundations into a governed plugin model without re-opening already-queued base lanes.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific socket installation preferences and trust overrides live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent socket ABI, loader, registry, and CLI/control-plane surfaces live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- socket manifests, signatures, admission receipts, and activation history remain in `state/` and are never treated as user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Keep current execution order: build Platform Oracle + Adaptation Channels first, then perform socket refactor | `V3-RACE-209`, `V3-RACE-210`, `V3-RACE-211` | done | Preserve foundation-first sequencing; socket work depends on prior adaptation lanes. |
| Define versioned/discoverable `PlatformSocket` interface + signed manifest format for WASM/native plugins (`CORE-005`) | `V3-RACE-356` | done | Add formal socket ABI + manifest schema with signature and capability policy contracts. |
| Implement socket discovery/loading/version pinning/hot-swap in oracle path (`CORE-006`) | `V3-RACE-357` | done | Add governed loader and runtime lifecycle contracts with fail-closed fallback behavior. |
| Add built-in `GenericSocket` carrying universal/general requirements and move system-specific logic to named sockets (`CORE-007`) | `V3-RACE-358` | done | Refactor current generic + specific channel split into socket modules without regressing controls. |
| Add operator surface `protheusctl socket list|install|update|test` + lane API (`CORE-008`) | `V3-RACE-359` | done | Add socket lifecycle CLI/API with receipted install/update/test flows. |
| Add formal verification + redteam admission gate for new sockets with HostProfile match tests and chaos gameday (`CORE-009`) | `V3-RACE-360` | done | Extend hardening gate to enforce socket admission proofs before activation/promotion. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V3-RACE-356 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | PlatformSocket ABI + Signed Manifest Contract | Adaptation channels provide host-aware behavior, but long-term extensibility requires one stable plugin ABI with auditable metadata and capability boundaries. | Added socket ABI/manifest contract implementation in `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_socket_runtime.{ts,js}` backed by `client/runtime/config/platform_socket_registry.json`; manifest fields now enforce identity/version/module type, HostProfile predicates, capability claims, attestation signature metadata, and rollback-compatible activation receipts. | V3-RACE-209, V3-RACE-210, V3-RACE-211 |
| V3-RACE-357 | primitive-upgrade | V3 | done | Socket Discovery/Loader Runtime (Registry + Version Pin + Hot-Swap) | A socket ABI alone is insufficient without governed discovery and lifecycle controls that keep runtime behavior deterministic and fail-closed. | Implemented discover/activate lifecycle in `platform_socket_runtime` with deterministic socket selection from registry, fail-closed fallback to generic socket, signed lifecycle receipts, and persisted install/update/test state under `state/ops/platform_socket_runtime/installed.json` plus audit history in `state/ops/platform_socket_runtime/history.jsonl`. | V3-RACE-356, V3-RACE-210, V3-RACE-176 |
| V3-RACE-358 | extension | V3 | done | GenericSocket + Named Host Socket Refactor (Channels -> Sockets) | Existing channel modules need a controlled refactor target so universal controls remain centralized while host-specific logic remains isolated and swappable. | Refactored adaptation representation into `client/runtime/config/platform_socket_registry.json` with `generic` plus named host sockets (`ubuntu`, `freebsd`, `nixos`, `raspios`, `alpine`), preserving host-predicate activation behavior and fallback semantics while keeping universal contracts centralized in the GenericSocket lane. | V3-RACE-212, V3-RACE-211, V3-RACE-356, V3-RACE-357 |
| V3-RACE-359 | extension | V3 | done | Socket Lifecycle Operator Surface (`protheusctl socket *` + API) | Operators need deterministic governance for socket inventory, install/update policy, and pre-activation testing without direct filesystem edits. | Wired operator lifecycle surfaces through `client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl.ts` and `platform_socket_runtime` for `protheusctl socket list|install|update|test|status|discover|admission`, with receipted audit trails, signature-aware manifest checks, and rollback-safe state tracking for lifecycle operations. | V3-RACE-356, V3-RACE-357, V3-RACE-213 |
| V3-RACE-360 | hardening | V3 | done | Socket Admission Proof Gate (Formal + Redteam + HostProfile Chaos Validation) | Community-installed sockets increase attack and reliability surface unless admission is blocked by explicit proof contracts and adversarial validation. | Added socket admission gate (`platform_socket_runtime admission`) with policy `client/runtime/config/platform_socket_admission_policy.json`, proof-stub requirements (`docs/client/proofs/platform/socket_*.md`), signature/capability validation, chaos eligibility checks, signed admission receipts, and fail/quarantine-ready outputs for non-compliant sockets. | V3-RACE-214, V3-RACE-187, V3-RACE-356, V3-RACE-357 |

### Human Backlog Intake (Socket Governance / Non-Automatable)

| Human Item (from intake) | Owner | Status | Backlog Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Approve socket registry trust policy (who can publish/sign, trust roots, revocation authority) | human | queued | Define governance for community socket publication and emergency revocation controls. |
| Approve hot-swap safety policy (which lanes are eligible, maintenance window constraints, production bypass rules) | human | queued | Set operational safety boundaries for runtime socket replacement behavior. |
| Approve external naming/branding for socket architecture (PlatformSocket vs Host Socket) before public docs | human | queued | Finalize operator-facing terminology and compatibility naming guidance for docs/client/tooling. |

## Human Items Intake (Company Surface + Launch Wrapper, Google Doc `1W2aFzIewL7ttxGwxVxJtHJq29UrKz4Lzwo3w9as-0YQ`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: add non-automatable founder/company-launch actions that shape first-impression trust without mixing them into autonomous technical execution lanes.

| Form legal operating entity for launch wrapper (company/ownership structure) | human | queued | Choose and execute legal formation path, then publish canonical company/legal identity references used in public surfaces. |
| Acquire and secure primary/secondary domains for launch surfaces | human | queued | Purchase/lock domain set, enable renewal/privacy controls, and define canonical redirect + ownership policy. |
| Create GitHub organization and transfer canonical repo from personal namespace | human | queued | Move repository to org ownership, retain commit history, and confirm post-transfer admin/recovery controls. |
| Set org governance defaults (owner/member model, privacy posture, default visibility) | human | queued | Approve org-level membership/privacy defaults and permission model before broad public promotion. |
| Approve public repo metadata and brand positioning text | human | queued | Finalize canonical description, topic tags, and website linkage so public discovery surfaces match launch narrative. |
| Approve repository community baseline (issue templates, code-of-conduct, contribution posture) | human | queued | Publish/ratify community-governance artifacts and response expectations for external contributors. |
| Approve Phase-1 website/landing ownership and launch scope | human | queued | Define MVP site scope, content ownership, and release criteria (including architecture/security/get-started surfaces). |
| Approve README/public docs polish pass and branded CLI output conventions | human | queued | Lock public-facing README structure + badge strategy and decide user-facing CLI branding conventions before launch. |
| Approve founder-origin narrative timing and disclosure policy | human | queued | Decide when/how solo-origin story is disclosed relative to company-facing launch surfaces and hiring narrative. |
| Approve launch communications package (announcement thread, site publish timing, demo collateral) | human | queued | Finalize launch comms sequence, channel ownership, and publish checklist with explicit go/no-go signoff. |

## Human Items Intake (Launch Escalation Governance, Google Doc `1ERJ6mgv0h3Hfa0LEAefeP57vke5bAcFX4CyVB0f2Slk`, 2026-03-02)

### Cycle Break Overrides (Dependency DAG Repair)

| ID | Class | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V3-RACE-260 | dependency-repair | V3 | done | Apple On-Device-First Sovereignty Gate (PCC-Only Cloud Exception) | Cycle edge to V3-RACE-255 blocked queue progression despite equivalent policy preconditions already covered by governance and sovereignty gates. | Dependency graph repaired to acyclic form; downstream queue execution must verify gate behavior and preserve explicit rollback/fallback controls during promotion. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_queue_executor.ts`. | V3-RACE-214, V3-RACE-017 |
