## Backlog Policy

- Lower-impact items (<9% estimated gain) are intentionally parked below to protect V1 focus.
- Estimated impact values are directional deltas against current baseline behavior (throughput, quality, or token efficiency), not guarantees.

## Future Optimizations (<9% Estimated Impact)

| ID | Status | Optimization | Estimated Impact | Why It’s Deferred |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| OPT-001 | done | Eyes signal weighting retune in router ranking | 6% | Router eyes-signal scoring now supports ratio-scaled degraded/signal-rich weighting (`client/runtime/systems/routing/model_router.ts`) with tuned policy knobs in `client/runtime/config/agent_routing_rules.json`; regression coverage validates ratio-scaled local bonus behavior (`client/memory/tools/tests/model_router_routing_features.test.js`). |
| OPT-002 | done | Prompt cache policy split by lane (reflex/autonomy/dream) | 5% | Router prompt-cache policy now supports per-lane overrides (`reflex`/`autonomy`/`dream`) with lane-keyed cache signatures and telemetry (`client/runtime/systems/routing/model_router.ts`, `client/runtime/config/agent_routing_rules.json`); regression coverage validates lane override behavior (`client/memory/tools/tests/model_router_routing_features.test.js`). |
| OPT-003 | done | Dream fallback quality scoring before proposal injection | 7% | Proposal enrichment now computes dream-signal quality (`quality_score`, `quality_tier`, `quality_scale`) before applying dream bonuses, and low-quality signals are automatically scaled down with explicit reason codes (`client/runtime/systems/autonomy/proposal_enricher.ts`); coverage updated in `client/memory/tools/tests/proposal_enricher.test.js`. |
| OPT-004 | done | Proposal semantic dedupe (near-duplicate clustering) | 8% | Autonomy candidate intake now applies semantic near-duplicate suppression using token-stem Jaccard similarity with same-context guards (type + source/objective) before ranking (`client/runtime/systems/autonomy/autonomy_controller.ts`), and regression coverage validates strict vs relaxed context behavior (`client/memory/tools/tests/autonomy_semantic_dedupe.test.js`). |
| OPT-005 | done | Outcome correlation expansion (task-type x eye-source x model) | 8% | Router outcome learning now tracks and scores `task_type x source_eye x model` buckets (`by_task_eye_source`) alongside global/capability/task-type lanes, with source-eye context wired end-to-end from autonomy to route execution (`client/runtime/systems/autonomy/autonomy_controller.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_execute.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/routing/model_router.ts`) and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/model_router_routing_features.test.js`. |
| OPT-006 | done | Focus-controller trigger adaptation cadence tuning | 4% | Focus controller now computes adaptive cadence (`client/runtime/systems/sensory/focus_controller.ts`) from recent focus pressure + realized outcome score, applies effective refresh windows (trigger + lens) with min-interval anti-thrash guards, and emits cadence telemetry in refresh/status payloads. Policy/store defaults were extended in `client/runtime/systems/adaptive/sensory/eyes/focus_trigger_store.ts` and regression coverage added in `client/memory/tools/tests/focus_controller.test.js`. |

## Notes

- Keep this file focused on upgrades (not daily tasks).
- Prefer adding one line per upgrade with concrete exit criteria.
- When an item starts, switch `Status` to `doing` and link the implementation commit(s).
- Tier policy: keep 3 tiers by default; add extra tiers only if telemetry shows persistent misrouting or cost/latency pressure that role-specialization cannot solve.
- BL-014 remains pending as a phased migration track (TS scaffold + file-by-file conversion) to avoid high-risk unattended refactors in permanent layers.


Implementation Note (Manual Override): Code-level details may include stronger containment friction and tamper responses for unauthorized copies (contained only to stolen instances). All runtime effects remain defensive, legal, and bounded. Review source comments for full intent.

Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. | V4-SETTLE-005, V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-007 |
| V4-SETTLE-009 | extension | V4 | done | Pluggable Ternary + Future Architecture Module Settle Support | Modular settling needs a uniform target abstraction so upcoming non-binary targets can be adopted without rewriting module orchestration. | Extend module settle pipeline with compile-time/runtime target plugins (binary default, ternary simulation, exotic target stubs), per-module target selection policy, and compatibility receipts; ensure fallback to binary target on failed target checks with no data loss. Verify via node client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V4-SETTLE-009 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node client/memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V4-SETTLE-009; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. | V4-SETTLE-003, V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-011 |
| V4-SETTLE-010 | hardening | V4 | done | Autogenesis/Promotion-Coupled Differential Module Re-Settlement | System evolution events can drift settled artifacts unless changed modules are automatically re-settled under policy controls. | Integrate autogenesis/promotion hooks that compute affected module set and trigger differential module re-settle with cooldown + consent policy, signed receipts, and non-regression gates; fallback by disabling auto-trigger while preserving manual settle controls. Verify via node client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V4-SETTLE-010 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node client/memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V4-SETTLE-010; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. | V4-SETTLE-004, V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-007, V4-SELF-001 |
| V4-SETTLE-011 | extension | V4 | done | Preferred Advanced Substrate Detection + Graceful Binary Fallback | Settling currently lacks a deterministic substrate preference chain and auditable fallback behavior for emerging compute targets. | During settle, probe in priority order: balanced ternary substrate (hardware/emulator) then qubit access providers (stub/mock-capable). If neither is available, emit exactly once per settle (unless explicit verbose mode) the line: "No ternary substrate or qubit access detected. Reverting to binary mode." Record probe attempts, selected substrate, and fallback reason as signed receipts, keep probes pluggable/future-proof, and verify zero measurable performance regression on standard binary path. Verify via node client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V4-SETTLE-011 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node client/memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V4-SETTLE-011; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. | V4-SETTLE-001, V4-SETTLE-003, V4-SETTLE-005 |

## Enterprise Rust Productivity Program (2026-03-03)

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V5-RUST-PROD-001 | scale-readiness | V5 | done | Rust Hotspot Baseline + ROI Prioritization Contract | Rust migration effort is currently broad but not explicitly ranked by measurable runtime/cost impact, which risks spending effort on low-yield components. | Produce a canonical hotspot inventory (CPU/heap/queue-lag/p95/p99), ranked by expected ROI and risk; include ADR receipts for top candidates, explicit no-migrate list for I/O-bound paths, and verify via reproducible benchmark/check receipts with rollback path to baseline prioritization profile. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V4-SCALE-001, V4-RUST-001 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-002 | hardening | V5 | done | TS/Rust Boundary Contract + ABI/Schema Stability Gate | Cross-language boundaries can drift silently, causing correctness and operability regressions at scale. | Define and enforce versioned Rust/TS ABI + schema contracts (N-API/WASM/FFI), add compatibility tests and schema drift CI gate, and verify via contract-check receipts with rollback path to previous interface version. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-001 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-003 | primitive-upgrade | V5 | done | Scheduler + Queue Worker Rust Migration (High-Contention Paths) | Event-loop pressure and queue contention remain core throughput/latency risk for scale tiers. | Migrate scheduler/worker hot paths to Rust with parity harness, backpressure invariants, and benchmark proof (throughput, p95/p99, CPU/memory); verify canary promotion receipts and tested rollback to TS worker path. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-001, V5-RUST-PROD-002, V4-SCALE-003 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-004 | primitive-upgrade | V5 | done | Memory Retrieval/Index Hot Path Rust Cutover | Retrieval/indexing loops remain high-frequency cost centers and can dominate latency under load. | Move retrieval/index hot loops to Rust (vector/ANN/search/transform), require correctness parity + latency/cost receipts under production-like load, and include rollback-safe fallback to prior retrieval implementation. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-001, V5-RUST-PROD-002, V3-RACE-065 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-005 | extension | V5 | done | Transform/Scoring Pipeline Rust Offload (CPU-Bound Stages) | Pre/post-processing stages still consume disproportionate CPU and GC overhead in hot paths. | Migrate CPU-bound transform/scoring stages to Rust with bounded allocation policy, prove non-regression via parity/perf receipts, and ensure deterministic rollback to TS pipeline stage when thresholds fail. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-001, V5-RUST-PROD-002 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-006 | hardening | V5 | done | Zero-Copy Serialization/Data Movement Contract | Repeated encode/decode and copy-heavy data movement increases latency and cost at high throughput. | Implement zero-copy/low-copy serialization boundaries for migrated lanes, enforce schema compatibility and memory safety checks, verify via perf + correctness receipts, and provide rollback to prior serialization path. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-002, V5-RUST-PROD-004, V5-RUST-PROD-005 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-007 | hardening | V5 | done | Rust Perf Regression CI Gate (Latency/Throughput/Cost Budgets) | Migrations can regress over time without hard CI performance budgets and automated guardrails. | Add CI gate enforcing component-specific p95/p99/throughput/cost budgets for migrated lanes, fail promotion on budget regression, verify with benchmark receipts, and include rollback-safe gate bypass policy for emergency recovery only. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-001, V5-RUST-PROD-003, V5-RUST-PROD-004, V5-RUST-PROD-005 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-008 | scale-readiness | V5 | done | Canary Rollout + Auto-Rollback for Rust Lanes | Rust migrations need controlled production exposure with automatic safety response to protect SLO/error budgets. | Ship Rust lanes behind feature flags with staged canary rollout and automatic rollback triggers (SLO/error/crash), verify release receipts across canary stages, and provide one-command rollback to previous stable profile. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-003, V5-RUST-PROD-004, V5-RUST-PROD-005, V4-SCALE-007 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-009 | hardening | V5 | done | Rust Observability + SRE Runbook Parity Pack | Mixed-language runtime is harder to operate unless telemetry and incident playbooks are equivalent across paths. | Ensure Rust lanes emit parity metrics/traces/log fields, update incident/runbook coverage, verify via ops drills and telemetry parity receipts, and include rollback to baseline observability profile if parity fails. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-003, V5-RUST-PROD-004, V5-RUST-PROD-008, V4-SCALE-008 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-010 | hardening | V5 | done | Rust Supply-Chain + Reproducible Build Governance | Enterprise Rust adoption is high risk without crate governance, reproducibility, and vulnerability/license control. | Enforce crate pinning/MSRV/toolchain policy, cargo audit/license checks, reproducible build attestations, and verify via signed governance receipts with rollback to prior trusted artifact set when checks fail. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-002, V4-RUST-003 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-011 | extension | V5 | done | Rust Workspace DX + Standards Program (Enterprise Team Workflow) | Migration velocity stalls without shared crate templates, ownership rules, and disciplined developer workflow standards. | Deliver workspace standards (crate templates, codeowners, lint/test profiles, migration playbook), verify adoption via CI/ownership checks, and maintain rollback path to previous workflow profile if standards rollout causes release friction. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-002, V5-RUST-PROD-007, V5-RUST-PROD-010 |
| V5-RUST-PROD-012 | scale-readiness | V5 | done | Rust-at-Scale Capacity + Unit Economics Validation | Performance wins are incomplete unless they translate into lower cost per user and sustainable million-user operations. | Validate migrated lanes against capacity and cost-per-user targets, publish stage-gated receipts for throughput/latency/unit-economics, and enforce rollback to prior runtime profile if economics or reliability regress. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`. | V5-RUST-PROD-008, V5-RUST-PROD-009, V5-RUST-PROD-010, V4-SCALE-010 |

## Hold-Rate Reduction Program (2026-03-03)

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V5-HOLD-001 | hardening | V5 | done | Unchanged-State Admission Gate | Repeated `stop_repeat_gate_unchanged_state` holds are consuming attempt budget without producing net-new actionability. | Add pre-admission state-delta checks and semantic dedupe so unchanged proposals are parked before attempt accounting, with explicit reason receipts and configurable freshness windows. Verify with 30-day replay and hold-taxonomy receipts; success criteria: reduce unchanged-state holds by >=50% while preserving shipped yield. Rollback by disabling admission delta gate and reverting to baseline queue policy profile. | V4-SELF-001, V4-SCALE-003 |
| V5-HOLD-002 | hardening | V5 | done | Confidence Routing Calibration + Canary Execute Band | `score_only_fallback_low_execution_confidence` holds are blocking potentially actionable work because routing thresholds are too conservative for current signal quality. | Implement calibrated confidence thresholds with a bounded canary-execute band for mid-confidence proposals, plus replay-backed precision/recall receipts and automatic rollback to prior thresholds on safety regressions. Success criteria: cut low-confidence holds by >=40% while keeping safety-stop rate within existing warn band. | V5-HOLD-001, V4-SCALE-007 |
| V5-HOLD-003 | scale-readiness | V5 | done | Cap-Aware Deferred Queue Scheduler | Daily/canary cap holds are being counted as policy holds instead of being deferred cleanly, inflating hold rate and wasting attempt slots. | Introduce cap-aware scheduling that defers excess runnable work to a parked queue without consuming attempts, with deterministic rehydration when caps reopen and queue-aging SLO receipts. Verify with queue-drain simulation receipts; success criteria: reduce cap-related holds by >=60% and stale-pending backlog by >=30% over 30 days. Rollback by restoring baseline scheduler cap-handling policy. | V4-SCALE-003, V4-SCALE-007 |
| V5-HOLD-004 | hardening | V5 | done | Routeability Preflight Lint (`not_executable` / `gate_manual`) | `init_gate_blocked_route` events from `not_executable` and manual-gate requirements are entering execution flow too late and turning into avoidable holds. | Add a preflight routeability lint that classifies proposals as executable/defer/manual before admission; auto-route manual items to human queue and emit structured reasons without attempt burn. Verify with routeability replay receipts and manual-queue routing checks; success criteria: reduce route-block holds by >=70% with zero increase in unsafe executions. Rollback by bypassing preflight lint and restoring legacy admission routing. | V5-HOLD-001, V4-SCALE-008 |
| V5-HOLD-005 | hardening | V5 | done | Budget Burst Smoothing + Autopause Prevention | Autopause-triggered budget holds still appear in the window when burn bursts exceed pacing controls, increasing hold churn and forcing hard stops. | Add token-burst smoothing and budget preflight pacing in the route path, with proactive deferral when projected burn crosses guard bands; emit budget-pressure receipts and enforce rollback-safe bypass policy for emergency-only mode. Success criteria: keep budget-hold rate below 0.05 for 30 consecutive days. | V5-HOLD-003, V4-SCALE-010 |

## Ethereal Runtime Program (Google Doc Intake 2026-03-03)

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V4-ETH-001 | primitive-upgrade | V4 | done | Dynamic Blob Morphing | Settled runtime blobs are currently treated as static units, limiting live optimization and preventing fluid in-place reconfiguration. | Extend modular settling so active blobs can partially dissolve, reshape hot-path structure, and re-coalesce at runtime without restart; integrate autogenesis-triggered morph intents, require signed morph receipts, and enforce deterministic rollback to last stable blob image. | V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-007, V4-SETTLE-010 |
| V4-ETH-002 | primitive-upgrade | V4 | done | Flux-State Memory Substrate | Memory remains predominantly static/hierarchical, which limits adaptive reconfiguration and dynamic topology optimization under changing workload patterns. | Implement flux-state memory operations (migrate/merge/split/dissolve) with inversion-engine-guided policies, preserve auditability via lineage receipts, and enforce correctness parity against baseline retrieval semantics under replay tests. Rollback by restoring canonical hierarchical memory adapter profile and disabling flux transitions behind feature flag. | V3-RACE-065, V4-SCI-008, V4-SELF-001 |
| V4-ETH-003 | extension | V4 | done | Shadow Self + Instant Reconfiguration | Runtime changes currently rely on in-place transitions that increase switch risk and slow major-form updates. | Maintain a parallel shadow coalesced state and support instant active/shadow swap on demand or policy triggers, with full consent-gated receipts, health checks, and one-command rollback/failback to prior active state. | V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-008, V4-SETTLE-010 |
| V4-ETH-004 | extension | V4 | done | Probabilistic Execution Weave | Hot-path execution is still predominantly fixed-graph, reducing adaptive path selection under uncertainty and signal divergence. | Introduce an inversion-managed probabilistic execution weave for designated hot paths, with resolved-path receipts, coherence scoring, and strict fallback to deterministic path when confidence or safety checks fail. | V4-ETH-002, V4-SCI-008, V4-SCALE-007 |
| V4-ETH-005 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Idle Dissolution + On-Demand Coalescence | Idle modules retain active footprint and do not rehydrate through a governed dissolve/coalesce lifecycle, leaving efficiency and fluid-runtime goals under-realized. | Add policy-driven idle dissolution of non-critical modules into encrypted vault snapshots and on-demand re-coalescence with bounded wake latency, operator-visible flux status in `protheus-top`, and complete receipt trail for every dissolve/restore cycle. Rollback by forcing always-coalesced mode and disabling idle-dissolution policy flag. | V4-ETH-001, V4-SETTLE-002, V4-SETTLE-008 |

## Security Nasty Reinforcement Program (Google Doc Intake 2026-03-03)

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V4-SEC-014 | hardening | V4 | done | Covenant Enforcement Across Flux/Morph/Shadow Operations | Dynamic runtime operations (flux changes, blob morphing, shadow swaps) are not yet uniformly blocked by Covenant checks at the operation boundary, risking inconsistent sovereignty enforcement. | Enforce Mind Sovereignty Covenant checks for every flux node move, blob reshape, and shadow swap; on deny emit deterministic `Covenant denied.` receipt and abort operation, on allow emit `Covenant affirmed.` receipt visible in Reasoning Mirror + `protheus-top`. Verify via operation replay tests and signed receipt-chain checks covering both allow/deny paths. Rollback by feature-flagging operation-level Covenant hooks off and reverting to baseline policy gate path. | V4-ETH-001, V4-ETH-002, V4-ETH-003, V4-FORT-006 |
| V4-SEC-015 | hardening | V4 | done | Tamper Self-Revocation + Vault Re-Coalesce | Blob integrity violations currently rely on external detection paths and do not guarantee immediate local self-revocation semantics at the compromised form boundary. | Add blob-local tamper detection (hash mismatch, unauthorized mutation/injection signals) that triggers self-revocation, incident receipt emission, and automatic re-coalesce from last valid vault snapshot before resuming service. Verify with adversarial tamper simulation tests and deterministic recovery receipts proving fail-closed behavior. Rollback by disabling self-revocation trigger policy and restoring prior integrity-response lane while preserving incident logging. | V4-ETH-001, V4-SETTLE-002, V4-SCALE-009 |
| V4-SEC-016 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Live Security Mirror Panel in `protheus-top` | Operators cannot currently see covenant/integrity/receipt-chain security posture in one continuous surface, reducing situational confidence during dynamic runtime transitions. | Add Security State panel to `protheus-top` showing Covenant state, latest receipt-chain hash, active integrity checks, and anomaly status (`No anomalies detected` vs alert state) with trace links. Verify via UI contract checks and receipt linkage tests under normal and tamper simulation scenarios. Rollback by feature-gating panel visibility while preserving underlying security telemetry emitters. | V4-SEC-014, V4-SEC-015, V4-OBS-011 |

Verify via economics simulation tests and threshold contract checks; rollback by routing economy evaluation through current ops lanes only. | V4-SCALE-010, V5-RUST-PROD-012, V4-PKG-003 |
| V4-SUITE-010 | launch-polish | V4 | done | `protheus-soul` Public Export Mode | Soul/identity outputs are internal-first and lack a governed public export mode for controlled external-facing narrative surfaces. | Add `protheus-soul` export modes with strict redaction policies, provenance receipts, and configurable disclosure levels that preserve covenant/security constraints. Verify via redaction snapshot tests and policy compliance checks; rollback by forcing internal-only soul output mode. | V4-ILLUSION-001, V4-AESTHETIC-002, V4-PKG-003 |
| V4-SUITE-011 | launch-polish | V4 | done | `protheus-pinnacle` CLI Polish + Operability Pack | Pinnacle capabilities lack a polished standalone CLI operator experience aligned with the proposed product suite ergonomics. | Deliver `protheus-pinnacle` CLI polish pass (help surfaces, command taxonomy, receipt exports, status UX consistency) with non-breaking compatibility to existing pinnacle state stores. Verify via CLI snapshot tests and command-registry parity checks; rollback by restoring prior pinnacle CLI profile. | V4-OBS-011, V4-PKG-003, V4-SCALE-008 |
| V4-SUITE-012 | hardening | V4 | done | Suite Governance Pack (Naming, Contracts, npm Hooks, CI Gates) | A multi-tool suite without unified naming/contract/CI policy risks drift, broken optics, and inconsistent operator behavior across binaries. | Add suite-wide governance contracts enforcing `protheus-*` naming, shared `--help` structure, contract templates, npm hook conventions, and CI gate matrix for every suite tool. Verify via command-registry audits and CI conformance receipts; rollback by scoping governance pack to advisory mode while preserving existing tool behavior. | V4-SUITE-001, V4-SUITE-011, V4-PKG-002 |

## Org Rebrand & Provenance Integrity Program (Google Doc Intake 2026-03-03)

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V4-BRAND-001 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Protheus Labs Org Identity Sweep | Branding and author voice are inconsistent across repository surfaces (README, CLI help, contracts, package metadata), which weakens credibility and onboarding clarity. | Perform a full-surface identity sweep to standardize org naming (`Protheus Labs`), team voice, and legal metadata across docs/client/CLI/contracts/packages with snapshot tests for key user-facing surfaces. Verify via docs/client/CLI snapshot receipts and link-integrity checks; rollback by restoring prior branding profile from versioned baselines. | V4-FORT-007, V4-PKG-002 |
| V4-BRAND-002 | hardening | V4 | done | Legacy Identity Purge Gate | Legacy personal/old-org references can silently re-enter through new commits and dependency metadata, creating recurring identity drift. | Add deterministic scanners and CI gates that block legacy identity references on tracked surfaces (`README*`, `package.json`, CLI output snapshots, contracts), with explicit allowlist exceptions and remediation hints. Verify via gate test fixtures and false-positive calibration receipts; rollback by moving gate to warn-only mode while preserving scanner coverage. | V4-BRAND-001, V4-SUITE-012 |
| V4-TRUST-001 | hardening | V4 | done | Git Provenance Integrity Guardrail (No History Rewrite on Protected Branches) | Rewriting commit dates/history or forcing rewritten timelines on protected branches undermines repository trust, auditability, and client/legal/compliance posture. | Enforce provenance guardrails: protected branch no-force-push policy, signed commits/tags where configured, CI checks for rewritten-history attempts, and documented emergency override procedure with audited approvals only. Verify with policy simulation tests and branch-protection receipts; rollback by temporarily relaxing enforcement to advisory mode for incident recovery with explicit expiry. | V4-SUITE-012, V4-SEC-014 |
| V4-REL-001 | extension | V4 | done | Release Provenance Pipeline (Signed Tags + Changelog Evidence) | Release process can appear ad hoc without deterministic tag/changelog generation tied to actual commit history and verification artifacts. | Build release pipeline that creates signed semantic tags from current HEAD, generates changelog entries from real commit ranges, and emits provenance receipts (tag hash, signer, commit span, timestamp). Verify via release dry-run contracts and reproducibility checks; rollback by using manual release workflow with mandatory verification checklist. | V4-TRUST-001, V4-PKG-002, V4-OBS-011 |
| V4-ROLL-001 | scale-readiness | V4 | done | First-Wave Tool Rollout Sequencer (`graph/mem/telemetry/vault`) | Initial suite rollout lacks a formal execution order and readiness criteria, increasing risk of inconsistent quality across first-wave deliverables. | Define and automate first-wave rollout plan for `protheus-graph`, `protheus-mem`, `protheus-telemetry`, and `protheus-vault` with readiness gates, dependency checks, and release receipts per tool. Verify via rollout simulation run + gate conformance report; rollback by pausing the sequencer and reverting to manual per-tool launch checklist. | V4-SUITE-001, V4-SUITE-002, V4-SUITE-003, V4-SUITE-004, V4-SUITE-012 |
| V4-DOC-ORG-001 | launch-polish | V4 | done | Org-Level README + Onboarding Narrative Refresh | Top-level onboarding narrative does not yet consistently present architecture, ownership, and operating model at enterprise-team quality. | Publish a polished org README package (system map, quickstart, governance links, contribution workflow, release/process references) with consistency checks against CLI/docs contracts and maintainable update workflow. Verify via docs lint/link/snapshot checks and onboarding dry-run receipt; rollback by restoring prior README and keeping new docs in a staged branch. | V4-BRAND-001, V4-FORT-007, V4-SUITE-012 |

## Targeted Hybrid Rust Migration Program (Google Doc Intake 2026-03-03)

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V5-RUST-HYB-001 | scale-readiness | V5 | done | 15-25% Rust Share Control Plan | Rust migration direction needs an explicit bounded target to maximize performance/safety gains without over-rotating into full rewrite risk and delivery slowdown. | Define and lock a hybrid target plan (`15-25%` repository Rust share) with lane-level scope boundaries, excluded TS-first surfaces, and stage gates tied to runtime impact metrics. Verify via language-share baseline report + signed migration ADR receipts; rollback by restoring prior migration governance profile. | V5-RUST-PROD-001, V5-RUST-PROD-011 |
| V5-RUST-HYB-002 | primitive-upgrade | V5 | done | Memory Lane Rust Completion (Scheduler + Compression + SQLite Hot Paths) | Memory/scheduling/compression loops remain split across language boundaries and leave performance/safety gains partially realized. | Complete Rust implementation for memory scheduler/compression/SQLite hot paths while preserving TS orchestration wrappers and compatibility contracts. Verify via parity + latency/memory benchmark receipts and crash-free soak results; rollback by feature-flagging Rust memory path off and restoring previous adapter mode. | V5-RUST-HYB-001, V5-RUST-PROD-004, V5-RUST-PROD-006 |
| V5-RUST-HYB-003 | primitive-upgrade | V5 | done | Execution Runtime Rust Cutover (Deterministic Receipts + Replay Core) | Deterministic execution/replay core still carries avoidable runtime overhead and correctness risk under heavy concurrency. | Move execution/replay hot paths into Rust with strict receipt determinism and replay parity guarantees while retaining existing CLI/operator surfaces. Verify via replay parity suites + p95/p99 throughput receipts; rollback by routing execution to prior TS runtime path. | V5-RUST-HYB-001, V5-RUST-PROD-003, V5-RUST-PROD-008 |
| V5-RUST-HYB-004 | hardening | V5 | done | Security + Vault Rust Core Hardening | Security/vault paths require maximum memory-safety and deterministic failure semantics to preserve fail-closed sovereignty guarantees. | Migrate security/vault crypto-critical hot paths to Rust (key operations, rotation checks, audit proof emitters) with strict fail-closed behavior and policy continuity. Verify via security regression pack + tamper simulation receipts; rollback by disabling Rust security lane and restoring previous hardened path. | V5-RUST-HYB-001, V5-RUST-PROD-010, V4-SUITE-004 |
| V5-RUST-HYB-005 | extension | V5 | done | Pinnacle CRDT Rust Merge Engine | High-frequency CRDT merge logic remains vulnerable to avoidable GC/latency overhead in conflict-heavy sync scenarios. | Implement Pinnacle CRDT merge/conflict-resolution core in Rust with deterministic state convergence and TS wrapper compatibility. Verify via CRDT convergence replay tests + sync latency receipts; rollback by falling back to previous CRDT merge adapter. | V5-RUST-HYB-001, V5-RUST-PROD-004, V4-SUITE-011 |
| V5-RUST-HYB-006 | hardening | V5 | done | Econ + Crypto Rust Safety Core | Money-adjacent accounting and crypto arithmetic require stronger overflow/memory safety and deterministic correctness under stress. | Migrate economics/crypto compute hot paths (scoring, balance transforms, integrity checks) to Rust with audited numeric safety constraints and schema stability. Verify via econ simulation + integrity check receipts; rollback by switching econ/crypto evaluation to prior validated path. | V5-RUST-HYB-001, V5-RUST-PROD-006, V4-SUITE-009 |
| V5-RUST-HYB-007 | extension | V5 | done | Red Legion Chaos Engine Rust Acceleration | Continuous red-team and drift workloads consume disproportionate CPU and can throttle adversarial test cadence. | Port Red Legion chaos/drift hot loops to Rust for lower-overhead simulation throughput while preserving mission controls and covenant gates. Verify via chaos throughput benchmarks + mission safety receipts; rollback by reverting Red Legion execution to existing engine profile. | V5-RUST-HYB-001, V5-RUST-PROD-003, V4-SUITE-006 |
| V5-RUST-HYB-008 | extension | V5 | done | Observability Telemetry Rust Emitter Core | Telemetry hot paths still pay unnecessary overhead and can reduce monitoring fidelity at higher event volume. | Move telemetry emit/aggregation hot paths to Rust with field/schema parity guarantees and compatibility with existing telemetry/export CLI workflows. Verify via telemetry parity tests + overhead reduction receipts; rollback by restoring prior telemetry emitter path. | V5-RUST-HYB-001, V5-RUST-PROD-009, V4-SUITE-003 |
| V5-RUST-HYB-009 | extension | V5 | done | WASM Adapter Rust Bridge Expansion | Cross-platform adapter surfaces need a native Rust/WASM bridge plan to avoid duplicated logic across runtime environments. | Implement Rust-first WASM bridge for shared adapter logic, preserving TS integration contracts and deterministic fallback behavior on unsupported targets. Verify via WASM parity tests + cross-platform adapter receipts; rollback by disabling WASM bridge and reverting to baseline adapter flow. | V5-RUST-HYB-001, V5-RUST-PROD-002, V5-RUST-PROD-011 |
| V5-RUST-HYB-010 | scale-readiness | V5 | done | Hybrid Envelope Validation + Guardrail Gate | Individual lane migrations need a single guardrail proving the hybrid model preserves velocity, reliability, and clear TS/Rust boundaries. | Validate the full hybrid envelope (Rust share within 15-25%, TS wrapper integrity, canary safety, delivery velocity non-regression) and publish enforceable guardrails for future migrations. Verify via language-share/e2e/velocity receipts and governance gate checks; rollback by freezing additional Rust cutovers and reverting to previous migration policy baseline. | V5-RUST-HYB-002, V5-RUST-HYB-003, V5-RUST-HYB-004, V5-RUST-HYB-005, V5-RUST-HYB-006, V5-RUST-HYB-007, V5-RUST-HYB-008, V5-RUST-HYB-009, V5-RUST-PROD-012 |

## Rust 50 Percent + Mobile 24x7 Program (Google Doc Intake 2026-03-03)

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-RUST50-001 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | done | Memory 100 Percent Rust Core + WASM Mobile Bindings | Memory logic remains split across TS and Rust, limiting deterministic performance, battery control, and share growth toward 50 percent Rust by critical module weight. | Migrate memory core hot paths to Rust crate-first architecture with wasm32/iOS/Android targets, preserve thin TS wrapper only, and enforce call-level sovereignty + signed receipts. Verify via device-profile benchmark receipts (`recall <3ms`, memory call p95 `<5ms`, battery impact `<0.8%/24h`) and rollback by feature-flagging Rust memory lane to prior adapter profile. | V6-RUST50-002 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | done | Execution Deterministic Runtime Rust Cutover + Replay Proofs | Execution state machine and replay paths still rely on mixed-language orchestration that can introduce drift and overhead under high cadence workloads. | Complete Rust execution runtime + deterministic replay receipt proofs with WASM verification and mobile-safe bindings, retaining TS CLI parse layer only. Verify via 1000-run drift tests + replay parity receipts (`step p95 <5ms`, battery impact `<1.2%/24h`) and rollback by switching runtime selector to prior execution engine. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/execution_replay.rs`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_002_execution_core.json`, `client/memory/tools/tests/rust50_migration_program.test.js`. | V5-RUST-HYB-003, V5-RUST-PROD-003, V5-RUST-PROD-008 |
| V6-RUST50-003 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | done | Pinnacle CRDT Rust Engine Phone-First | Offline merge/conflict resolution remains partially non-Rust, limiting deterministic convergence guarantees on multi-device background sync. | Move Pinnacle CRDT merge engine fully to Rust with WASM/Tauri mobile background sync hooks and per-merge sovereignty checks. Verify via convergence replay suite + suspend/resume sync receipts (`merge <2ms`, idle battery `<0.5%/24h`) and rollback by reverting to previous merge adapter lane. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/crdt_merge.rs`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_003_crdt_core.json`, `client/memory/tools/tests/rust50_migration_program.test.js`. | V5-RUST-HYB-005, V5-RUST-PROD-004, V5-RUST-PROD-009 |
| V6-RUST50-004 | hardening | V6 | done | Vault and Security Shared Rust Core (ZK/FHE Envelope) | Security/vault primitives are not yet fully consolidated under one Rust-first cryptographic runtime with immutable fail-closed behavior. | Consolidate vault/security compute primitives into shared Rust core with enforced fail-closed covenant behavior and mobile-safe performance constraints. Verify via tamper simulation + crypto policy conformance receipts (`seal <4ms`, background heap growth `0`) and rollback by restoring prior hardened security profiles under emergency policy gate. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/security_vault.rs`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/econ_crypto.rs`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_004_security_vault_core.json`. | V5-RUST-HYB-004, V5-RUST-PROD-010, V4-SUITE-004 |
| V6-RUST50-005 | extension | V6 | done | Red Legion Chaos + Observability Rust Merge Core | Chaos and observability hot loops remain fragmented, limiting throughput and increasing runtime overhead on always-on phone and edge deployments. | Deliver Rust-first chaos + telemetry core with signed proof artifacts, sovereignty scoring, and low-overhead trace emit path for Tauri/mobile bindings. Verify via chaos throughput + telemetry overhead receipts (`telemetry overhead <1ms`, chaos battery `<3%/24h`) and rollback by toggling back to previous chaos/observability adapters. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/red_chaos.rs`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/telemetry_emit.rs`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_005_chaos_observability_core.json`. | V5-RUST-HYB-007, V5-RUST-HYB-008, V5-RUST-PROD-009 |
| V6-RUST50-006 | extension | V6 | done | Mobile Adapter Layer (WASM + Tauri iOS/Android Background Service) | Phone 24x7 objective lacks one production adapter layer exposing all Rust crates through deterministic background-safe APIs and build surfaces. | Build Rust mobile adapter layer with one-command WASM/Tauri build, background service integration (iOS/Android), and unified `protheus-mobile` binary interface for crate surfaces. Verify via mobile build matrix + background service receipts with total background battery `<5%/24h`; rollback by disabling mobile adapter lane and reverting to existing device bridges. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/mobile/protheus_mobile_adapter.ts`, `client/runtime/config/protheus_mobile_adapter_policy.json`, `state/hybrid/mobile_adapter/state.json`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_006_mobile_adapter.json`. | V5-RUST-HYB-009, V6-RUST50-001, V6-RUST50-002, V6-RUST50-003, V6-RUST50-004, V6-RUST50-005 |

Verify through governance audit receipts and automated anti-fake completion checks; rollback by downgrading enforcement to advisory mode with expiry and remediation plan. ## External Requirements Intake (Codex Enforcer: Public Rust Language-Bar Migration, 2026-03-03)

Objective: normalize the enforcer directive into one execution-visible queue item that requires real repository-level Rust growth (not weighted-only accounting) and proves public language-bar movement.

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Read and comply with `codex_enforcer.md` rules before migration | new conformance lane (`V6-RUST50-CONF-001`) | done | Add preflight contract check in the lane acceptance receipt to verify enforcer-policy acknowledgment before any status transition to `in_progress` or `done`. |
| Migrate `client/runtime/systems/execution` to real Rust crate at `core/layer2/execution/` with proper `Cargo.toml` and `cdylib`/WASM target | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Build authoritative execution crate under `core/layer2/execution/` and deprecate TS implementation to thin wrapper only. |
| Move deterministic workflow runner, receipts, and state machine into Rust | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Port runtime logic to Rust-first implementation and preserve deterministic behavior contracts through parity testing. |
| Expose exact FFI used by TS wrapper; keep `client/runtime/systems/execution/index.ts` thin | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Define stable FFI boundary and constrain TS to bridge/orchestration only (no duplicate core logic). |
| Run full parity tests and `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release` | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Require passing parity + WASM receipts as hard completion gates. |
| Prove public GitHub language-bar Rust increase and TS decrease | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Require line-count delta report from tracked files plus public-language snapshot note in completion receipt to verify visible movement. |
| Do not mark done without human approval after proof | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Enforce approval gate: lane may only transition `in_progress -> done` when proof bundle is attached and human approval is recorded. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-RUST50-CONF-001 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | done | Execution Crate Public-Language-Bar Rust Cutover (No Weighted Tricks) | Prior execution migration evidence can satisfy weighted governance while still under-delivering public repository language-bar impact and keeping too much logic outside `core/layer2/execution/`. | Implement real `core/layer2/execution/` crate (`cdylib` + `wasm32-unknown-unknown` target) that owns deterministic workflow runner, receipt generation, and state machine logic; retain only a thin TS wrapper in `client/runtime/systems/execution/index.ts` through stable FFI. Verify with full parity tests, successful `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release`, full git diff evidence, and tracked line-count delta showing Rust increase and TypeScript decrease (`git ls-files | xargs wc -l` baseline vs after). Rollback by feature-flagged runtime selector restoring previous execution lane and preserving replay compatibility receipts. Completion is blocked until explicit human approval after proof bundle review. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_conf001_execution_cutover.ts`, `client/runtime/config/rust50_conf001_execution_cutover_policy.json`, `client/memory/tools/tests/rust50_conf001_execution_cutover.test.js`, `state/ops/rust50_conf001/latest.json`. | V6-RUST50-002, V6-RUST50-007 |

Objective: normalize the sprint-mode directive into canonical backlog requirements that enforce one-shot batch execution, strict order, and proof-first auditing for visible public Rust language-bar movement.

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |

| Permanent scaffolding: enforce session preamble `ENFORCER RULES ACTIVE — READ codex_enforcer.md FIRST.` when needed | new sprint conformance lane (`V6-RUST50-CONF-002`) | done | Add sprint preflight contract that blocks lane start without enforcer acknowledgment receipt and preserves immutable enforcement note in proof bundle. |
| Use batch/sprint execution (single long backlog payload, minimal micro-prompting, end-of-sprint audit) | `V6-RUST50-CONF-002` | done | Define deterministic sprint run contract: execute tasks in sequence, defer status completion until full proof bundle, and capture audit summary at sprint end. |
| Execute Sprint 1 in strict sequential order with no skips and no done-status before full completion + visible public language-bar change | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Create one canonical Sprint 1 lane with ordered subcontracts and explicit `no_partial_done` gating. |
| Task 1: `core/layer2/execution/` full Rust core + FFI `run_workflow(yaml: &str) -> ExecutionReceipt` + thin TS wrapper + parity/chaos | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Require crate-first execution runtime ownership and TS wrapper-only compatibility bridge under `client/runtime/systems/execution/index.ts`. |
| Task 2: `core/layer0/pinnacle/` full Rust CRDT core + `merge_delta`/`get_sovereignty_index` + thin TS wrapper + sync parity | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Require Rust-first CRDT merge/index logic and parity receipts for multi-device sync semantics. |
| Task 3: `core/layer0/vault/` expanded to 100% Rust with existing `vault_policy.blob`, `seal/rotate/audit` FFI, wrapper, fail-closed tests | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Require full Rust vault/security core ownership with policy-blob integration and sovereignty fail-closed verification. |
| Task 4: `core/layer0/red_legion/` full Rust chaos engine + formal hooks + observability blob integration + wrapper + resilience tests | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Require Rust-first chaos runtime and proof of resilience/sovereignty behavior parity. |
| Task 5: integrate blob system in all 4 crates with real logic blob + `load_embedded_xxx()` + manifest update | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Require per-crate embedded logic blob contract using existing fold/unfold + signed manifest conventions. |
| Task 6: thin TS wrappers for all new crates with backward compatibility | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Require non-breaking system path compatibility (`client/runtime/systems/*/index.ts`) and wrapper-only core policy. |
| Task 7: full system parity suite + WASM build per crate + phone simulation battery test `<5% daily` | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Require parity/test/battery receipts as hard gate for proof acceptance. |
| Task 8: update root `Cargo.toml` workspace for all new crates | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Require workspace membership and build-surface consistency across all migrated crates. |
| Completion proof: full `crates/` + `client/runtime/systems/` diff, successful WASM builds for every crate, current public Rust percentage visible increase, fixed commit message | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Require deterministic proof bundle and commit evidence; block completion without visible Rust uplift and exact commit-message contract. |
| Governance: do not mark backlog done until explicit human approval after audit | `V6-RUST50-CONF-003` | done | Enforce approval gate for `in_progress -> done` transition using signed audit receipt. |

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## External Requirements Intake (Mega Sprint 1: Public 50 Percent Visible Rust, 2026-03-03)

Objective: normalize the mega-sprint directive into canonical backlog requirements that force repository-visible Rust ownership across major modules and block completion until public language-bar evidence reaches `>=50%` Rust.

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Enforcer preamble required at sprint start (`ENFORCER RULES ACTIVE — READ codex_enforcer.md FIRST.`) | sprint conformance lane (`V6-RUST50-CONF-004`) | blocked | Require signed preflight acknowledgment receipt before sprint execution can transition from `queued` to `in_progress`. |
| Execute strict sequential order for all tasks, no skip, no done before public bar >=50% Rust | `V6-RUST50-CONF-004` | blocked | Add deterministic sequence contract with `no_skip` + `no_premature_done` enforcement and hard fail on ordering violations. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-RUST50-CONF-004 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | blocked | Mega Sprint 1 Public 50 Percent Rust Migration (8 Crates + Blobized Core Paths) | Public repository language composition remains overwhelmingly TypeScript, and prior internal/weighted migration signals do not guarantee externally visible Rust ownership at the required threshold. | Execute all eleven mega-sprint steps in strict sequence: migrate `execution`, `pinnacle`, `vault`, `red_legion`, `observability`, `graph`, `swarm`, and `mobile` to crate-first Rust ownership; integrate blob fold/unfold + manifest in all eight crates; maintain thin backward-compatible TS wrappers; update root Cargo workspace; and complete full parity + WASM + battery proof suite. Completion requires deterministic artifact bundle: git diff summary for `crates/` and `client/runtime/systems/`, successful `wasm32-unknown-unknown --release` builds for each migrated crate, and public GitHub language-bar evidence showing Rust `>=50%` with TypeScript decrease. Completion is hard-blocked pending explicit human approval after audit. Commit contract: `feat(rust-migration): Mega Sprint 1 - visible 50% Rust + 8 core crates + blob integration`. BLOCKED evidence: `state/ops/rust50_mega_sprint1_batch/latest.json` (`rust_share_pct_after=6.253`, `rust_share_min_pct=50`, violation `rust_share_min_ok`). | V6-RUST50-CONF-002, V6-RUST50-CONF-003, V6-RUST50-007, V6-RUST50-003, V6-RUST50-004, V6-RUST50-005, V5-RUST-HYB-009 |

## Fortune-100 Software Readiness Path (Queued, 2026-03-03)

Target: move software readiness from current baseline (`sophistication ~64/100`, `appearance ~83/100`) to `>=90/100` across both dimensions using measurable, auditable execution.

### Net-New Canonical Queue

Execution order in this queue is dependency-valid and impact-ranked:
1) reliability/change/supply-chain foundations, 2) scale + tenant safety, 3) compliance and lifecycle governance, 4) launch polish, 5) executive scorecard.

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-F100-001 | hardening | V6 | done | Reliability Certification Program (SLO/Error-Budget Gate) | Reliability evidence is distributed across lanes and not promoted as one enforceable release contract. | Added Rust-authoritative lane `protheus-ops f100-reliability-certification run|status` (`core/layer0/ops/src/f100_reliability_certification.rs`) with tiered SLO catalog in `client/runtime/config/f100_reliability_certification_policy.json`; release gate blocks when error-budget burn exceeds policy and monthly scorecard receipts emit `uptime`, `receipt_latency_p95_ms`, `receipt_latency_p99_ms`, `incident_rate`, and `change_fail_rate` plus drill/rollback evidence inventories. |  |
| V6-F100-002 | governance | V6 | done | SDLC Change-Control Plane (RFC/ADR/Risk Class) | High-risk changes still rely on mixed conventions rather than one deterministic approval and traceability flow. | Added Rust lane `protheus-ops sdlc-change-control run|status` (`core/layer0/ops/src/sdlc_change_control.rs`) with policy `client/runtime/config/sdlc_change_control_policy.json`; merge gate workflow `.github/workflows/sdlc-change-control.yml` fail-closes PRs when risk class is understated or required RFC/ADR/approver/rollback evidence is missing. PR template now captures required fields (`.github/pull_request_template.md`) and receipts are persisted in `state/ops/sdlc_change_control/`. | V6-F100-001 |
| V6-F100-003 | hardening | V6 | done | Secure Supply-Chain Provenance v2 (SBOM + Signed Artifacts) | Dependency and artifact trust posture exists but is not yet one end-to-end provenance contract visible at release time. | Added Rust gate `protheus-ops supply-chain-provenance-v2 run|status` (`core/layer0/ops/src/supply_chain_provenance_v2.rs`) with policy `client/runtime/config/supply_chain_provenance_v2_policy.json`; release workflow now emits per-artifact SBOM/signature/vulnerability summary/provenance bundle and fail-closes before publish (`.github/workflows/release-security-artifacts.yml`) when signature/hash parity, vulnerability SLA, or rollback-to-last-known-good contract is violated. | V6-F100-002 |
| V6-F100-005 | scale-readiness | V6 | done | 1M-User Performance Certification Harness | Scale-readiness lanes exist, but no single certification harness proves sustained 1M-user target behavior with promotion gates. | Implemented in `core/layer0/ops/src/f100_readiness_program.rs` lane `V6-F100-005` with profile budgets (`client/runtime/config/one_million_performance_profile.json`) and strict receipts under `state/ops/f100_readiness_program/`. | V6-F100-001, V6-F100-003 |
| V6-F100-006 | hardening | V6 | done | Multi-Tenant Isolation + Data Governance Contract | Tenant-boundary and data-governance assurances are not yet unified into one fail-closed certification path. | Implemented in lane `V6-F100-006` with isolation contract (`client/runtime/config/multi_tenant_isolation_contract.json`) + adversarial evidence (`state/security/multi_tenant_isolation_adversarial/latest.json`) and strict fail-closed receipts. | V6-F100-003, V6-F100-005 |
| V6-F100-004 | governance | V6 | done | Compliance Evidence Automation (SOC2/ISO Audit Bundle) | Compliance controls are present in parts but audit evidence packaging is not yet deterministic and repeatable for external review. | Implemented in lane `V6-F100-004` with control map (`client/runtime/config/compliance_control_map.json`) and deterministic bundle output (`state/ops/compliance_evidence_bundle/latest.json`) including freshness enforcement. | V6-F100-002, V6-F100-003 |
| V6-F100-007 | hardening | V6 | done | API/CLI Contract Lifecycle (Versioning + Deprecation) | Public/internal interfaces evolve quickly without a single contract lifecycle policy that guarantees backward compatibility windows. | Implemented in lane `V6-F100-007` with semver/deprecation/changelog contract checks against `client/runtime/config/api_cli_contract_registry.json` and strict receipts. | V6-F100-002 |
| V6-F100-008 | governance | V6 | done | On-Call + Incident Command Maturity Pack | Incident handling exists but lacks one codified IC model with measurable response-performance gates. | Implemented in lane `V6-F100-008` with on-call policy (`client/runtime/config/oncall_incident_policy.json`), runbooks, game-day evidence checks, and MTTA/MTTR gate receipts. | V6-F100-001, V6-F100-002 |
| V6-F100-009 | launch-polish | V6 | done | Golden-Path Onboarding + Developer Portal | Onboarding quality is improving but still spread across files, reducing first-week productivity and enterprise onboarding confidence. | Implemented in lane `V6-F100-009` via role onboarding docs + bootstrap script `client/cli/bin/protheus_onboarding_bootstrap.sh` and success telemetry gates (`state/ops/onboarding_portal/success_metrics.json`). | V6-F100-001 |
| V6-F100-010 | launch-polish | V6 | done | Enterprise Architecture Narrative + Evidence Pack | Architecture credibility is high internally but lacks one tightly curated external-facing evidence-backed packet for enterprise evaluators. | Implemented in lane `V6-F100-010` with enterprise architecture evidence pack `docs/client/ops/ENTERPRISE_ARCHITECTURE_EVIDENCE_PACK.md` and required artifact token checks. | V6-F100-009 |
| V6-F100-011 | launch-polish | V6 | done | Operator Surface Consistency Program (UX/Tone/State Model) | UX and operator surfaces are strong but not yet governed by one consistent state/tone/interaction contract across all commands/panels. | Implemented in lane `V6-F100-011` with surface policy (`client/runtime/config/operator_surface_consistency_policy.json`) + snapshot contract (`docs/client/ops/operator_surface_consistency_snapshot.json`) checks. | V6-F100-009, V6-F100-010 |
| V6-F100-012 | governance | V6 | done | Executive Engineering Readiness Scorecard (Path to 90) | Progress toward Fortune-100 software readiness is currently qualitative and not tracked against one explicit score model. | Implemented in lane `V6-F100-012` with weighted scorecard receipts under `state/ops/executive_readiness_scorecard/`, two-cycle sustain gating, and strict promotion block on unsustained scores. | V6-F100-001, V6-F100-002, V6-F100-004, V6-F100-005, V6-F100-009, V6-F100-010, V6-F100-011 |

## Persona Seamless Workflow Integration Pack (2026-03-03)

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-PERS-SEAM-001 | extension | V6 | done | Workflow Trigger Commands + Persona Dashboard | Persona consults required manual command memory, which reduced day-to-day adoption and made state visibility fragmented. | Added `protheus lens trigger <pre-sprint|drift-alert|weekly-checkin>` and `protheus lens dashboard [--window=<n>]` in `client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts` with trigger telemetry (`metric=trigger_activation`) and markdown dashboard summaries tied to `personas/organization/triggers.md`. Verified by `node client/memory/tools/tests/personas_lens_cli.test.js` (trigger + dashboard assertions). Rollback by removing trigger/dashboard branches and trigger telemetry rows. | V3-RACE-197, V4-OPS-014 |
| V6-PERS-SEAM-002 | hardening | V6 | done | Pre-Commit Persona Safety Lens Gate | Commits could bypass fast local risk screening for security/sovereignty regressions before CI, increasing review load and rollback churn. | Added deterministic pre-commit gate `client/runtime/systems/personas/pre_commit_lens_gate.ts` (wrapper: `.js`) and wired `.githooks/pre-commit` to run it after staged format/lint. Gate writes receipts under `state/personas/pre_commit_lens_gate/` and fail-closes on high-risk staged diff patterns unless explicitly overridden. Verified by `node client/memory/tools/tests/pre_commit_lens_gate.test.js`. Rollback by removing hook invocation and script lane. | V3-FCH-002, V3-RACE-060 |
| V6-PERS-SEAM-003 | extension | V6 | done | Inversion Persona Feed Auto-Push (Policy-Gated) | Inversion loop insights were not automatically enriching persona feeds, leaving persona context stale between manual feed updates. | Added optional policy-gated feed push path in `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/inversion_controller.ts` (`persona_lens_gate.feed_push`) that writes hash-verified `## System Passed` entries to persona feeds and emits feed-push receipts. Default remains off in `client/runtime/config/inversion_policy.json` for behavior-preserving rollout. Verified by `node client/memory/tools/tests/inversion_persona_lens_gate.test.js` with temp persona feed root + receipts checks. Rollback by setting `persona_lens_gate.feed_push.enabled=false` (default) or removing feed-push hook path. | V4-SELF-001, V6-PERS-SEAM-001 |
| V6-PERS-SEAM-004 | launch-polish | V6 | done | Trigger Playbook + Operator Docs Wiring | Trigger workflows lacked a canonical documented source, reducing repeatability and operator onboarding clarity. | Added `personas/organization/triggers.md` and linked trigger/dashboard operations in `personas/README.md` plus CLI usage surfaces (`client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts`). Verified through `personas_lens_cli` command-path tests and help-surface coverage in existing CLI suite. Rollback by removing docs references and restoring previous CLI usage text. | V6-PERS-SEAM-001 |

## Persona Conflict-Arbitration Automation Pack (2026-03-03)

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-PERS-SEAM-005 | extension | V6 | done | Multi-Persona Lens Query + Deterministic Arbitration + Surprise/Recall Signal | Manual persona synthesis created projection bias and reduced scalability for conflict-based reasoning across personas. | Added native multi-persona CLI path `protheus lens <persona1> <persona2> [personaN...] "<query>" [--expected="..."]` in `client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts` with divergence-based disagreement detection (`>20%`), deterministic arbitration driven by `personas/organization/arbitration_rules.json`, client/memory/feed recall surfacing, and surprise scoring versus expected baseline. Updated operator help surfaces (`client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl.ts`, `personas/README.md`) and regression coverage in `client/memory/tools/tests/personas_lens_cli.test.js` (`multi persona`, conflict detection, arbitration winner, surprise section). Verify via `node client/memory/tools/tests/personas_lens_cli.test.js` plus command proof `node client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.js vikram rohan "Prioritize memory or security first?" --expected="Prioritize memory core determinism first."`; rollback by removing multi-persona branch and restoring single/all persona routing semantics. | V6-PERS-SEAM-001, V6-PERS-SEAM-004 |

## Protheus Conduit Program (Requirements Intake 2026-03-05)

Objective: establish a narrow Rust-first conduit between the TCB and TypeScript surfaces so TS remains optional and removable without core behavior drift.

| ID | Class | Wave | Status | Title | Problem | Acceptance | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-CONDUIT-001 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | done | Conduit Core Scaffold + Typed Message Schema | Rust/TS boundary lacks a single typed transport contract, increasing wiring drift risk and reducing substrate-swap confidence. | `core/layer2/conduit` owns typed schema for 7 TS commands and Rust event responses, deterministic crossing receipts, fail-closed Rust validation, and Unix socket + stdio handlers; `client/runtime/systems/conduit/conduit-client.ts` provides typed client transport scaffold; `cargo test -p conduit` passes. | V6-RUST50-007 |
| V6-CONDUIT-002 | hardening | V6 | done | Constitution and Policy Gate Binding for Conduit | Conduit validation currently covers structural + policy-shape checks but is not yet bound to full AGENT-CONSTITUTION and runtime policy registries. | `core/layer2/conduit` now includes `RegistryPolicyGate` bound to `AGENT-CONSTITUTION.md` and `client/runtime/config/guard_check_registry.json`, deny-by-default command capability mappings, and deterministic per-request `policy_receipt_hash` output on validation. | V6-CONDUIT-001 |
| V6-CONDUIT-003 | extension | V6 | done | Conduit Feature Migration: Agent Lifecycle Path | At least one real feature path must run through conduit to prove TS/Rust bridge viability beyond scaffolding. | `client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusd.ts` now supports conduit-first lifecycle routing (`start`, `stop`, `status`) via typed conduit client with graceful fallback to legacy control-plane execution when conduit transport is unavailable. | V6-CONDUIT-001, V6-CONDUIT-002 |
| V6-CONDUIT-004 | scale-readiness | V6 | done | Substrate-Swap and Performance Certification Gate | Conduit requires objective proof that core behavior is identical with and without TS and that overhead stays within the stated latency budget. | Added certification suite in `core/layer2/conduit/tests/certification.rs` proving direct-core vs stdio parity and asserting round-trip latency budgets (`<5ms hosted`, `<20ms embedded`) with deterministic receipt checks. | V6-CONDUIT-003 |
| V6-CONDUIT-005 | hardening | V6 | done | Message Signing for Every Crossing | Unsigned conduit requests allow tampering risk at the Rust/TS boundary. | Added `core/layer2/conduit-security` `MessageSigner` and enforced signature verification on every command in `core/layer2/conduit`; invalid signatures fail closed with policy-violation receipts and regression tests. | V6-CONDUIT-002 |
| V6-CONDUIT-006 | hardening | V6 | done | Capability Token Enforcement | TS callers need explicit bounded authority so command scope cannot exceed granted capabilities. | Added signed capability tokens (`CapabilityTokenAuthority`) and command-scope validation in conduit runtime; missing/expired/invalid scopes are denied fail-closed with deterministic receipts and tests. | V6-CONDUIT-002, V6-CONDUIT-005 |
| V6-CONDUIT-007 | hardening | V6 | done | Conduit Rate Limiting + Backpressure | Unbounded conduit traffic can create resource contention and denial-of-service risk. | Added per-client and per-client-command rate-limiter enforcement in conduit runtime with deterministic denial reasons, plus test coverage for throttling windows and recovery after reset. | V6-CONDUIT-002 |
| V6-CONDUIT-008 | governance | V6 | done | Conduit Regression Rebuild Ledger | Conduit hardening requirements need explicit backlog traceability so full behavior can be reconstructed after regressions. | `UPGRADE_BACKLOG.md` and `docs/client/requirements/REQ-05-protheus-conduit-bridge.md` now include complete phase + hardening requirement mapping with concrete code/test evidence for rebuild. | V6-CONDUIT-001, V6-CONDUIT-007 |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-PRIM-001 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | in_progress | Task Primitive Rust Completion | Task scheduling authority must be Rust-native to keep kernel portability and deterministic gate enforcement. | `core/layer1/task` exposes deterministic task evaluation and claim-evidence gate behavior with Rust tests and no TS-owned task primitive logic in kernel paths. | REQ-08-001 |
| V6-PRIM-002 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | in_progress | Resource Primitive Rust Completion | Resource quota/budget authority in TS introduces drift risk for kernel builds and policy auditability. | `core/layer1/resource` owns quota and allocation checks with deterministic tests; TS budget/task surfaces are wrapper-only. | REQ-08-001 |
| V6-PRIM-003 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | in_progress | Isolation Primitive Rust Completion | Capability isolation must remain fail-closed in Rust for secure substrate swaps. | `core/layer1/isolation` owns capability/sandbox gate logic and test coverage for denied/default paths. | REQ-08-001 |
| V6-PRIM-004 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | done | IPC Primitive Rust Completion | IPC substrate remained scaffold-level and lacked deterministic Rust message policy checks. | `core/layer1/ipc` owns channel/message envelope validation with deterministic receipt hash behavior and tests. | REQ-08-001 |
| V6-PRIM-005 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | done | Storage Primitive Rust Completion | Storage primitive remained scaffold-level and lacked Rust-native optimistic/versioned state contracts. | `core/layer1/storage` owns key/value/version mutation logic with fail-closed key validation and tests. | REQ-08-001 |
| V6-PRIM-006 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | in_progress | Observability Primitive Rust Completion | Observability primitive must remain Rust-authoritative for deterministic evidence and resilience scoring. | `core/layer1/observability` remains Rust source-of-truth with stable tests, blob loading, and deterministic scoring receipts. | REQ-08-001 |
| V6-PRIM-007 | primitive-upgrade | V6 | done | Update Primitive Rust Completion | Update/install policy at scaffold level blocks auditable kernel-side update safety enforcement. | `core/layer1/update` owns update package validation and fail-closed policy checks with tests. | REQ-08-001 |
| V6-PRIM-008 | hardening | V6 | in_progress | Primitive TS Wrapper Contract Enforcement | Primitive-adjacent TS lanes can regress into logic ownership unless contract-enforced. | `contract_check` validates `primitive_ts_wrapper_contract` in `client/runtime/config/rust_source_of_truth_policy.json`; wrappers require conduit bridge tokens and forbid legacy bridge imports. | REQ-08-002, REQ-08-004 |
| V6-PRIM-009 | governance | V6 | in_progress | Primitive Migration Residual Audit | Remaining primitive-like TS files need explicit residual inventory and burn-down tracking. | Publish/update primitive residual report with each migration wave, listing non-wrapper TS primitive candidates and target crate mapping. | REQ-08-005 |
