| V6-GAP-006 | blocked | Public Release + Announcement Publication Authority | Final publication (GitHub release, npm publish, public posting) requires maintainer account authority and client/legal/commercial approval outside autonomous execution. | Human-owned publication evidence is attached via authority artifacts (`HMAN-028`, `HMAN-029`) and corresponding release/post links are recorded in state evidence. | V6-F100-034, V6-SEC-001, V6-GAP-005 |

## Technical Excellence Roadmap Intake (Google Doc `19DO7nvxizNJmLuoRUFrYYTNOmMnHJCGKI44AlGHbcSw`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: convert roadmap claims into executable contracts while separating automatable engineering lanes from human-authority publication/legal lanes.

| ID | status | title | problem | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-TECH-301 | done | Formal three-plane spec surface + guard | Three-plane formal claims were not backed by an executable repo contract. | `planes/spec/tla/three_plane_boundary.tla` + cfg + `client/runtime/systems/ops/formal_spec_guard.ts` + CI workflow `.github/workflows/formal-spec-guard.yml` exist and `npm run -s ops:formal-spec:check` passes. |
| V6-TECH-302 | done | Inter-plane contract source-of-truth scaffold | Inter-plane envelope requirements lacked a canonical contracts directory. | `planes/contracts/conduit_envelope.schema.json` exists with required keys (`domain`, `command`, `payload`) and is validated by formal spec guard. |
| V6-TECH-303 | done | Architecture + verify integration for formal surfaces | Formal surfaces were not integrated into architecture docs or the default verify path. | `ARCHITECTURE.md` references `planes/spec` + `planes/contracts`; `verify.sh` runs dependency-boundary and formal-spec checks before origin-integrity checks. |
| V6-TECH-304 | done | Full formal proof runtime lane in CI | Spec scaffolding exists but heavy proof lanes (`Kani`/`Prusti`/`Lean`) were not integrated end-to-end. | Added executable runtime gate `client/runtime/systems/ops/formal_proof_runtime_gate.{ts,js}` with policy `client/runtime/config/formal_proof_runtime_gate_policy.json`, strict fail-closed required checks (`ops:formal-spec:check`, `critical_path_formal_verifier`, `formal:invariants:run`, `critical_protocol_formal_suite`) plus optional toolchain probes (`cargo kani`, `prusti-rustc`, `lean`) and reproducible state/proof-pack artifacts linked to commit hash. CI job `.github/workflows/formal-proof-runtime.yml` now runs the lane and uploads receipts/artifacts. |
| V6-TECH-305 | done | Reproducible benchmark/proof-pack threshold enforcement | Roadmap-level benchmark thresholds were not fully CI-enforced with reproducible artifact proofs. | Added threshold gate `client/runtime/systems/ops/proof_pack_threshold_gate.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/proof_pack_threshold_gate_policy.json` to evaluate mech benchmark, 6-month harness, parity, and invariants against explicit thresholds; receipts now include commit hash + deterministic metric/check contract and publish `threshold_gate_latest.json` proof artifacts. CI workflow `.github/workflows/proof-pack-threshold-gate.yml` enforces dockerized reproducible execution with uploaded artifacts. |

Human-owned carryovers from this intake are tracked in `docs/client/HUMAN_ONLY_ACTIONS.md` (`HMAN-041..HMAN-044`).

## Contributor Signal Program Intake (Google Doc `19IXz6Rjbn9ltXJV-Me2P9oRZ3KLs3KV90VG3k_PdC7E`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: convert launch-day contributor-signal goals into executable, integrity-safe contracts (consent-bound attribution, verifiable counts, and no fabricated contribution claims).

| ID | status | title | problem | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-FORT-219-001 | done | Consent-bound all-contributors ingestion lane | Contributor-wall goals are currently ad hoc and can drift from verified consent/source records. | Added `scripts/add_contributors_from_csv.js` with schema-validated CSV intake (`github_username`, `role`, `consent_token`) and deterministic output to `.all-contributorsrc` plus `docs/client/community/contributors_manifest.json`; lane fail-closes on duplicates, malformed usernames, or missing consent tokens. Regression coverage: `client/memory/tools/tests/add_contributors_from_csv.test.js`. |
| V6-FORT-219-002 | done | Co-author trailer automation with compliance guard | Manual co-author trailer assembly is error-prone and can create invalid attribution or fabricated-credit risk. | Added `scripts/empty_fort_coauthor_inject.sh` with `--dry-run` default plus guarded `--apply` mode, strict consent/email validation (`@users.noreply.github.com`), evidence artifact emission under `state/ops/evidence/`, and fail-closed behavior on invalid rows. Regression coverage: `client/memory/tools/tests/empty_fort_coauthor_inject.test.js`. |
| V6-FORT-219-003 | done | README + CONTRIBUTORS contract generator | Launch messaging and contributor metadata are manually maintained and can become inconsistent across surfaces. | Added deterministic generator `scripts/generate_contributor_surfaces.js` that updates README contributor block (marker-scoped) and writes `CONTRIBUTORS.md` from one manifest source (`docs/client/community/contributors_manifest.json`). Regression coverage: `client/memory/tools/tests/generate_contributor_surfaces.test.js`. |
| V6-FORT-219-004 | done | Launch visual telemetry workflow | Contributor/commit signal badges are not currently produced as a release artifact bundle. | Added `.github/workflows/empty-fort-visuals.yml` that runs on release publish/manual dispatch and generates contributor/commit telemetry artifacts via `scripts/empty_fort_visuals.js`; artifacts are uploaded with immutable run IDs. Regression coverage: `client/memory/tools/tests/empty_fort_visuals.test.js`. |
| V6-FORT-219-005 | done | Empty-fort integrity verification gate | Contributor-signal execution lacks one strict gate proving legality/integrity before launch publication. | Added `scripts/verify-empty-fort.sh` and CI workflow `.github/workflows/empty-fort-verify.yml` enforcing consent manifest presence, placeholder/malformed identity rejection, noreply email validation, contributor threshold checks, and README claim-evidence marker consistency (`V4-FORT-006`). Regression coverage: `client/memory/tools/tests/verify_empty_fort.test.js`. |
| V6-FORT-219-006 | done | Transparent post-launch pulse automation | Post-launch activity shaping is not standardized and risks crossing integrity boundaries if not transparently constrained. | Added `scripts/empty_fort_pulse_scheduler.js` and policy `client/runtime/config/empty_fort_pulse_policy.json` enforcing service-account-only apply mode, daily PR caps, and immutable audit trail (`artifacts/empty-fort/pulse_audit.json`). Added workflow `.github/workflows/empty-fort-pulse.yml` for scheduled/manual execution and public disclosure in README governance section. Regression coverage: `client/memory/tools/tests/empty_fort_pulse_scheduler.test.js`. |

## Dominance Roadmap Intake (Google Doc `1u_KCXofHnG2OGFsLp3J0r-Yk-YyN0Ft0dzeEeOFd4kk`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: translate competitive-roadmap claims into executable, integrity-safe requirements focused on measurable adoption/performance/interoperability gains without fabricated or unverified public claims.

| ID | status | title | problem | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-DOM-401 | queued | Polyglot GA release contract (`v0.1.0`) | Multi-channel release packaging is fragmented, limiting adoption and credibility across Rust/TS/Python ecosystems. | Ship versioned release contract for GitHub Releases + crates.io + npm + PyPI with signed multi-arch artifacts and SBOM bundle; release gate fails if any channel or signature artifact is missing. |
| V6-DOM-402 | queued | Native Python SDK parity lane (Rust-backed) | Existing Python wrapper surface is too thin to compete with first-class Python agent frameworks. | Add Rust-backed Python SDK under `sdk/python` with async client parity for core APIs (`lens`, `orchestrate`, `shadow`, `research`), receipt propagation, and parity regression tests against TS client behavior. |
| V6-DOM-403 | queued | Public benchmark crown harness + reproducibility gate | Performance claims are not yet continuously published through one reproducible benchmark lane. | Add benchmark workflow + artifact pack that runs concurrency/cold-start/throughput/token-efficiency suites in reproducible containers, publishes machine-readable reports, and fails on regressions against policy thresholds. |
| V6-DOM-404 | queued | Zero-to-production developer bootstrap UX lane | New-user onboarding requires too many manual steps across languages and safety primitives. | Add `protheus init --lang <python|typescript>` scaffolder + `protheus demo --python` path generating runnable examples with Safety Plane + receipts prewired; success measured by scripted bootstrap check completing in under 15 minutes on clean host profiles. |
| V6-DOM-405 | queued | Tool registry + policy-gated marketplace ingestion lane | Ecosystem growth is constrained without one governed package/tool ingestion path. | Add registry + install contract (`protheus hub install`) with constitution/policy checks, signed package metadata, tool manifest schema validation, and deterministic install receipts. |
| V6-DOM-406 | queued | Crew/debate/shadow orchestration contract uplift | Competitors win on simple orchestration semantics while Protheus orchestration surfaces remain fragmented. | Add declarative orchestration entrypoints (`crew`, `debate`, persistent shadow scheduling) that compile through Safety Plane graphs with typed receipts and conflict-resolution tests. |
| V6-DOM-407 | queued | MCP + A2A interoperability runtime lane | Protocol-war interoperability is incomplete without first-class MCP/A2A support in the core runtime boundary. | Add native MCP server surface and A2A session bridge through conduit with signed identity/session receipts, conformance tests, and no-bypass three-plane boundary enforcement. |
| V6-DOM-408 | queued | WebMCP/browser substrate adapter | Browser-native agent execution path is missing, limiting zero-install reach and standards alignment. | Add substrate adapter under `planes/substrate/webmcp/` with policy-gated browser session contracts, receipt chaining, and fallback-safe capability restrictions. |
| V6-DOM-409 | queued | Agent identity and trust control plane | Cross-agent authentication/authorization remains under-specified for standards-aligned deployments. | Add identity plane contracts for agent IDs, signed session assertions, and trust-chain verification tied to Merkle receipts and governance policy; include replay/revocation tests. |
| V6-DOM-410 | queued | Kubernetes operator + multi-cloud runtime packaging | Helm/Terraform exist, but there is no first-class operator for autonomous lifecycle management at enterprise scale. | Add `protheus-operator` packaging and CRDs with lifecycle reconciliation, autoscaling, and receipt export hooks; conformance suite validates install/upgrade/rollback behavior across Kubernetes profiles. |
| V6-DOM-411 | queued | Open Agent Contracts specification pack | Three-plane/conduit interfaces are documented but not published as one machine-readable external compatibility contract. | Publish versioned Open Agent Contracts spec (schemas + examples + compatibility tests) covering three planes, conduit envelopes, identity/session primitives, and backward-compatibility policy checks. |
| V6-DOM-412 | queued | Vertical compliance template compiler + TRiSM report lane | Regulated-market adoption is slowed by lack of precompiled policy templates and auditable compliance report generation. | Add template compiler for regulated profiles (finance/healthcare/defense) that emits policy bundles + deterministic TRiSM-style evidence reports from receipts with validation and replay tests. |

## Conduit Schema Hardening Intake (Google Doc `17E9b15Lw5AkyvuqeVW8QwNd3Q2vm1hrui-aWPC7ub7M`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: harden schema/endpoint exposure at the conduit boundary through runtime-only schema materialization, session-scoped cryptographic scrambling, and strict governance-safe debugging controls.

| ID | status | title | problem | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-SCHEMA-501 | queued | Runtime-only schema materialization lane | Static artifacts can leak high-fidelity protocol semantics when schema contracts are embedded in files/binaries. | Real internal schema contracts are materialized only in process memory after key-gated bootstrap; no plaintext runtime schema file is shipped in release artifacts; release scan gate asserts absence of plaintext signatures. |
| V6-SCHEMA-502 | queued | Conduit scrambler boundary primitive | TS/Core traffic currently relies on typed envelopes but lacks mandatory session-level payload scrambling at the boundary. | Add Rust scrambler module in conduit path with authenticated encryption and deterministic session bootstrap receipts; all client/core messages pass through scramble/unscramble path with parity tests proving no behavior drift. |
| V6-SCHEMA-503 | queued | Per-build protocol-shape randomization contract | Repeated static envelope identifiers increase pattern-learning surface for reverse engineering. | Build pipeline generates per-release randomized public protocol aliases mapped to stable internal commands via signed mapping artifacts; compatibility tests verify command integrity and backward-safe migration path. |
| V6-SCHEMA-504 | queued | Debug deactivation key + fail-closed key policy | Security hardening without operator override blocks development, while weak override controls create bypass risk. | Introduce explicit debug-key bypass mode with strict env-gated activation, signed audit receipts, and fail-closed default when key state is invalid or missing in hardened builds. |
| V6-SCHEMA-505 | queued | Decoy/honeypot schema telemetry lane | Recon attempts are currently under-observed at schema/protocol probing surfaces. | Add controlled decoy schema/honeypot responses for unauthorized probes with immutable telemetry receipts and alert thresholds; lane must not alter documented public contracts or violate claim-evidence policy (`V4-FORT-006`). |
| V6-SCHEMA-506 | queued | Scrambler performance + resilience verification gate | Boundary hardening can regress latency/reliability without explicit budgets and chaos checks. | Add benchmark + chaos gate for scrambler path enforcing overhead budget, replay resilience, and fallback integrity; CI fails when p95 latency or error-rate thresholds are exceeded. |

## Reverse-Engineering Resistance Intake (Google Doc `1B932-wmk94Hyo4GxKPENZZ7nv02-hLbmmZn3BNwlfXw`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: raise reverse-engineering cost through governance-safe misdirection, build polymorphism, and runtime compartmentalization while preserving deterministic operations and developer recoverability.

| ID | status | title | problem | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-OBF-601 | queued | Decoy architecture artifact generator | Public architecture surfaces remain too consistent, enabling efficient static pattern analysis. | Add build-lane generator for clearly labeled non-authoritative decoy architecture artifacts under `docs/architecture/decoy_variants/` with deterministic provenance metadata and strict guard that real contracts remain in canonical specs. |
| V6-OBF-602 | queued | Polymorphic build randomization hooks | Stable symbol/layout patterns across builds reduce attacker effort for binary diffing and model-assisted inference. | Add compile-time polymorphism hooks (symbol aliasing, string protection, layout variation toggles) with per-build seed receipts and deterministic debug override mode; release pipeline records build fingerprint manifests. |
| V6-OBF-603 | queued | Runtime schema shard loader and in-memory reassembly | Sensitive protocol semantics are still too recoverable from static artifacts without memory-only assembly controls. | Implement schema shard loading contract with encrypted fragments, runtime in-memory reassembly, zeroization on shutdown, and boot-time receipt proof that no plaintext shard files persist on disk. |
| V6-OBF-604 | queued | False-invariant noise lane with integrity boundaries | External analysis can overfit to deterministic invariants if all published traces are semantically direct. | Add optional noise-lane generator for non-authoritative invariants/trace artifacts that is explicitly tagged as synthetic and never contaminates canonical receipts, benchmarks, or compliance evidence (`V4-FORT-006` guard enforced). |
| V6-OBF-605 | queued | Anti-analysis telemetry and honeypot module guard | Reverse-engineering attempts lack dedicated signal capture and rate-aware response at module boundaries. | Add honeypot/anti-analysis module lane that emits bounded telemetry receipts on suspicious probe patterns with strict privacy policy, no undeclared outbound traffic, and configurable deny/degrade responses. |
| V6-OBF-606 | queued | Cryptographic watermark and provenance tracing lane | Attribution of leaked or tampered binaries is weak without embedded provenance markers. | Add cryptographic watermark embedding and verification tooling for release binaries and receipt bundles; verification must prove artifact lineage without exposing private keys or sensitive payload contents. |
| V6-OBF-607 | queued | Obfuscation safety and operability regression gate | Security hardening can unintentionally degrade operator workflows and incident response if not continuously checked. | Add CI gate covering obfuscated vs debug builds for parity, startup health, and incident-debug workflow checks; gate fails if operability SLOs regress beyond policy thresholds. |
| V6-OBF-608 | queued | Layered obfuscation policy contract | Ad hoc obfuscation controls drift without one canonical policy for allowed techniques and safety constraints. | Add `client/runtime/config/obfuscation_policy.json` + contract checker defining allowed modes, prohibited behaviors (fabricated claims, hidden network exfil, receipt contamination), key handling requirements, and required approvals for hardened release mode. |

## Extension & Application Platform Intake (Google Doc `1oXL2gBlIbWrl3sSs8qjWintSgssy-58XjoAJP3jPwEk`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: make governed tool/app development on top of InfRing fast and low-friction while preserving core sovereignty, conduit-only boundaries, and receipt-first enforcement.

| ID | status | title | problem | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-EXT-701 | queued | `protheus new-tool` scaffold lane | Extension developers currently lack one-command governed tool scaffolding, slowing ecosystem growth. | Add CLI scaffold command generating tool manifest, conduit bindings, policy stub, test harness, and receipt wiring; command passes smoke test from clean workspace. |
| V6-EXT-702 | queued | `protheus new-app` scaffold lane | Full application scaffolding requires manual wiring across UI/runtime/governance surfaces. | Add CLI app scaffold command producing baseline app skeleton (UI surface, extension manifest, policy file, marketplace metadata, CI checks) with deterministic template receipts. |
| V6-EXT-703 | queued | Extension SDK parity pack (TS/Python/Go/Rust) | Polyglot extension authoring is fragmented and inconsistent across languages. | Publish official conduit SDK surfaces for TS/Python/Go/Rust with shared schema contracts, version compatibility checks, and parity tests on core actions. |
| V6-EXT-704 | queued | Capability-manifest + least-privilege enforcement lane | Extensions can drift into broad permissions without strict declarative capability boundaries. | Add extension capability manifest schema and runtime enforcement gate that issues scoped tokens, denies undeclared access, and records deny/allow receipts for each privileged call. |
| V6-EXT-705 | queued | Extension sandbox runtime + isolation contract | Unisolated extensions increase blast radius and governance bypass risk. | Add isolated extension runtime profile (process/container sandbox), policy-controlled resource/network bounds, and fail-closed startup if isolation guarantees are not met. |
| V6-EXT-706 | queued | One-click publish lane with governance checks | Publishing extensions requires manual multi-step checks and inconsistent policy validation. | Add `protheus publish` flow that runs constitution/policy/receipt checks, schema compatibility validation, and trusted metadata generation before registry admission. |
| V6-EXT-707 | queued | Marketplace registry + dependency resolver lane | Extension discovery/composition lacks a canonical verified registry with dependency governance. | Add registry contracts for extension metadata, dependency graph resolution, signature verification, and deny-list handling with deterministic install receipts. |
| V6-EXT-708 | queued | Extension trust scoring and reputation receipts | Users lack objective trust signals tied to runtime governance behavior. | Add trust score lane combining policy violations, receipt integrity, and runtime health signals; expose deterministic score artifacts and regression tests for score reproducibility. |
| V6-EXT-709 | queued | Developer hot-reload sandbox (`protheus dev`) | Local extension iteration is slow without integrated sandbox + live reload workflow. | Add `protheus dev` mode that runs extension in isolated sandbox with hot reload, policy simulation, and live receipt viewer; include guardrails preventing non-sandbox bypass. |
| V6-EXT-710 | queued | Extension overhead performance gate | Extension framework overhead can erode core runtime performance without explicit SLO enforcement. | Add benchmark gate proving extension call boundary overhead stays below configured threshold (default <3ms p95) and fails CI when regressions exceed policy budget. |

## Universal Seed OS Intake (Google Doc `1OwCGjqHtWlpY-p2mH7-cZ4TjhQsEdtJir84nGaoAAVs`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: stage the V4 universal-seed vision into executable backlog lanes for host adaptation sockets, Rust-kernel authority, sovereign lane evolution, and release-grade verification.

| ID | status | title | problem | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-SEED-801 | queued | Platform Oracle + signed HostProfile lane | Host adaptation is fragmented without one authoritative probe/identity contract at boot and lane-promotion time. | Implement Platform Oracle service that emits signed HostProfile artifacts (OS/distro/kernel/capabilities/cloud/immutability fields) with deterministic receipts and promotion-hook integration. |
| V6-SEED-802 | queued | PlatformSocket trait + signed socket discovery/runtime | Platform-specific adaptation lacks a typed contract and safe loading lifecycle across built-in and external sockets. | Define PlatformSocket trait + signed manifest schema, implement socket discovery/validation loader with fail-closed fallback to GenericSocket and hot-swap/version checks. |
| V6-SEED-803 | queued | Socket lifecycle CLI/API surface | Operators lack one consistent command surface for socket management and validation. | Add `protheusctl socket list|install|update|test|validate` command set with machine-readable status output and deterministic management receipts. |
| V6-SEED-804 | queued | GenericSocket baseline capability/security profile | No single baseline socket currently guarantees cross-platform security, support, and resource posture defaults. | Deliver GenericSocket baseline enforcing immutable/atomic update compatibility, LSM/capability policy hooks, air-gapped mode, power/thermal awareness, and minimal-footprint runtime policy checks. |
| V6-SEED-805 | queued | Rust microkernel authority completion (WASM lanes) | Mixed runtime ownership weakens determinism and portability for universal-seed execution. | Complete Rust-authoritative microkernel cutover for scheduler/IPC/sandbox/memory/crypto primitives with WASM lane execution (`wasmtime/WASI`) and parity receipts against existing behavior. |
| V6-SEED-806 | queued | Memory + security + systems acceleration pack | Core performance/security features are not yet consolidated into one verified universal runtime pack. | Deliver integrated pack for hierarchical memory substrate upgrades, adversarial-chaos expansion, kernel hook integrations (eBPF/io_uring/LSM), disk-encryption hooks, and power-aware scheduling with regression evidence. |
| V6-SEED-807 | queued | Multi-socket implementation wave (Ubuntu/FreeBSD/NixOS/RPi/Alpine) | Universal-host promise is unproven without concrete socket implementations for major target families. | Implement and validate first-party sockets for Ubuntu, FreeBSD, NixOS, Raspberry Pi OS, and Alpine with match-only activation and per-socket conformance suites. |
| V6-SEED-808 | queued | Sovereign lane superstructure (safe autogenesis + ToT/GoT/Debate/Weaver) | Advanced orchestration and self-evolution paths need one governance-safe pipeline instead of scattered feature lanes. | Implement safe autogenesis promotion pipeline (sandbox→unit/redteam/formal→human gate) plus native ToT/GoT/Debate/Weaver orchestration surfaces with signed promotion receipts. |
| V6-SEED-809 | queued | Platform-wide security/governance verification pack | Cross-platform expansion increases risk without consistent PQ crypto, compliance, and immutable audit controls. | Add post-quantum cryptography baseline, immutable receipt ledger, compliance tooling adapters, and mandatory redteam+chaos platform checks enforced in CI/release gates. |
| V6-SEED-810 | queued | Socket-aware observability/dashboard + CLI polish | Platform adaptation state is hard to reason about without unified telemetry and operator surface clarity. | Deliver socket-aware observability exports (OpenTelemetry + native adapters), upgraded `protheus-top` dashboard, and enhanced `protheusctl` host-adaptation status/controls with regression tests. |
| V6-SEED-811 | queued | Universal matrix validation + reproducible signed release lane | Universal-seed claims are not credible without hardware-matrix proof and reproducible signed release artifacts. | Add real-hardware test matrix (5 OS families × variants), before/after performance proof artifacts, reproducible signed release pipeline, and community socket registry prototype with integrity guard checks. |

## Infallible Origin Master Plan Intake (Google Doc `1QuwfA-EGZA4kCqfzrvFaeahszRskJLg7Moc92u4m2U0`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: formalize “infallible origin” execution weeds and seed-ignition sequencing into auditable backlog lanes, while mapping standards/launch objectives to existing governance constraints.

| ID | status | title | problem | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V6-MPLAN-901 | queued | Root invariant enforcer layer0-root consolidation lane | Origin hardening primitives exist but are distributed; there is no single authoritative “root-before-all” enforcement package. | Consolidate origin invariants into explicit pre-boot layer0-root gate with compile-time + runtime checks proving conduit-only boundary, constitution non-weakening controls, and resurrection-lane rollback prerequisites before runtime activation. |
| V6-MPLAN-902 | queued | Conduit-only type/runtime guard completion lane | Boundary guards exist but cross-language bindings are not yet fully uniform and compiler-level enforcement is incomplete. | Implement/complete conduit-only guard suite across Rust + TS/Python bindings with compile/runtime deny paths and parity tests proving non-conduit access fails closed. |
| V6-MPLAN-903 | queued | Receipt-to-origin cryptographic binding verifier lane | Receipt binding exists, but independent verifier ergonomics and origin-state proofs need one production-ready operator flow. | Extend `protheus-verify` standalone verification flow to validate receipts against exact Safety Plane Merkle/root bindings with deterministic CLI reports and CI regression coverage. |
| V6-MPLAN-904 | queued | `protheusd` self-audit daemon hardening lane | Startup self-audit exists but needs expanded trigger coverage and operator evidence UX for hot-reload/network-join events. | Ensure 30-second integrity checks run on start/hot-reload/network-join with fail-closed behavior, clear degraded-state signaling, and auditable receipts under unified self-audit state paths. |
| V6-MPLAN-905 | queued | Constitution interpreter formal diff/rollback lane | Constitution checks are present but formal diff-to-rollback pipeline is not yet one explicit lane with deterministic operator proofs. | Add constitution interpreter lane that computes formal diffs, enforces tiered approval gates, and triggers resurrection rollback automatically on unauthorized weakening attempts; include proof receipts and regression tests. |
| V6-MPLAN-906 | queued | Full formal-proof command surface (`cargo prove`) lane | Formal verification is partially integrated but lacks one canonical multi-tool command contract tied to release gating. | Add unified formal command lane (`cargo prove`/equivalent wrapper) orchestrating Lean/TLA+/Rust proof suites with deterministic artifact manifests and release-blocking policy integration. |
| V6-MPLAN-907 | queued | Public benchmark crown gate uplift (<80ms, <20MB, 5k tps targets) | Current benchmark contracts do not yet enforce the stricter “master plan” target envelope in one gate. | Extend benchmark threshold policy/gate to include master-plan target bands (cold-start, idle memory, throughput), reproducible container execution, and explicit pass/fail receipts per target. |
| V6-MPLAN-908 | queued | Native MCP+A2A seed-bootstrap integration lane | Interoperability and seed-bootstrap are tracked separately, reducing confidence in secure network-join sequencing. | Integrate MCP/A2A runtime lanes with seed-bootstrap join protocol such that new nodes must verify origin certificate + invariant status before admission; emit signed join receipts and denial traces. |
| V6-MPLAN-909 | queued | Vertical compliance compile-to-graph templates lane | Regulated deployment templates are not yet fully wired into compile-to-verified Safety Plane graph outputs. | Produce FedRAMP/HIPAA/IL5 template compiler outputs that generate verifiable Safety Plane graphs + compliance evidence bundles with replayable receipt chains. |
| V6-MPLAN-910 | queued | Self-hosted verification farm and annual proof-refresh lane | Long-term canyon maintenance lacks an explicit recurring verification farm contract tied to research/proof refresh cadence. | Add nightly self-hosted full-proof farm lane (Lean/Kani/TLA+/chaos/bench regressions), artifact retention policy, and annual substrate-proof refresh checklist with drift alerts. |
